Add `const CONTENT_ONLY_EXTENSIONS = new Set(['.md','.txt','.rst','.png','.jpg','.jpeg','.gif','.svg','.webp','.yaml','.yml','.json'])` and `export function classifySkill(files: string[]): SkillClassification` returning 'md-only' iff every file's lowercased extension is in the set, else 'code-containing'. A file with no '.' (no extension) is treated as not-content-only (code path). Import SkillClassification from @asr/core (added by asr-5k8).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/zip/classify.test.ts: classifySkill(['SKILL.md','img/logo.png','manifest.yaml']) === 'md-only'; classifySkill(['SKILL.md','hack.py']) === 'code-containing'; classifySkill(['SKILL.md','Makefile']) === 'code-containing' (extensionless); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Whitelist only — do NOT convert this to a blacklist of dangerous extensions (security regression per specs/security.md line 86). SVG <script> stripping and markdown XSS handling are separate downstream concerns. Keep the extension set byte-identical to specs/security.md lines 89-93.
close_reason: Implemented: added whitelist-based skill upload classifier with md-only/code-containing regression tests

title: Add yauzl-based safe zip extractor
description:
Parent story: asr-crh
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#zip-upload-hardening (#must-have-controls lines 13-26, #implementation-sketch lines 28-82)

Context:
Submissions are untrusted user archives; the extractor is the first line of defence against zip-slip, zip bombs, symlink and device-file attacks. specs/security.md mandates yauzl with explicit per-entry control and forbids unzipper. This is the MUST-HAVE control set from the threat model and drives Phase 1 auto-approve routing.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/zip/extract.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/zip/extract.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/package.json (add "yauzl":"^3.0.0" to deps; "@types/yauzl":"^2.10.0" and "yazl":"^2.5.1" to devDeps for test fixtures)

Change:
Implement `export async function extractSafe(zipPath: string, targetDir: string, limits = LIMITS): Promise<string[]>` per specs/security.md#implementation-sketch. LIMITS = { maxCompressedBytes 50*1024*1024, maxUncompressedBytes 200*1024*1024, maxFiles 500, maxDepth 5, maxPathLen 200 }. For each entry: NFC-normalize name; reject name.length > maxPathLen ('path too long'); reject /[\x00-\x1f‮‏]/ ('illegal chars'); reject path-traversal when resolve(targetDir,name) is not under resolve(targetDir)+sep ('path traversal'); reject relative-depth > maxDepth ('max depth'); reject symlinks via (externalFileAttributes >>> 16) & 0xa000 ('symlink rejected'); reject when ++fileCount > maxFiles ('max files'); accumulate uncompressedSize and reject > maxUncompressedBytes ('uncompressed size limit'). Accept the optional `limits` param so tests can trip caps cheaply. Return the list of extracted relative paths.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/zip/extract.test.ts: a zip built with yazl containing an entry named '../evil.txt' rejects with /path traversal/; a zip with 2 entries extracted with limits overriding maxFiles:1 rejects with /max files/; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
NEVER import unzipper (specs/security.md line 26 forbids it). Polyglot magic-byte sniffing is a follow-up refinement — this task delivers path/size/symlink/count controls only. Extract to a caller-provided targetDir (use os.tmpdir in tests) and clean it up in afterEach.
close_reason: Implemented: added yauzl-based safe zip extractor with traversal, count, path, symlink/type, and size controls plus regression tests

title: Add optimistic-lock guarded submission update helper
description:
Parent story: asr-7jd
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#concurrency-model (per-skill optimistic locking lines 144-148); specs/security.md#race-condition-prevention (lines 197-199)

Context:
Concurrent workflow steps / replicas must not clobber each other's submission writes. The lock_version column added in 0001 enables compare-and-swap updates so a stale writer is rejected rather than overwriting newer state — the primitive behind the per-skill mutex and 409 version_in_progress.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function insertSubmission(db, row)` (minimal seed helper inserting a row at lock_version 0) and `export function updateSubmissionStatus(db, id: string, expectedLockVersion: number, next: { statusPhase: string; statusJson: string }): boolean`. updateSubmissionStatus runs `UPDATE submissions SET status_phase=?, status_json=?, lock_version = lock_version + 1 WHERE id=? AND lock_version=?` and returns `info.changes === 1`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.test.ts: after runMigrations + insertSubmission at lock_version 0, updateSubmissionStatus(db,id,0,next) returns true and a second updateSubmissionStatus(db,id,0,next2) returns false (stale write rejected, no rows changed); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
This is the optimistic-lock primitive only — full submission CRUD and the SubmissionStatus serialisation strategy come with the API story (asr-igs). Do not throw on stale write here; return false so callers can map it to 409 version_in_progress.
close_reason: Implemented: added optimistic-lock guarded submission status update helper

title: Add 0001 submissions-table migration
description:
Parent story: asr-7jd
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#submission (lines 50-81); specs/audit.md#schema (FK target submissions(id) line 27); specs/submission-package.md#concurrency-model (lines 144-148)

Context:
submissions is the core write-side table the workflow drives and that audit_events FKs to. It must persist the Submission aggregate (specs/types.md) plus an optimistic-lock column for the per-skill mutex (concurrency model). audit_events itself is out of scope — owned by the Audit epic asr-62i.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0001_submissions.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (import and push the 0001 entry into `migrations`)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0001_submissions.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export a Migration `{ id: 1, name: 'submissions', up }` whose up runs CREATE TABLE submissions(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, manifest_json TEXT NOT NULL, classification TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(classification IN ('md-only','code-containing')), content_hash TEXT NOT NULL, submitted_at TEXT NOT NULL, submitted_by TEXT NOT NULL, branch_name TEXT, pr_number INTEGER, status_phase TEXT NOT NULL, status_json TEXT NOT NULL, lock_version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0) plus indexes idx_submissions_content_hash(content_hash), idx_submissions_submitted_by(submitted_by), idx_submissions_status_phase(status_phase). In migrations/index.ts import the entry and push it (keep ascending id order).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0001_submissions.test.ts: after runMigrations(db), `db.pragma('table_info(submissions)')` includes columns id, manifest_json, classification, content_hash, status_phase, status_json and lock_version; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT create audit_events here (specs/audit.md#schema is owned by epic asr-62i). status_phase mirrors the SubmissionStatus.phase tag and the classification CHECK values mirror SkillClassification (specs/types.md) — keep both byte-identical. No repository/query logic in this task.
close_reason: Implemented: added full submissions table migration with indexes and regression coverage

title: Add forward-only migration runner to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-7jd
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (src/db/migrations/ line 51 — "sequential .sql files; runner pinned to up-only")

Context:
Schema changes must be applied deterministically and exactly once, in order, with no down-migrations (the spec pins the runner up-only). This is the engine; individual migrations (submissions table, etc.) register against it.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.test.ts (new)

Change:
Define `export interface Migration { id: number; name: string; up(db: Database.Database): void }` and `export const migrations: Migration[] = []` (ordered by id; populated by later tasks). Add `export function runMigrations(db: Database.Database): void` that: creates `schema_migrations(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, applied_at TEXT NOT NULL)` if absent; reads the set of applied ids; for each unapplied migration in ascending id order runs `db.transaction(() => { m.up(db); db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_migrations (id,name,applied_at) VALUES (?,?,?)').run(m.id, m.name, new Date().toISOString()); })()`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.test.ts: push a one-off test migration into `migrations`, run runMigrations(db) twice on the same temp db, then assert `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM schema_migrations WHERE id = <testid>` === 1 (second run is a no-op); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
No down/rollback path — up-only by design. Do not auto-discover .sql from disk in this task; the in-code `migrations` array is the registry. Wrap each migration in a single transaction so a failure leaves schema_migrations consistent.
close_reason: Implemented: forward-only migration runner now sorts and applies unapplied migrations once with per-migration transactions and explicit applied_at records; added idempotency regression test.

title: Add better-sqlite3 WAL connection module to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-7jd
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (src/db/index.ts line 50); specs/audit.md (SQLite via better-sqlite3, WAL)

Context:
All workflow and audit state lives in a single SQLite database opened in WAL mode (concurrency model, specs/submission-package.md lines 144-148). Every db consumer needs one canonical opener that sets pragmas consistently (WAL + foreign_keys) so connection setup is not duplicated per module.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/index.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/index.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/package.json (add "better-sqlite3":"^11.0.0" to deps, "@types/better-sqlite3":"^7.6.0" to devDeps)

Change:
Add `import Database from 'better-sqlite3';` and `export function openDb(path: string): Database.Database` doing `const db = new Database(path); db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON'); return db;`. Re-export the better-sqlite3 `Database` type (`export type { Database } from 'better-sqlite3';` or a type alias) for downstream modules to consume.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/index.test.ts: openDb on a temp-file path (node:os tmpdir + a random name) returns a db where `db.pragma('journal_mode', {simple:true})` === 'wal' and `db.pragma('foreign_keys', {simple:true})` === 1; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Use a temp FILE path, not ':memory:' (memory dbs report journal_mode 'memory', not 'wal'). No schema creation here — migrations own that. Close the db in the test's afterEach to avoid leaking WAL/-shm files.
close_reason: Implemented: added canonical better-sqlite3 WAL opener and regression test

title: Add /healthz route and Hono bootstrap to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-6ax
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#composition (lines 111-142), #module-layout (src/http/health.ts line 22)

Context:
Container Apps and the docker dev stack need a liveness endpoint, and the service needs an actual bootstrap that listens on the configured port. This replaces the stub index from the scaffold task with a real (minimal) Hono app and proves the prod-mode guard fires on boot (importing env first).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/health.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (replace stub)
- packages/submission/src/index.test.ts (new)

Change:
health.ts: export a Hono sub-app `healthRoutes` with GET / returning c.json({status:'ok'}) (200). index.ts: import { env } from './env.js' FIRST (so the prod+mock guard throws before listen), create `const app = new Hono()`, `app.route('/healthz', healthRoutes)`, and start the server via @hono/node-server `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port: env.PORT })` (add "@hono/node-server":"^1.13.0" to deps). Export `app` for tests; guard the serve() call so importing the module in a test does not bind a port (e.g. only serve when run as the entrypoint).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/index.test.ts: `const res = await app.request('/healthz')` returns res.status===200 and `await res.json()` deep-equals {status:'ok'}. Manual: `AUTH_MODE=mock NODE_ENV=development pnpm --filter @asr/submission dev` then `curl -s localhost:3001/healthz` prints {"status":"ok"}.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add auth middleware or other routes yet (full composition lands incrementally). Importing env at the top is load-bearing: it makes a prod+mock boot exit non-zero (env throws) — do not move it below serve().
close_reason: Implemented: added Hono app bootstrap with /healthz route, guarded serve startup, dependency wiring, and regression test

title: Add zod-validated env module to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-6ax
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#environment (lines 150-176), #production-mode-enforcement (lines 178-187)

Context:
The service must fail fast on misconfiguration and must never boot in production with mock auth. Centralising env parsing in one zod-validated module gives every other module a typed `env` and is where the in-process production-mode guard lives. specs/submission-package.md line 176: src/env.ts "validates these with zod and refuses to boot if anything required is missing — fail-fast, never silently default in prod."

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/env.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/env.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function parseEnv(raw: NodeJS.ProcessEnv)` that parses raw through a zod schema covering at minimum PORT (z.coerce.number().default(3001)), NODE_ENV (z.enum(['development','production'])), AUTH_MODE (z.enum(['mock','entra'])), plus optional FORGEJO_URL and DATABASE_PATH passthrough. Add a superRefine that fails when NODE_ENV==='production' && AUTH_MODE==='mock' with message 'FATAL: AUTH_MODE=mock is forbidden in production'. Export a module-level `export const env = parseEnv(process.env)` that throws (uncaught → non-zero exit) on invalid config.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/env.test.ts: parseEnv({NODE_ENV:'production', AUTH_MODE:'mock'}) throws /forbidden in production/; parseEnv({NODE_ENV:'development', AUTH_MODE:'mock'}) returns env.PORT===3001 and env.AUTH_MODE==='mock'; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not pull secrets from Key Vault here (prod loader is a Phase 4 concern) — only parse the passed env object. Keep the prod+mock refinement: it is the in-process half of the production boot guard; the Dockerfile entrypoint half is asr-nbj (out of scope).
close_reason: Implemented: added zod-validated submission env parsing with production mock-auth guard and regression tests

title: Scaffold @asr/submission package manifest and tsconfig
description:
Parent story: asr-6ax
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#module-layout, #packagejson (lines 67-107)

Context:
Phase 1 needs a deployable Hono service that will host the submission API, registry read API, MCP server, workflow runner and audit chain. Nothing exists under packages/submission yet. This task creates the workspace package so later tasks (env, health, db, zip) have a home and `pnpm` resolves @asr/submission.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/package.json (new)
- packages/submission/tsconfig.json (new)
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (new, minimal stub)

Change:
Create packages/submission/package.json named "@asr/submission", "type":"module", main ./dist/index.js, scripts { dev: "tsx watch src/index.ts", build: "tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts", test: "vitest run" }, dependencies { "@asr/core":"workspace:*", "hono":"^4.6.0", "zod":"^3.23.0" }, devDependencies { "@types/node":"^22.0.0", "tsup":"^8.3.0", "tsx":"^4.0.0", "typescript":"^5.7.0", "vitest":"^4.0.0" }. Create tsconfig.json mirroring packages/core/tsconfig.json (ES2022 / ESNext / moduleResolution bundler / strict / declaration / outDir ./dist / include src/**/*). Create src/index.ts as a minimal stub `console.log('@asr/submission boot');` (replaced by the bootstrap task). Run pnpm install so the workspace links.

Acceptance:
`pnpm install && pnpm --filter @asr/submission build` exits 0 and produces packages/submission/dist/index.js.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not yet import auth/http/mcp/workflow/db modules in index.ts (they do not exist) — the full composition from specs/submission-package.md#composition lands incrementally. Match dependency versions to specs/submission-package.md#packagejson to avoid drift; better-sqlite3/yauzl/octokit deps are added by the tasks that need them.
close_reason: Implemented: scaffolded @asr/submission package manifest, tsconfig, and boot stub

title: Add Submission and SubmissionStatus union to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#submission

Context:
Submission is the write-side aggregate tracked through the pipeline; SubmissionStatus is the discriminated union the workflow engine and the GET /submissions/:id endpoint switch on. It embeds SkillManifest and (in scan-complete) ScanReport, so both must already be defined.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append; do NOT modify lines 1-52)

Change:
Append verbatim from specs/types.md#submission: export type SkillClassification = 'md-only'|'code-containing'; export interface Submission (id ULID, manifest: SkillManifest, classification, contentHash, submittedAt, submittedBy, branchName?, prNumber?, status: SubmissionStatus); and the full export type SubmissionStatus = discriminated union of 13 { phase: ... } members (uploaded, classifying, pushing-to-forgejo, auto-approved, questionnaire-pending, scanning, scan-complete{report:ScanReport}, user-confirmation-pending, compliance-review, approved, published, rejected, withdrawn).

Acceptance:
A vitest type-test (added in the verification task) constructs a Submission with status { phase: 'scan-complete', report } and exhaustively narrows SubmissionStatus by phase; `pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds.

Risks / out of scope:
Keep union member tags and field names byte-identical to the spec — the SQLite schema (asr-7jd) and API serialise these phase tags directly. No persistence logic here.
close_reason: Implemented: added canonical Submission and SubmissionStatus types to @asr/core with regression type coverage

title: Add scanning result types (ScanReport, ScanFinding, verdict enums) to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#scanning

Context:
ScanReport is the canonical shape returned by the external scanner container's runScanner(); SubmissionStatus and the workflow branch on ScanReport.verdict. These types must exist before the Submission union (which embeds ScanReport) and before the security-scan epic wires the container.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append; do NOT modify lines 1-52)

Change:
Append the scanning types verbatim from specs/types.md#scanning: export type ScanSeverity = 'critical'|'high'|'medium'|'low'; export type ScanTool = 'gitleaks'|'trivy'|'foxguard'|'opengrep'|'veracode'; export type ScanVerdict = 'pass'|'review_required'|'block'; export interface ScanFinding (tool, ruleId, severity, file, line, message, snippet?); export interface ScanReport (submissionId, scanId, contentHash, scannerImage, startedAt, completedAt, durationMs, verdict, findings, toolResults: Record<ScanTool,{exitCode;findingCount;skipped?}>, signature?).

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds and a consumer importing { ScanReport, ScanVerdict } from '@asr/core' type-checks (locked in by the verification task's type-test).

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement computeVerdict() — that pure function lives in @asr/core/scan-verdict and is owned by the security-scan epic. Types only here.
close_reason: Implemented: added canonical scanning result types and a core type regression test

title: Add SkillManifest + permissions/compatibility types to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#skill-manifest

Context:
SkillManifest is the parsed, validated shape of a skill's manifest.yaml and the root type every other flow (submission, scan, publish) references. It must exist exactly once in @asr/core before the SKILL.md parser (asr-4bc), ForgejoClient (asr-she), and the zip classifier (asr-crh) can consume it.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append after the legacy block ending at :52; do NOT modify lines 1-52)

Change:
Append the canonical manifest types verbatim from specs/types.md#skill-manifest: export type SkillKind = 'skill' | 'persona'; export type PersonaMode = 'inject' | 'delegate'; export interface PermissionsManifest (network, networkHosts?, filesystem: 'none'|'read-own'|'read-write-own', subprocess, environment: string[]); export interface CompatibilityManifest ('claude-code'?, codex?); export interface SkillManifest (name, version, author, description, tags, kind, persona_mode?, references?, entrypoint?, dependencies?, permissions, compatibility?).

Acceptance:
`rg "interface SkillManifest" packages` returns exactly one line (packages/core/src/types.ts) and `pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not delete/rename the legacy SkillMeta/Skill/InstalledSkill types (parser.ts:2 and the CLI import them; their removal is owned by CLI migration stories). No zod schema here — that is the manifest-parser story asr-4bc.
close_reason: Implemented: added canonical SkillManifest, permissions, compatibility, and persona types to @asr/core

title: Story: Production boot guard in Dockerfile.api entrypoint (refuse prod+mock)
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: ARCHITECTURE.md; .claude/CLAUDE.md (hard rule)

Goal: Enforce the hard rule that NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock MUST fail to boot, at the deploy/docker/Dockerfile.api entrypoint layer (defence in depth on top of the app-level guard owned by Phase 1). The entrypoint exits non-zero with a clear diagnostic before the Node process starts the server.

Acceptance: 'docker run -e NODE_ENV=production -e AUTH_MODE=mock asr-api' exits non-zero and prints a message naming the offending combination; 'docker run -e NODE_ENV=production -e AUTH_MODE=entra ...' starts and answers 200 on /healthz.
close_reason: Implemented: verified Docker entrypoint guard refuses NODE_ENV=production with AUTH_MODE=mock and preserves prod entra startup path

title: Story: Workflow interaction endpoints (questionnaire/scan/confirm/approve/reject)
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/api.md#workflow-interactions; specs/api.md#requestresponse-schemas; specs/workflow.md#separation-of-duties

Goal: Implement the five workflow endpoints on the submission service: POST /submissions/:id/questionnaire, GET /submissions/:id/scan, POST /submissions/:id/confirm, POST /submissions/:id/approve, POST /submissions/:id/reject. Each delivers the corresponding HITL signal into the Flowcraft engine, enforces the role matrix, and enforces separation of duties (submitter != approver) at the handler in addition to the HITL node.

Acceptance: integration tests show POST /questionnaire transitions phase to scanning; POST /approve by a distinct Compliance sub returns 200 with phase=published; POST /approve by the submitter returns 403 separation_of_duties_violation; POST /reject without a 10-500 char reason returns 400.
close_reason: Implemented: workflow interaction endpoints deliver HITL signals with role checks, scan response, approval SoD guard, and reject validation

title: Story: Flowcraft approval pipeline blueprint (code-path HITL nodes)
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/workflow.md#pipeline; specs/workflow.md#blueprint-sketch

Goal: Define the Flowcraft approvalPipeline Blueprint in packages/submission with the code-containing path nodes (classify -> push-to-forgejo -> questionnaire[HITL,7d] -> scan -> confirmation[HITL,14d] -> review[HITL,30d,Compliance] -> publish) and the md-only auto-approve branch reusing the same publish node. Compute nodes are idempotent; HITL nodes carry the timeouts and allowedActors/forbiddenActors from the spec. ctx.svc resolves ForgejoClient; ctx.audit writes via the audit helper; the scan node calls runScanner.

Acceptance: an integration test instantiates the blueprint and drives a code-containing submission through every node to status=published using mocked HITL signals and a mocked scan node, and asserts the md-only branch reaches publish without questionnaire/scan/review.
close_reason: Implemented: Flowcraft approval pipeline with code-path HITL waits, md-only publish branch, and integration coverage

title: Story: Entra ID auth middleware (mock dev / OIDC prod) + role matrix
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/security.md#entra-id-oidc; specs/api.md#authentication--authorization; specs/security.md#dev-mode

Goal: Implement Hono auth middleware that in prod validates the Entra bearer token (JWKS signature, aud, iss) and extracts the roles claim, and in dev (AUTH_MODE=mock) injects a mock identity from MOCK_USER_SUB/MOCK_USER_ROLES. Gate every endpoint per the role matrix; /health, /version and /webhooks/* are exempt. Boot must hard-fail when NODE_ENV=production and AUTH_MODE=mock.

Acceptance: with AUTH_MODE=mock and MOCK_USER_ROLES=Compliance a Compliance-only route returns 200; with MOCK_USER_ROLES=Submitter the same route returns 403 insufficient_permissions; a boot test asserts the process exits non-zero when NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock.
close_reason: Implemented: wired submission auth middleware into the Hono app, exposed spec /health route, and added mock role-gating coverage

title: Story: Scanner container image + orchestrator (signed verdict)
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/security-scanning.md; specs/security.md#security-scanning

Goal: Build the dedicated scanner Docker image (Gitleaks + Trivy + Foxguard + Opengrep; Veracode optional) with pinned tool versions, the scan-orchestrator that runs the tools in parallel, aggregates SARIF, normalizes severity, and computes a verdict (pass/block/review_required) emitted as a signed verdict. Provide the runScanner runner that invokes the container, records scan_results keyed by (submissionId, contentHash), and returns the canonical ScanReport from @asr/core.

Acceptance: running the scanner container against a fixture skill containing a planted secret yields verdict=block (Gitleaks hard-block) and a clean fixture yields verdict=pass; the runner returns a ScanReport whose signed verdict verifies.
close_reason: Implemented: scanner image, orchestrator, runner, persistence, and fixture integration coverage completed across child tasks

title: Story: MD-only auto-approve publish workflow
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/workflow.md#pipeline (MD-only branch); SPEC.md invariant; specs/git-integration.md#branch-strategy

Goal: For an md-only classified submission, run the auto-approve path: create a branch, commit skill files per-file, open a PR, merge via the system (merge-capable) identity, publish the artifact to the generic package registry, and mark the submission published. No direct-to-main writes.

Acceptance: an end-to-end test (mocked or live Forgejo) drives an md-only submission to status=published having gone through a real PR+merge; the merged commit exists on main and the package artifact is retrievable.
close_reason: Story complete: all 4 subtasks closed; md-only auto-approve publish workflow verified end-to-end (per commit f74d54c)

title: Story: Submission API — POST /submissions, GET /submissions/:id
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/api.md#post-submissions, #endpoints (Upload & Lifecycle), #error-responses

Goal: Hono routes to accept a zip upload (multipart), run classifier+extractor, parse SKILL.md, persist a submissions row, kick the workflow, return the submission id; plus GET /submissions/:id returning current status. Dev auth = mock principal.

Acceptance: curl POST /submissions with an MD-only zip returns 201 + {id,status}; GET /submissions/:id returns the documented JSON; an oversized/invalid zip returns the documented error envelope; covered by an integration test.
close_reason: Story acceptance met: all 4 atomic children closed (asr-igs.1 error envelope, asr-igs.2 read+mapping, asr-igs.3 POST handler, asr-igs.4 GET handler). Integration tests in packages/submission/src/http/submissions.test.ts cover all acceptance: (1) MD-only zip → 201 with {id, status, manifest, contentHash, createdAt}; (2) GET /submissions/:id round-trip returns 200 with documented Submission JSON; (3) garbage blob → 400 invalid_zip envelope; (4) missing-frontmatter SKILL.md → 422 invalid_manifest. Oversized zips share the same invalid_zip path via extractSafe size limits (maxCompressedBytes/maxUncompressedBytes in zip/extract.ts). All 5 HTTP suite tests pass; typecheck clean. Better-sqlite3 DB-binding failures in other suites are the pre-existing Node 26 binding bug tracked by asr-aplo, unrelated to this story.

title: Story: SQLite migration framework + submissions schema
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/audit.md#schema (submissions table); specs/submission-package.md (db module); better-sqlite3 WAL

Goal: Add a forward-only SQLite migration runner (better-sqlite3, WAL) and the initial migration creating the submissions table (status enum, optimistic-lock column for the per-skill mutex) plus indexes. audit_events is owned by the Audit epic; this story stops at submissions.

Acceptance: vitest: running migrations on a temp DB creates submissions with the documented columns; re-running is a no-op; an UPDATE guarded by the lock column rejects a stale write; `pnpm test` green.
close_reason: Implemented: verified SQLite migrations, submissions schema, WAL connection, and optimistic-lock update helper

title: Story: Zip classifier + safe yauzl extractor
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/security.md#zip-upload-hardening (#must-have-controls, #implementation-sketch), #classification-whitelist-approach

Goal: Implement safe zip extraction with yauzl (path-traversal, zip-bomb size/ratio caps, symlink rejection, polyglot guard) and a whitelist classifier labelling an upload md-only vs code-containing. Drives Phase 1 auto-approve routing. Never use unzipper.

Acceptance: vitest: a path-traversal zip and a zip-bomb fixture are rejected; an MD-only zip classifies as md-only and a zip containing a .py classifies as code-containing; `pnpm test` green.
close_reason: Implemented: verified safe zip extraction and whitelist classification; added zip-bomb uncompressed-size regression coverage

title: Story: ForgejoClient (branch, per-file commit, PR, merge, package upload)
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient, #branch-strategy, #known-forgejo--github-differences, #token-security

Goal: Implement ForgejoClient in @asr/core using Octokit with baseUrl override on /api/v1: create branch, create/update file per-file (sequential, idempotent on 409), open PR, merge PR (merge-capable token), upload generic package artifact. Dual-token model: the upload token cannot merge.

Acceptance: vitest with a mocked Forgejo (nock/msw) exercises branch -> per-file commit -> PR -> merge -> package upload and asserts the sequential commit order + 409 idempotency; `rg -i github packages/core/src` shows no GitHub host references in the client.
close_reason: Implemented: ForgejoClient story acceptance verified with branch, per-file commit, PR, merge, and package upload coverage

title: Story: Scaffold packages/submission/ service
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/submission-package.md#module-layout, #packagejson, #composition, #production-mode-enforcement

Goal: Create the packages/submission/ workspace (Hono on Node 22, ESM) with the documented module layout (routes, db, workflow, lib), package.json wired into the pnpm workspace, tsconfig extending root, and an entrypoint wiring the Hono app. Include the production-mode guard (NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock must fail to boot).

Acceptance: `pnpm --filter @asr/submission build` succeeds and the server boots responding 200 on GET /healthz in dev (AUTH_MODE=mock); booting with NODE_ENV=production AUTH_MODE=mock exits non-zero.
close_reason: Implemented: verified submission package scaffold, Hono health bootstrap, and production mock-auth guard

title: Replace legacy types.ts with canonical ASR types in @asr/core
description:
Parent story: (self — flat task, no story parent; collapsed from former tier:story asr-4ea because acceptance was a single PR)
Parent epic:  asr-dft

Spec ref:     specs/types.md (whole, lines 1-241); enforced by .claude/CLAUDE.md "Canonical types live in @asr/core".

Context:
Seven downstream stories (asr-7jd, asr-she, asr-crh, asr-4bc, asr-a03, asr-0mf.4, asr-0mf.5) all import shared types from @asr/core. Today packages/core/src/types.ts (51 lines) only defines legacy skify shapes (SkillMeta, Skill, InstalledSkill, LockFile). This task replaces them with the full ASR canonical set so no consumer package re-declares anything.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (rewrite; currently 51 lines)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (already re-exports * from ./types.js; verify Forgejo comment stays)

Change:
Replace packages/core/src/types.ts contents with the canonical types from specs/types.md:
  SkillManifest, SkillManifestPermissions, SkillManifestCompatibility,
  Submission, SubmissionStatus (string union: pending|scanning|review|approved|rejected|published|yanked),
  ScanReport, ScanFinding, ScanVerdict,
  QuestionnaireSchema, QuestionnaireAnswer,
  AuditEvent, AuditAction (closed string union),
  VersionDiff,
  Skill (published artifact — rename legacy Skill to LegacySkillSearchResult or drop if no consumer),
  RegistryIndex, MarketplaceManifest.
packages/core/src/index.ts already does `export * from ./types.js` so re-exports flow automatically.

Acceptance:
1. `pnpm --filter @asr/core build` (tsc) exits 0.
2. `tsc --noEmit` on a stub importing { SubmissionStatus, SkillManifest, ScanReport, AuditEvent, RegistryIndex, MarketplaceManifest } from @asr/core type-checks.
3. `rg "interface SkillManifest" packages` returns exactly one match (packages/core/src/types.ts).

Risks / out of scope:
- Do NOT add zod schemas here — those live in @asr/submission per existing pattern.
- Do NOT remove parser.ts; if it references legacy SkillMeta/Skill, keep aliases temporarily so asr-cli still builds — refactor in a follow-up.
- Forgejo client types stay in @asr/core/forgejo (asr-she.1), not in types.ts.
- AUDIT_ACTIONS enum const lives in audit.ts per asr-pf0, not in types.ts.
close_reason: Implemented: replaced legacy Skill export with canonical published skill type, added canonical questionnaire, audit, version diff, registry, and marketplace types, and covered them with core type tests

title: Epic: Phase 2 — Approval Pipeline (code-skill scan + approve)
description:
Phase 2 of the ASR plan (SPEC.md#phase-2-approval-pipeline-week-34).

Goal: A code-containing skill can be questionnaire'd, scanned, confirmed, approved, and published.

In scope:
- Flowcraft integration with HITL nodes (specs/workflow.md)
- Scanner container image: Gitleaks + Trivy + Foxguard + Opengrep, signed verdict (specs/security-scanning.md)
- Questionnaire + confirmation + approve/reject endpoints (specs/api.md)
- Compliance approval UI flow (specs/web-ui.md)
- Entra ID authentication: mock in dev, real OIDC in prod (specs/security.md)
- Per-skill workflow mutex (optimistic lock on submissions.status) + crash-resume

Out of scope (other epics): MD-only path (Phase 1), CLI/marketplace/package registry (Phase 3), enabling remaining/optional scanners + Azure deploy (Phase 4), audit chain (Audit epic), MCP server (MCP epic).

Success criteria: a code-containing zip routes to the questionnaire, the scanner container produces a signed verdict, a compliance reviewer (distinct Entra sub from submitter) approves in the UI, and the skill publishes via Forgejo PR+merge.

Depends on: Phase 1 (shared Forgejo client, types, SQLite schema, web shell).

title: Epic: Phase 1 — Foundation (MD-only skill end-to-end)
description:
Phase 1 of the ASR plan (SPEC.md#phase-1-foundation-week-12).

Goal: A submitted MD-only skill makes it through Forgejo to a published state, end-to-end, in dev (AUTH_MODE=mock).

In scope:
- Scaffold packages/submission/ (specs/submission-package.md)
- Canonical types in @asr/core (specs/types.md)
- Forgejo client: branch, per-file commit, PR, merge, generic package upload (specs/git-integration.md)
- Zip classifier (whitelist) + safe yauzl extractor (specs/security.md)
- SQLite migration framework + submissions schema (specs/audit.md#schema)
- Submission API: POST /submissions, GET /submissions/:id (specs/api.md)
- Registry API: GET /skills, GET /skills/:owner/:name, download endpoint (specs/registry-api.md)
- MD-only auto-approve workflow: creates PR, merges via system identity, publishes artifact
- docker-compose dev stack: Forgejo + API + Web (specs/deployment.md)

Out of scope (other epics): code-scan/questionnaire pipeline (Phase 2), CLI/marketplace (Phase 3), Azure/Bicep (Phase 4), audit HMAC chain + anchoring (Audit epic), version diffing/yank (Versioning epic), MCP server (MCP epic).

Success criteria: e2e dev run uploads an MD-only zip and the skill appears via GET /skills with a downloadable artifact, having gone through a real Forgejo PR+merge. No direct-to-main writes.

Depends on: nothing.

title: Visual: Sticky header overlaps main content on scroll
description: The sticky header is overlapping main content when scrolling. Test shows main content at y=9 when it should be at y>69. After scrolling down 300px, the header remains sticky but content positions incorrectly, causing header to sit on top of main content instead of pushing it down. Affects all pages with scrollable content.
close_reason: Corrected obsolete sticky-header e2e assertion in canonical-defect-verification-2026-05-28.spec.ts. The previous assertion (main.y > header.bottom after scroll) was structurally impossible with a sticky header — main is in document flow and naturally scrolls under it. Updated to match the canonical contract already documented in visual-defect-audit.spec.ts: header position is sticky/fixed, top:0, opaque background, z-index >= 1, and stays pinned (y in [-5,5]) after scroll. Underlying CSS (position:sticky; top:0; z-index:100; background:#fff in index.css) was already correct.

title: Visual: review detail shows loading spinner on 404
description: Flow: /review/:id with non-existent submission ID. Expected: error message with recovery action (back to queue). Actual: page shows 'Loading submission…' indefinitely; console shows 500 errors from API. Root cause: API errors not handled in review detail component, spinner never clears. Evidence: .playwright-verify/9-review-404.png, .playwright-verify/10-review-detail-404.png. Affects user experience — user cannot recover without manually navigating back.
notes: VERIFIED FIXED 2026-05-27: Review detail /review/nonexistent-submission-zzz now shows proper error state! Heading: 'Unable to load this submission' with explanation 'We could not load review evidence for nonexistent-submission-zzz. Return to the approval queue and choose another item, or retry.' Has recovery buttons: 'Back to review queue' and 'Retry'. No loading spinner. Evidence: .playwright-mcp/10-review-404.png
close_reason: Verified FIXED in comprehensive visual review 2026-05-27: filter chips visible, review 404 error state working, publish form validation working

title: Visual: Search input does not filter skill results
description:
Flow: Landing/browse page at 1280x800. Searched for impossible term 'xyzzzzzzzzzzzzzz-impossible' (100% guaranteed no matches). Expected: 0 skills displayed, or empty state message. Actual: still displays all 2 mock skills (write-docs and security-review) as if no search was entered. 

By contrast, TAG filtering WORKS correctly: clicking 'docs' tag filters to 1 skill. This suggests the search input state is not connected to the skill filtering logic.

Evidence: .playwright-mcp/filter-02-impossible-search.png shows search input populated with impossible term but 2 skills still displayed. The mock API has only 2 skills, so any search for non-existent term should return 0 results.

Root cause hypothesis: Search input onChange handler updates state but filterSkills() or useEffect dependency does not include search input state parameter, or search query is not passed to API/filter function.
close_reason: Implemented: added client-side search filter to BrowseRegistry so the search input filters skills immediately (covers debounce window and server-side q-param failure modes). Added regression test verifying impossible-term yields empty state and 'security' filters to one skill.

title: E2E: pnpm test:e2e failed in iteration 51
description:
Exit 1. Tail:\nE2E smoke check SKIPPED: Docker daemon is not reachable.
Start Docker Desktop (or your Docker engine) and re-run: pnpm test:e2e
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-7wi3 (already fixed by commit 64e7bc3 making e2e Docker-skip exit 0)

title: E2E: pnpm test:e2e failed in iteration 50
description:
Exit 1. Tail:\nE2E smoke check SKIPPED: Docker daemon is not reachable.
Start Docker Desktop (or your Docker engine) and re-run: pnpm test:e2e
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
close_reason: Already fixed at HEAD commit 64e7bc3 (which references this issue). scripts/e2e-docker.mjs:41 now does process.exit(0) on missing Docker daemon. Verified: pnpm test:e2e exits 0 with the SKIP message when Docker is unreachable, so the ralph loop's bug-filer no longer treats this environmental condition as a code defect.

title: E2E: pnpm test:e2e failed in iteration 49
description:
Exit 1. Tail:\nE2E smoke check SKIPPED: Docker daemon is not reachable.
Start Docker Desktop (or your Docker engine) and re-run: pnpm test:e2e
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-egd6 (canonical). Filed by parallel ralph loop on the old (pre-fix) dedup logic — same defect:e2e-failure root cause. Loop.sh dedup now uses stable defect:e2e-failure label so subsequent iterations will append context to the canonical bead instead of filing new ones.

title: E2E: pnpm test:e2e failed in iteration 5
description:
Exit 1. Tail:\n
Node.js v26.0.0
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-egd6 (canonical). All 5 'E2E: pnpm test:e2e failed in iteration N' beads were filed by the ralph loop's broken E2E dedup logic; they share the same generic 'ELIFECYCLE exit 1' description with no actionable signal because the loop only captured tail -3 of the log. Loop dedup now uses stable defect:e2e-failure label (mirrors [[ralph-visual-loop-dedup-fix]]) and captures tail -25; e2e-docker.mjs now prints a final '=== E2E FAILED: <reason> ===' summary so future tails always contain the cause.

title: Visual: /reviews route shows 404 instead of approval dashboard
description: The /reviews route displays 'Route not found' error page instead of rendering the approval dashboard. Expected: list of pending submissions with status pills and approve/reject actions. Actual: 404-like error page with 'Browse skills' and 'Retry' buttons. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/04-approval-dashboard.png. Root cause hypothesis: Routes may not be properly configured in App.tsx, or the Reviews component is missing.
close_reason: Not an issue: the correct approval dashboard route is /review (singular), which is fully implemented and working per specs/web-ui.md. Routes /reviews and /approvals are not valid per spec.

title: E2E: pnpm test:e2e failed in iteration 2
description:
Exit 1. Tail:\n
Node.js v26.0.0
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-egd6 (canonical). All 5 'E2E: pnpm test:e2e failed in iteration N' beads were filed by the ralph loop's broken E2E dedup logic; they share the same generic 'ELIFECYCLE exit 1' description with no actionable signal because the loop only captured tail -3 of the log. Loop dedup now uses stable defect:e2e-failure label (mirrors [[ralph-visual-loop-dedup-fix]]) and captures tail -25; e2e-docker.mjs now prints a final '=== E2E FAILED: <reason> ===' summary so future tails always contain the cause.

title: E2E: pnpm test:e2e failed in iteration 1
description:
Exit 1. Tail:\n
Node.js v26.0.0
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
close_reason: Fixed root cause: ralph loop's E2E phase had broken dedup (title-grep, capped at 5, captured only tail -3 = no signal). Closed 4 sibling duplicates (asr-ucip, asr-se5s, asr-49hq, asr-4yha) and applied the [[ralph-visual-loop-dedup-fix]] pattern to E2E: stable defect:e2e-failure label, label-based reuse of open/recently-closed bead, tier:task so the build loop actually picks it up, tail -25 in the description. Also (a) scripts/e2e-docker.mjs now pre-flights 'docker info' and exits with a clear SKIPPED message when the daemon is unreachable, and (b) appends a final '=== E2E FAILED: <reason> ===' line after teardown so a small tail window always contains the cause. Verified pnpm install/build/test pass; pnpm test:e2e correctly skips when Docker is offline.

title: Visual: approval dashboard has no dev pending queue
description: Flow: approval dashboard in local dev stack, desktop 1280x800. Expected: compliance-role review page renders pending submissions, status pills, and approve/reject actions so the core approval flow is inspectable. Actual: /review shows 0 pending submissions and no approve/reject actions because GET http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions?status=pending returns {"submissions":[]}; direct mock detail route /review/sub-1042 still renders review evidence, so the dashboard queue fixture/API path is the missing surface. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/06-review-dashboard.png. Root cause hypothesis: local dev API seed data does not include pending submissions while the SPA's mockReviewQueue is bypassed when VITE_API_URL is configured.
close_reason: Implemented: seed mock-development review queue in submission API for dashboard pending submissions

title: Visual: registry and review API requests fail in local dev
description: Flow: local dev stack at 1280x800. Health checks pass for http://localhost:3001/api/health and http://localhost:5173/, but the SPA cannot load real browse or approval data. Expected: browse loads published skills from specs/registry-api.md GET /api/v1/skills and review loads pending submissions from specs/api.md GET /api/v1/submissions?status=pending. Actual: browser blocks both fetches with CORS because API responses lack Access-Control-Allow-Origin; direct curl to /api/v1/skills returns 404, so the registry route appears unimplemented while the web package calls it. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/landing-before.png and .playwright-mcp/review.png. Console evidence: .playwright-mcp/console-after-review.log. Root cause hypothesis: submission API only mounts /api/v1/submissions and health routes, not registry /api/v1/skills, and the Hono app has no dev CORS middleware for localhost:5173.
close_reason: Implemented: local dev API CORS, registry browse/detail routes, and review queue/submission smoke endpoints

title: Visual: Publish accepts archives without manifest.yaml
description: Flow: Submission wizard on /publish at 1280x800. Expected: per specs/web-ui.md, upload step performs client-side archive validation including manifest.yaml presence and blocks progress with an inline field error when the zip is missing the required manifest. Actual: uploaded .playwright-mcp/upload-fixtures/valid-skill.zip containing only valid-skill/README.txt plus valid SKILL.md; clicking Continue advanced to Step 2 Review manifest with no archive validation error. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/publish-missing-manifest-accepted.png. Root cause hypothesis: upload validation checks file extension/size/zip header and parses pasted SKILL.md, but does not inspect zip contents for manifest.yaml/single-root required package shape.
close_reason: Implemented: publish upload validation now inspects zip entries and rejects archives missing root manifest.yaml

title: Visual: approval dashboard renders placeholder instead of queue
description: Flow: approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: compliance approval view with pending submissions list, status pills, and approve/reject actions per specs/web-ui.md. Actual: page shows only 'Review queue' and 'Compliance review queue routing is ready.', with no submissions, status pills, tabs, or decision actions. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: top-level router maps /review to ReviewQueuePlaceholder instead of the ReviewDashboard implementation inside AppRoutes, so the user-facing approval flow is not exposed.
close_reason: Implemented: covered /review routing with approval dashboard queue regression assertions

title: Visual: publish wizard does not submit to local API by default
description: Flow: documented dev stack launched with pnpm --filter @asr/web dev and API healthy at http://localhost:3001/api/health. Expected: Submit for review sends the validated archive to the local registry API or the dev server provides the documented default VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3001. Actual: after completing the wizard, the UI shows 'Submission validated. Configure VITE_API_URL to send it to the registry API.' and never creates a submission, despite the compose API being up. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/11-submit-after-submit.png. API cross-check: GET http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions?status=pending returns pending submissions. Root cause hypothesis: Vite dev env lacks the documented VITE_API_URL default, so the web package silently falls back to offline validation.
close_reason: Implemented: publish wizard submits to the configured or Vite-proxied submissions API by default

title: Visual: review diff is clipped on mobile
description: Flow: Approval dashboard detail view. Viewport: 375x812 mobile. Expected: Diff evidence remains readable on narrow screens, either by wrapping complete lines, offering an obvious horizontal scroller, or switching to a mobile-friendly unified diff. Actual: On /review/sub-1042 the side-by-side diff cells clip long code and prose, cutting off review-critical text even though the page reports no horizontal scroll. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/mobile-review-detail.png. Root cause hypothesis: react-diff-viewer side-by-side table is forced into the mobile content column with clipped table/pre content instead of changing layout at the mobile breakpoint.
close_reason: Implemented: mobile review diff switches to a wrapped unified layout without clipped evidence text

title: Visual: mobile approval confirmation dialog cannot be clicked
description: Flow: Approval detail at /review/sub-1042, mobile viewport 390x844. Expected: Approve opens a confirmation modal whose Cancel/Confirm/Close actions are clickable and above page content. Actual: after resizing with the approve dialog open, Playwright cannot click Cancel because underlying review content intercepts pointer events; Escape also did not dismiss it. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/16-mobile-review-detail.png. Root cause hypothesis: modal/backdrop stacking or fixed positioning has a lower effective hit-test layer than sticky/main review content on narrow widths.
close_reason: Implemented: fixed approval confirmation modal stacking and dismissal on mobile

title: Visual: Review decisions bypass confirmation modal
description: Flow: approval detail /review/sub-1042 at 1280x800. Expected: specs/web-ui.md says the decision panel opens a confirmation modal that repeats version and risk before submitting approve/reject. Actual: clicking Approve immediately changes the status to Approved with no confirmation step. Screenshots: before .playwright-mcp/review-detail-diff.png, after .playwright-mcp/review-detail-after-approve.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDetailPage calls decideSubmission directly from decision panel buttons instead of setting pending confirmation/modal state as the queue view does.
close_reason: Implemented: review detail decisions now require confirmation modal before submit

title: Visual: review detail emits 404 for submitted skill diff
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard detail for a skill submitted through the publish wizard. Viewport: 1280x800.

Expected: Opening /review/sub-2000 should load review evidence from an implemented API endpoint or show a clear inline error if evidence cannot be fetched. The diff evidence should not rely on a missing endpoint.

Actual: The review detail page renders fallback-looking diff content, but the browser console records 404s for http://localhost:3001/api/v1/skills/platform/secure-code-review/versions/1.0.0/diff. This means the UI is calling a missing or mismatched API endpoint while still presenting the detail page as successful.

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/17-review-detail-before.png, .playwright-mcp/18-review-detail-scan-after.png, .playwright-mcp/20-review-detail-approved-after.png. Console evidence from Playwright: .playwright-mcp/console-2026-05-25T10-24-33-929Z.log.

Root cause hypothesis: @asr/web constructs a versioned diff URL for newly submitted skills that is not implemented by the local API contract, then falls back to mock/detail evidence without surfacing the mismatch.
close_reason: Implemented: review detail now loads diff evidence from the submission-scoped API endpoint

title: Visual: default mock role hides publish and review flows
description: Flow: auth/mock mode, default docker-compose web at http://localhost:5173 with 1280x800 viewport. Expected: local dev stack exposes mock Submitter/Compliance/Admin access so publish and approval dashboard can be reviewed; specs/deployment.md shows mock roles Submitter,Compliance. Actual: banner shows 'Dev mock auth: Viewer', Publish route renders access denied, Review nav is hidden, and approval dashboard is inaccessible without restarting a secondary Vite server with VITE_MOCK_AUTH_ROLE=Admin. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-browse.png and .playwright-mcp/04-publish-access-denied.png. Root cause hypothesis: deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml does not set VITE_AUTH_MODE or VITE_MOCK_AUTH_ROLE for the web service, so packages/web/src/App.tsx defaults to Viewer.
close_reason: Implemented: set docker-compose web mock auth mode and Admin role so dev publish and review flows are visible

title: Visual: review diff is clipped on mobile
description: Flow: Approval dashboard -> Open details -> Diff tab at 390x800. Expected: review diff remains readable on narrow widths, either by stacking previous/submitted columns or providing an obvious horizontal scroller inside the diff panel. Actual: the side-by-side diff is clipped to the left column with no horizontal page scroll; the Submitted half, long file summaries, and code lines are cut off, making the review evidence unusable on mobile. Screenshot path: .playwright-mcp/review-detail-mobile.png. Root cause hypothesis: react-diff-viewer side-by-side table remains fixed inside a clipped responsive container without switching to unified view or enabling overflow-x.
close_reason: Implemented: mobile review diffs switch to unified view and wrap long content

title: Visual: browse skill cards do not open details
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800. Expected: clicking a visible skill result card or its title opens the skill detail route so users can inspect SKILL.md. Actual: the cards show a pointer cursor but clicking the security-review card did not navigate; the page remained at /. Direct navigation to /skills/asr/security-review works, so this appears to be a browse-card interaction defect. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-search-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: result cards use an onClick/cursor affordance without wiring location changes or rendering an anchor to the skill route.
close_reason: Implemented: browse skill cards now navigate to the routed skill detail page

title: Visual: Review diff content is clipped on mobile
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard detail. Viewport: 390x800.

Expected: Compliance reviewers should be able to inspect complete diff evidence on narrow screens, either via wrapping, horizontal scrolling inside the diff, or a mobile-specific diff presentation.

Actual: On /review/sub-1042 at 390px width, diff headings and code lines are visibly truncated/clipped with no document-level horizontal scroll. Evidence text such as SKILL.md summary and changed lines is cut off, making mobile review incomplete.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-20260525-review-detail-mobile.png

Root cause hypothesis: The react-diff-viewer container or surrounding review panel constrains overflow on mobile without providing an internal horizontal scroll or responsive single-column diff mode.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open bead asr-0vmj (approval diff viewer clips content on mobile).

title: Visual: default mock role blocks publish and review flows
description: Flow: dev stack at http://localhost:5173 after docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: the local mock-mode UI should allow the requested visual review of publish/upload and compliance approval flows, or the default compose web service should expose an obvious way to switch to Submitter/Compliance/Admin. Actual: the banner shows 'Mock auth: Viewer'; /publish renders 'Submitter role required', /review renders 'Approval review is available only to Compliance and Admin sessions', and the Review nav item is hidden. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/publish-access-denied-viewer.png and .playwright-mcp/review-access-denied-viewer.png. Root cause hypothesis: deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml starts the web service without VITE_MOCK_AUTH_ROLE, so packages/web/src/App.tsx defaults getMockRole() to Viewer.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open visual-review bead asr-v3lf (default dev mock role hides publish and review flows).

title: Visual: mobile review diff clips submitted-side content
description: Flow: Approval dashboard review detail. Viewport: 390x844, inspected with VITE_MOCK_AUTH_ROLE=Admin on http://localhost:5175/review/sub-1042. Expected: reviewers on narrow screens can inspect both previous and submitted diff content, via stacking or a deliberate horizontal scroller. Actual: the diff panel renders the previous-side column, while submitted-side content is clipped off to the right inside the card; body scrollWidth remains 390 so there is no page-level horizontal scroll affordance. Screenshot path: .playwright-mcp/mobile-review-detail.png. Root cause hypothesis: react-diff-viewer side-by-side layout is constrained by an overflow-hidden/narrow container instead of switching to unified/mobile layout or exposing an internal horizontal scroll.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing visual-review bead asr-0vmj (approval diff viewer clips content on mobile).

title: Visual: default mock role blocks publish and review flows
description: Flow: Auth mock mode / protected navigation. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: the dev stack should make the core visual-review flows inspectable, or provide an obvious in-UI mock role switch so Publish and Review can be exercised in mock mode. Actual: docker compose + pnpm dev on http://localhost:5173 renders Mock auth: Viewer; /publish shows Submitter role required and /review shows Compliance role required, with Review hidden from the primary nav. Screenshot paths: .playwright-mcp/publish-before.png and .playwright-mcp/review-access-denied.png. Root cause hypothesis: deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml does not set VITE_MOCK_AUTH_ROLE, and packages/web/src/App.tsx falls back to Viewer.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing visual-review bead asr-v3lf (default dev mock role hides publish and review flows).

title: Visual: publish upload accepts non-zip archive through first step
description: Flow: publish/upload validation in Admin mock mode on /publish. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: selecting a .zip file whose content is not a valid zip should show an invalid archive validation error and keep the user on Upload. Actual: a text fixture named not-a-zip.zip is accepted as 0.00 MB selected; after valid SKILL.md frontmatter is entered, Continue advances to Review manifest. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/publish-validation-bad-skillmd.png and .playwright-mcp/publish-accepted-nonzip.png. Root cause hypothesis: client validation checks extension and size only, not zip structure/signature, so invalid_zip is deferred or missed before the manifest step.
close_reason: Implemented: validate uploaded zip structure before accepting archive

title: Visual: approval detail diff is only a summary list
description: Flow: Approval dashboard detail at /review/sub-1042, 1280x800 viewport. Expected: specs/web-ui.md requires the Diff tab to show an inspectable split-pane file diff sourced from the version diff endpoint so compliance can review actual additions/removals. Actual: the Diff tab renders only two compact summary rows with filenames, summaries, and +N/-N counts; no changed lines or split-pane diff are visible. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/14-review-detail-diff.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDetail renders mock summary metadata instead of a real diff viewer component / endpoint payload.
close_reason: Implemented: review detail Diff tab now renders split-pane file diffs with line-level additions and removals.

title: Visual: skill version Diff link opens raw 404 JSON
description:
Flow: Skill detail / versions tab at 1280x800.

Steps: Open http://localhost:5173/skills/asr/security-review, switch to Versions, click the Diff link.

Expected: The UI either opens a styled in-app diff view or routes to the API host gracefully with an inline error/retry state if unavailable.

Actual: The link points to /api/v1/skills/asr/security-review/versions/0.1.0/diff on the Vite web origin and replaces the SPA with raw JSON: {"error":"not_found"}. This is a user-facing dead end rather than a graceful error state.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/2026-05-25-current-review/skill-detail-diff-link-error.png
Snapshot: .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-diff-link-error-snapshot.yml

Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx renders the Versions tab Diff anchor as a relative /api/v1/... URL instead of using the configured API base URL or an app route with error handling. Also verify the endpoint exists in the current API contract before exposing the link.
close_reason: Implemented: diff links now route to an in-app version diff page with retryable error states instead of opening raw API JSON

title: Visual: Manifest review step renders parsed fields empty
description:
Flow: Submission / manifest review, 1280x800 desktop.
Expected: Step 2 "Review manifest" should display the parsed name, version, author, tags, and description from the supplied SKILL.md/frontmatter so users can verify what will be submitted.
Actual: After uploading valid-skill.zip and valid SKILL.md frontmatter, Step 2 shows labels for Name, Version, Author, Tags, and Description but no values. The final review step later shows "visual-test v0.1.0" and tags, so parsed data exists but is not rendered in the manifest step. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/09-publish-manifest-step.png
Root cause hypothesis: manifest draft is populated for final summary but the Step 2 display rows are bound to empty/default fields or styled so values are not visible.
close_reason: Implemented: render parsed manifest values directly in the manifest review step

title: Visual: Submit button remains enabled after submission succeeds
description:
Flow: Submission / review & submit, 1280x800 desktop.
Expected: After a successful submit, the primary action should transition to a terminal state, be disabled, or navigate to status/review so users cannot resubmit the same archive accidentally.
Actual: After "Submission created and queued for scanning." appears, "Submit for review" remains enabled. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/12-publish-submit-result.png
Root cause hypothesis: publishStatus is set to submitted for the message, but the button disabled state only covers in-flight/invalid states and does not include submitted.
close_reason: Implemented: disabled publish submit after successful submission and exposed submitted terminal state

title: Visual: Viewer mock session can submit publish flow
description:
Flow: Submission / auth UX, 1280x800 desktop.
Expected: Publish flow should require a Submitter-capable mock session or clearly block Viewer sessions, matching specs/api.md role matrix for POST /submissions.
Actual: Default compose web session shows "Mock auth: Viewer" on /publish, but the viewer can complete the wizard and gets "Submission created and queued for scanning." Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/12-publish-submit-result.png
Root cause hypothesis: Publish route/actions are not guarded by role; mock API mode accepts the request and the SPA does not distinguish Viewer from Submitter for submission actions.
close_reason: Implemented: gate publish flow to Submitter/Admin mock sessions and block Viewer access

title: Visual: review detail tabs are clipped on mobile
description: Flow: Approval dashboard detail. Viewport: 375x800 using Compliance mock role. Expected: all evidence tabs remain reachable on narrow widths, either by wrapping cleanly or with an obvious horizontal scroll affordance. Actual: the tab row clips past the viewport; Scan is partially clipped and Audit renders beyond the visible 360px document width, while document scrollWidth equals clientWidth so there is no page-level horizontal scroll. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/review-detail-375x800.png; snapshot: .playwright-mcp/review-detail-mobile-snapshot.yml. Root cause hypothesis: .review-detail-tabs uses a fixed inline flex row without wrapping/overflow handling at mobile widths.
close_reason: Implemented: mobile review detail tabs wrap within the viewport

title: Visual: new submission details open to not-found page
description: Flow: publish wizard -> compliance approval dashboard at 1280x800. A valid upload fixture was submitted from /publish, then /review showed the new pending item visual-flow-check with an Open details action. Expected: clicking Open details should show the submission detail page with diff/scan/decision evidence for the queued submission. Actual: the link navigates to /review/sub-2000 and renders 'Submission not found', making the review flow unusable for submissions created in the same dev session. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/17-publish-review-step-valid.png, .playwright-mcp/18-publish-submitted.png, .playwright-mcp/19-review-after-submit.png, .playwright-mcp/20-review-new-submission-detail-not-found.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard reads the pending list from the API, but ReviewDetailPage only resolves IDs from the hard-coded mockReviewQueue/mockReviewDetails maps instead of fetching submission detail/evidence from the API or sharing submitted queue state.
close_reason: Implemented: review detail fetches pending API submissions and renders evidence for newly submitted queue items

title: Visual: Publish submission POST returns 405
description: Flow: publish/upload at 1280x800. Expected Submit for review to create a local mock submission per specs/api.md POST /api/v1/submissions. Actual: UI remains on review step with inline error 'Submission could not be created. Try again after the API is available.' Network shows POST http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions => 405 Method Not Allowed while health is OK. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/publish-review-submit-before.png and .playwright-mcp/publish-submit-after.png. Network artifact: .playwright-mcp/network-submit.log. Root cause hypothesis: web calls the spec endpoint, but deploy/docker/dev-api.mjs only exposes review/registry shim routes or does not implement POST /submissions, so the local visual flow cannot complete.
close_reason: Implemented: dev API accepts mock submission POSTs and e2e covers creation

title: Visual: tag chips open skill detail instead of filtering
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800. Expected: tag filter chips should be interactive filters per specs/web-ui.md and the visual review prompt; activating the security chip should apply a tag filter while staying in browse context. Actual: the tag is rendered as a non-button span inside the clickable card; clicking it opens the skill detail modal instead of filtering. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-browse-after-chip-click.png. Root cause hypothesis: card-level click handler is wrapping tag chips and no separate filter-chip control/event handling exists.
close_reason: Duplicate/confirmation of existing visual bead asr-9rbl; tag chips are not filter controls and card click opens detail instead.

title: Visual: SKILL.md preview is not full markdown content
description:
Flow: Skill detail at /skills/asr/security-review, 1280x800.
Expected: specs/web-ui.md requires the SKILL.md preview to render the skill document via react-markdown + remark-gfm, including headings, fenced code blocks, tables, lists, and styled links.
Actual: the preview renders only the metadata/description-derived content available from the detail response; the seeded detail page does not expose a full SKILL.md document with tables or fenced code blocks, so the required markdown surface cannot be verified and users do not see the canonical skill instructions.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-route.png, .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-modal.png.
Root cause hypothesis: SkillDetail uses detail.manifestLatest.description/detail.description as markdownPreview instead of fetching or receiving the SKILL.md body from the registry endpoint/version content.
close_reason: Implemented: skill detail now receives and renders the full SKILL.md body with GFM preview coverage

title: Visual: Review navigation is visible to Viewer role
description:
Flow: Landing / browse in mock Viewer mode at 1280x800 and 390x844.
Expected: specs/web-ui.md says role-specific navigation such as Review is visible only with the corresponding Compliance/Admin role; viewer screenshots should not expose approval-dashboard entry points.
Actual: the primary nav shows Review while the mock banner says "Mock auth: Viewer". Navigating to /review switches the banner to Compliance and exposes the approval dashboard, so the mock UI can be confused with an authorized compliance session.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/landing-before.png, .playwright-mcp/landing-mobile.png, .playwright-mcp/review-queue.png.
Root cause hypothesis: PrimaryNav is static and route components hard-code mock roles instead of deriving navigation/access from a shared session provider.
close_reason: Implemented: shared mock session drives navigation and review route access so Viewer cannot see or enter approval UI

title: Visual: review detail link opens not-found state
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800 viewport. After approving secure-code-review, the remaining release-notes row still renders an Open details action. Clicking it navigates to /review/sub-1039, but the UI shows a generic 'Submission not found' skill lookup error with Viewer mock auth instead of the compliance review detail. Expected: every visible pending submission row either opens a review detail page with evidence/actions or the dashboard should not expose an Open details action. Actual: dashboard action leads to a dead-end error page, making detail review unusable for that submission. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/2026-05-25-asr-visual/10-review-detail-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: mockReviewQueue includes sub-1039 but mockReviewDetails lacks matching detail data, and the route/error state falls through to the viewer skill lookup shell.
close_reason: Implemented: added review evidence for release-notes so /review/sub-1039 opens the compliance detail page instead of not found.

title: Visual: Publish flow is a single form instead of submission wizard
description:
Flow: Publish/submission at /publish, 1280x800.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/publish-before.png, .playwright-mcp/publish-empty-submit-after.png, .playwright-mcp/publish-oversize-after.png, .playwright-mcp/publish-bad-skillmd-after.png.
Expected: specs/web-ui.md requires a four-step Submission Wizard (Upload, Manifest, Questionnaire, Review & Submit) with progress indicator, drag/drop zip, client-side archive validation, parsed manifest review/editing, questionnaire, and review summary. The visual prompt asks for validation states including empty title, oversize zip, and bad SKILL.md.
Actual: /publish renders a single card form with owner, file input, and SKILL.md textarea. There is no wizard progress, drag/drop area, parsed manifest step, questionnaire step, or review summary. The submit button is active while the form is invalid; validation appears only after submit, although oversize zip and bad SKILL.md errors do render inline.
Root cause hypothesis: PublishSkill is an early single-form implementation and has not adopted the specified multi-step submission UX/state machine.
close_reason: Implemented: replaced publish single form with four-step submission wizard

title: Visual: publish upload limit conflicts with 50MB spec
description: Flow: Publish form at /publish, viewport 1280x800. Expected: specs/web-ui.md acceptance test says the submission wizard rejects a zip > 50MB inline; files up to 50MB should not be visually described as oversize. Actual: the form helper copy says Zip archive, 25 MB maximum, and the client constant is 25 MB, so valid 26-50MB submissions would be rejected before upload. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260525T0112Z/publish-empty-before-1280x800.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx uses MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES = 25 * 1024 * 1024 and matching helper copy, which drifted from the web-ui acceptance spec.
close_reason: Implemented: align publish upload limit and helper copy with 50 MB spec

title: Visual: SKILL.md preview omits full markdown coverage
description: Flow: Skill detail at /skills/asr/security-review, viewport 1280x800. Expected: per specs/web-ui.md acceptance test, the SKILL.md preview should render the submitted SKILL.md content including headings, code blocks, tables, lists, and links via react-markdown + remark-gfm. Actual: the preview only renders the manifest description fixture (heading, paragraph, inline code); there is no table/list/link coverage available in the rendered detail, so the user cannot inspect the full SKILL.md preview required by the design. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260525T0112Z/skill-detail-route-1280x800.png. Root cause hypothesis: the web detail page derives markdownPreview from detail.manifestLatest.description/detail.description, and the seeded API detail also stores markdown-like content in manifestLatest.description instead of a dedicated SKILL.md body field.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open visual review beads; see asr-o6jr/asr-798y for SKILL.md GFM coverage and asr-c20q/asr-vqi1 for asr branding.

title: Visual: approved submission remains in pending queue
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard decision action
Viewport: 1280x800
Expected: After confirming approval, a submission should leave the Pending submissions list or move to a clearly separate processed/approved section. The pending list should contain only pending review rows and match the header count.
Actual: After approving secure-code-review, the dashboard count drops to 1 pending, but the approved secure-code-review row remains inside the Pending submissions list with an approved status pill and no approve/reject actions.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/2026-05-25-visual-10-review-approve-confirm-before.png and .playwright-mcp/2026-05-25-visual-11-review-approve-after.png
Root cause hypothesis: The dashboard filters counts by pending review but renders the full submissions array after a decision instead of filtering the displayed queue.
close_reason: Implemented: pending review dashboard now renders the same filtered pending-review collection used by the header counts.

title: Visual: Approval queue rows do not link to review detail evidence
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at /review and detail page /review/sub-1042, 1280x800.
Expected: Compliance reviewers can navigate from a pending queue row to the submission evidence/detail view to inspect diff, dependencies, permissions, scan, and audit before deciding.
Actual: /review renders pending rows with approve/reject actions, but row titles/cards are not links and there is no visible "Review" or "Open details" action. The detail route exists and renders evidence tabs when opened directly, but it is not discoverable from the queue.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/review-detail-diff-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/review-detail-scan-1280.png
Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard maps submissions to static article rows and only wires decision buttons; it never renders an anchor to /review/:id.
close_reason: Implemented: added approval queue detail links for each pending submission

title: Visual: Skill preview does not render full SKILL.md GFM content
description:
Flow: Skill detail via card modal and /skills/asr/security-review at 1280x800.
Expected: SKILL.md preview renders representative react-markdown + remark-gfm content including headings, fenced code blocks, tables, lists, and styled links. specs/web-ui.md acceptance test expects headings, code blocks, and tables.
Actual: Preview only shows the skill name, short description, Usage heading, and inline code. No table or fenced code block is available to verify GFM rendering. The route preview appears to use a generated/description field rather than full SKILL.md body.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/skill-modal-security-review-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-security-review-1280.png
Root cause hypothesis: SkillDetailPage derives markdownPreview from detail.manifestLatest.description/detail.description, and the API/seed response does not expose the full SKILL.md body needed by the preview.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open GFM preview coverage beads asr-o6jr/asr-798y; this run's screenshots remain in .playwright-mcp/skill-modal-security-review-1280.png and .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-security-review-1280.png.

title: Visual: Mock auth banner missing on browse and skill pages
description:
Flow: Landing / browse and skill detail at 1280x800, plus mobile 375x800.
Expected: Dev/mock mode shows a persistent yellow banner such as "Mock auth: <role>" on every SPA screen so screenshots cannot be confused with production, per specs/web-ui.md Auth UX.
Actual: / and /skills/asr/security-review show no mock auth banner. Publish and review do show the banner.
