Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-security-review-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/landing-mobile-375.png
Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry and SkillDetailPage headers omit the mock-auth-banner element used by PublishSkill and ReviewDashboard/ReviewDetailPage.
close_reason: Implemented: added persistent mock auth banner to browse and skill detail headers

title: Visual: skill detail page lacks required tabs and uses stale install command
description: Flow: Skill detail at /skills/asr/security-review, 1280x800. Expected: specs/web-ui.md requires SKILL.md preview, Versions, Permissions, and Audit tabs plus install snippet 'asr install owner/name'. Actual: detail page renders only a flat markdown section, no tab controls or versions/permissions/audit surfaces, and the snippet says 'asr add asr/security-review'. Screenshot path: .playwright-mcp/detail-direct-security.png. Root cause hypothesis: current detail implementation is an early simplified page/modal and has not been wired to the full route spec or current CLI command naming.
close_reason: Implemented: added routed skill detail tabs and updated install command copy to asr install

title: Visual: oversized archive validation is unclear in publish form
description:
Flow: Submission / publish form at http://localhost:5173/publish, viewport 1280x800.

Expected: A zip over the documented 25 MB maximum is rejected inline with a specific size-limit message, and the invalid file is not presented as an acceptable selected archive.

Actual: Uploading .playwright-mcp/fixtures/oversize.zip (26.00 MB) changes the archive control to show "oversize.zip - 26.00 MB" while retaining the generic "Upload a skill archive" error. This makes it look like the oversize file was accepted and does not explain the size-limit failure.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-oversize-after.png

Root cause hypothesis: the file selection label updates before size validation normalizes the error state; the validation branch likely reuses the missing-file message instead of a max-size message.
close_reason: Implemented: reject oversized publish archives at selection time with a clear size-limit message

title: Visual: browse page calls missing skills endpoint and renders empty registry
description:
Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800 on http://localhost:5173/.

Expected: The seeded registry skill list renders, search/filter flows operate, and users can click into skill details. specs/web-ui.md expects GET /skills-style browse data, result cards, tag filter chips, and at least one seeded skill in mock dev.

Actual: The page renders 0 skills and shows the generic empty state. Browser console reports GET http://localhost:3001/api/v1/skills? -> 404. Direct API check shows http://localhost:3001/api/skills returns the seeded skills, so the web package appears to be calling the wrong registry endpoint/response contract. Search for "security" repeats the 404 and remains empty.

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-before.png, .playwright-mcp/08-landing-search-security.png.

Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx fetches ${API_URL}/api/v1/skills and expects {items}, while the local API exposes /api/skills with {skills}. This blocks browse cards, filter verification, and real skill-detail navigation.
close_reason: Implemented: mock dev registry now serves /api/v1/skills with SkillSummary items and Docker E2E coverage

title: Visual: Unknown skill routes silently render browse instead of an error state
description: Flow: Empty + error states at 1280x800. Expected opening /skills/does-not-exist to render a graceful 404/inline error with retry or navigation, not a blank page or misleading data. Actual: the route is treated as BrowseRegistry and renders the normal browse experience; with fixture data installed for detail testing, /skills/does-not-exist even displayed the skill list. Users get no indication that the requested skill route is invalid. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-route-skills-does-not-exist.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx only checks window.location.pathname === '/review' and sends every other path to BrowseRegistry; no router or 404 boundary exists.
close_reason: Implemented: unknown skill routes now render an explicit not-found state with retry and browse actions

title: Visual: Publish upload flow is not exposed in the web UI
description: Flow: Submission / publish at 1280x800. Expected an exposed publish or upload flow with form validation for required fields, oversized zip, and bad SKILL.md. Actual: browse has no publish/upload action, no navigation item, and visiting /publish silently renders the browse page. Validation states cannot be exercised because the submission form is absent. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-publish-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx only switches between /review and BrowseRegistry; no submission route/component is wired.
close_reason: Implemented: exposed publish route with upload form and client-side validation

title: Visual: Browse page calls missing registry endpoint and shows empty connected state
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800. Expected the skill list to render from the local registry API per specs/registry-api.md GET /api/v1/skills, with an honest loading/error/empty state. Actual: the web UI requests http://localhost:3001/api/v1/skills? and receives 404, then renders 0 skills plus a green 'Connected to Registry' indicator and the generic 'No skills found' message. The local API does have data at the legacy /api/skills endpoint, so users cannot browse or click real skills. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse.png and .playwright-mcp/landing-search-security.png. Root cause hypothesis: @asr/web was updated to the v1 registry contract but the local API/response mapping still exposes the legacy /api/skills shape, and the UI collapses fetch failures into the same empty state.
close_reason: Implemented: browse registry fetch failures now render an explicit unavailable state with retry instead of a connected empty registry.

title: Visual: browse API calls legacy /api/skills endpoint
description:
Flow: Landing / browse
Viewport: 1280x800
Expected: Web UI should follow specs/registry-api.md and call GET /api/v1/skills for search/list data.
Actual: Browser network capture shows GET http://localhost:3001/api/skills? returning 200. The UI is coupled to a legacy endpoint and may fail when the API contract is enforced.
Screenshot/network artefact: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-current/network-api-requests.log
Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry fetchSkills in packages/web/src/App.tsx builds `${API_URL}/api/skills` instead of `${API_URL}/api/v1/skills` and expects data.skills rather than the documented data.items shape.
close_reason: Implemented: browse UI now calls /api/v1/skills and maps the documented items response shape

title: Visual: unknown skill URL renders browse instead of error state
description:
Flow: Empty + error states
Viewport: 1280x800
Expected: Opening /skills/does-not-exist should route to skill detail lookup and render a graceful 404/inline error with a retry or navigation affordance.
Actual: The SPA silently renders the normal browse page with the URL still at /skills/does-not-exist, so users get no indication that the skill does not exist.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-current/08-error-route-skills-does-not-exist-1280x800.png
Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only branches on window.location.pathname === "/review" and sends every other path to BrowseRegistry; no router/error boundary handles skill detail or unknown routes.
close_reason: Implemented skill-specific route handling and 404 state for missing skill URLs

title: Visual: Approved item remains in pending submissions list
description: Flow: Approval dashboard. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: after approving a pending submission, the pending queue should remove the item or move it to a clearly separate completed/approved state, and list copy/counts should remain consistent. Actual: after approving secure-code-review, the row remains inside the 'Pending submissions' panel with an approved status pill; summary says 1 pending while panel copy still says 2 submissions need compliance review. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/visual-20260524T2139-review-before.png, .playwright-mcp/visual-20260524T2139-review-approve-modal.png, .playwright-mcp/visual-20260524T2139-review-after-approve.png. Root cause hypothesis: local state updates the submission status in-place but the pending list and panel count are not derived from reviewable submissions or refreshed from GET /api/v1/submissions?status=pending.
close_reason: Implemented: derive the review panel from pending-review submissions so approved rows leave the pending queue and counts stay consistent

title: Visual: Missing skill route falls back to browse instead of 404 state
description: Flow: Empty + error states. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: opening /skills/does-not-exist should show a graceful 404 or inline error state with retry/back navigation, never unrelated registry results. Actual: /skills/does-not-exist renders the normal browse page with real skills and no error message, making a missing skill look like a successful registry browse. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-20260524T2139-error-skills-does-not-exist.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx routes every non-/review path to BrowseRegistry and does not parse /skills/:id or render route-level errors.
close_reason: Implemented: malformed skill routes now render a specific not-found state instead of browse results

title: Visual: Unknown skill route shows browse instead of error state
description: Flow: Empty + error states. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: opening /skills/does-not-exist shows a graceful 404/inline error card with retry or navigation, never an unrelated page. Actual: route renders the normal browse page and skill cards, giving no indication the requested skill was missing. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-404-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: missing router/error boundary; unrecognised routes are handled by BrowseRegistry instead of a route-level error screen.
close_reason: Duplicate visual-review findings already tracked: asr-f9me covers missing browse mock-auth banner, asr-4ztf covers missing submit/publish form route, asr-13n covers missing skill 404/error state, and asr-ujnk covers mobile horizontal overflow.

title: Visual: Approval dashboard records decisions without confirmation
description: Flow: Approval dashboard decision action. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: approve/reject flow opens a confirmation modal repeating version and risk before submitting; reject requires a comment/reason in the decision panel. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review immediately changes the status to Approved with no modal or comment step. Screenshots: before .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard.png, after .playwright-mcp/approval-after-approve.png. Root cause hypothesis: queue rows call POST /approve or mutate mock state directly from row buttons rather than routing through the approval detail decision panel.
close_reason: Implemented: approval decisions now require a confirmation modal, and rejection requires a reviewer reason

title: Visual: Review detail route falls back to browse page
description: Flow: Approval dashboard detail. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: /review/:id renders approval detail with diff, dependencies, permissions, scan, audit tabs, and sticky decision panel. Actual: opening /review/sub-1042 renders the browse page with registry cards; no review detail content appears. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/review-detail-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only checks pathname === /review and lacks routing for /review/:id.
close_reason: Implemented: added routed review detail page for /review/:id with evidence tabs and sticky decision panel

title: Visual: Submit route falls back to browse instead of upload wizard
description: Flow: Submission. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: /submit renders the required four-step upload wizard with drag/drop zip, inline validation for missing title/oversize zip/bad SKILL.md, manifest step, questionnaire, and review submit. Actual: opening http://localhost:5173/submit renders the browse page, so no submission controls or validation states are available. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx routes only /review specially and sends every other pathname through BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Duplicate visual-review findings already tracked: asr-f9me covers missing browse mock-auth banner, asr-4ztf covers missing submit/publish form route, asr-13n covers missing skill 404/error state, and asr-ujnk covers mobile horizontal overflow.

title: Visual: Mock auth banner missing on browse surface
description: Flow: Auth mock mode on Landing / browse. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: persistent yellow banner labelled Mock auth: <role> in dev/mock mode so screenshots cannot be confused with production. Actual: browse page shows no mock auth banner; only /review shows Mock auth: Compliance. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse.png. Root cause hypothesis: mock auth banner is implemented only in ReviewDashboard rather than shared app shell.
close_reason: Duplicate visual-review findings already tracked: asr-f9me covers missing browse mock-auth banner, asr-4ztf covers missing submit/publish form route, asr-13n covers missing skill 404/error state, and asr-ujnk covers mobile horizontal overflow.

title: Visual: approval dashboard actions fail against dev API
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800. Expected: compliance queue loads pending submissions from the dev API, status pills render without an API error, and Approve/Reject record decisions or show endpoint-specific validation. Actual: /review calls GET /api/v1/submissions?status=pending and POST /api/v1/submissions/sub-1042/approve; the dev API returns 404 for the submissions endpoints. The UI shows mock queue rows plus an API error banner, and clicking Approve leaves the row pending with 'Decision could not be recorded.' Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard-before.png and .playwright-mcp/review-approve-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: the web approval dashboard is wired to the specified /api/v1 submissions contract before the dev API implements those routes, and it falls back to mock rows while still reporting API failure.
close_reason: Duplicate visual review beads already exist: asr-f9me covers browse mock-auth banner, asr-4ztf covers missing publish/submission form, asr-13n covers missing skill 404, and asr-5bx2/asr-3fn8 cover approval API/action failures.

title: Visual: unknown skill route renders browse instead of graceful 404
description: Flow: Empty + error states at 1280x800. Expected: opening /skills/does-not-exist triggers a 404-style inline error with recovery action/retry and does not show unrelated registry data. Actual: /skills/does-not-exist renders the normal browse page and shows the two available skills, so users receive wrong data instead of a not-found state. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-route-does-not-exist.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx treats every path except /review as BrowseRegistry and does not route skill details or unknown paths to an error component.
close_reason: Duplicate visual review beads already exist: asr-f9me covers browse mock-auth banner, asr-4ztf covers missing publish/submission form, asr-13n covers missing skill 404, and asr-5bx2/asr-3fn8 cover approval API/action failures.

title: Visual: publish upload flow is not reachable
description: Flow: Submission at 1280x800. Expected: a publish/upload flow is exposed so users can upload a skill zip and see validation for empty title, oversized zip, and bad SKILL.md. Actual: there is no visible publish/upload entry point on /, and direct navigation to /submit renders the generic browse page with skills instead of a submission form or not-found state. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only routes /review specially and otherwise renders BrowseRegistry; no submission component or route is wired.
close_reason: Duplicate visual review beads already exist: asr-f9me covers browse mock-auth banner, asr-4ztf covers missing publish/submission form, asr-13n covers missing skill 404, and asr-5bx2/asr-3fn8 cover approval API/action failures.

title: Visual: approval dashboard shows API errors for seeded queue
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: compliance queue loads pending submissions from the dev API, status pills render without an error banner, and approve/reject actions record decisions or show a meaningful domain error. Actual: page renders mock rows but also shows 'Unable to load pending submissions from the API.' because GET http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions?status=pending returns 404; clicking Approve posts to /api/v1/submissions/sub-1042/approve and receives 405, leaving an inline action failure. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/04-approval-dashboard.png and .playwright-mcp/05-approval-action-error.png. Root cause hypothesis: web calls the specs/api.md /api/v1 submission routes, but the running dev API/docker stack does not expose matching endpoints or seeded submissions for this UI.
close_reason: Implemented: dev API exposes seeded approval queue and decision endpoints for the review dashboard

title: Visual: publish route does not expose submission form
description: Flow: Submission at /publish and discoverability from /. Expected: a publish/upload flow is exposed with form validation for required title, oversize zip, and invalid SKILL.md. Actual: browse has no publish/upload link or button, and direct navigation to /publish renders the browse page instead of a submission form or route-level error. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/09-publish-route-missing.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only routes /review specially; all other paths render BrowseRegistry, and there is no submission form component wired into the SPA.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open bead asr-4ztf (publish route has no submission form).

title: Visual: approval decision action fails with 405
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800 viewport.

Expected: Clicking Approve on a pending submission records the decision through the API, updates the row to an approved state, and does not leave the user with an API failure.

Actual: The dashboard renders pending rows, but clicking Approve for secure-code-review triggers POST http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions/sub-1042/approve and receives 405 Method Not Allowed. The row remains pending and an inline error appears: Decision could not be recorded. Try again after the API is available.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-20260524-review-approve-after.png
Console: .playwright-mcp/visual-20260524-console-errors.log

Root cause hypothesis: The web approval dashboard is wired to /api/v1/submissions/:id/approve, but the current local API/router does not expose that method for the mocked submission IDs, so the primary compliance action is non-functional in dev.
close_reason: Implemented: mounted submission workflow routes at /api/v1/submissions and added a regression test for web approval decisions

title: Visual: approval dashboard shows mock rows while API actions fail
description: Flow: approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions list and approve/reject actions should reflect the active mock/dev data source or a working API contract; if the API cannot load, actions should not appear actionable against stale mock data. Actual: the dashboard renders three mock submissions while also showing 'Unable to load pending submissions from the API.' because GET /api/v1/submissions?status=pending returns 404. Clicking Approve then calls POST /api/v1/submissions/sub-1042/approve and displays 'Decision could not be recorded.' Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/review-before.png and .playwright-mcp/review-approve-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: @asr/web uses mockReviewQueue as initial state but still calls /api/v1/submissions when VITE_API_URL is set; the local API does not expose the v1 submission endpoints used by the review UI.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open bead asr-o2do, which covers the same /review API load and approve/reject failure.

title: Visual: approval dashboard calls missing submission endpoints
description: Flow: approval dashboard at /review, viewport 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions load from the API and approve/reject actions record decisions per specs/api.md. Actual: UI renders mock rows but shows 'Unable to load pending submissions from the API'; console shows GET http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions?status=pending returns 404, and clicking Approve returns 405 for POST /api/v1/submissions/sub-1042/approve. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/04-approval-dashboard.png and .playwright-mcp/05-approval-action-error.png. Root cause hypothesis: web uses /api/v1 submission endpoints from the spec but the local API container does not implement or route them, leaving compliance actions unusable.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open bead asr-o2do.

title: Visual: Approval dashboard cannot load or record decisions against dev API
description: Flow: approval dashboard at /review, viewport 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions load from the dev API and Approve/Reject actions record decisions or show actionable retry states. Actual: UI calls http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions?status=pending and receives 404, then Approve calls /api/v1/submissions/sub-1042/approve and receives 405; visible queue error remains and decision action cannot complete. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-before.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-after-approve.png. Root cause hypothesis: web package is using /api/v1 submission endpoints that are absent or method-mismatched in the current dev API contract/implementation.
close_reason: Duplicate visual review finding; canonical open beads already exist: asr-o2do, asr-sbr4/asr-7u0, asr-cfrl/asr-n61.

title: Visual: Unknown skill route renders browse instead of graceful 404
description: Flow: empty/error state at /skills/does-not-exist, viewport 1280x800. Expected: a route-level 4xx error card with retry/back navigation per specs/web-ui.md, never wrong content. Actual: the URL remains /skills/does-not-exist but the SPA renders the normal browse page with live skills, so users see unrelated registry data rather than a not-found state. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-skills-does-not-exist.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only switches /review vs BrowseRegistry and has no router/error route handling for /skills/:owner/:name failures.
close_reason: Duplicate visual review finding; canonical open beads already exist: asr-o2do, asr-sbr4/asr-7u0, asr-cfrl/asr-n61.

title: Visual: approval dashboard cannot load or act on submissions
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions list loads from the API contract in specs/api.md and Approve/Reject actions record decisions. Actual: GET http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions?status=pending returns 404, the UI shows an error while still rendering stale mock rows, and POST /api/v1/submissions/sub-1042/approve returns 405 so approve cannot complete. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/07-review-dashboard-initial.png and .playwright-mcp/08-review-approve-error.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx is wired to /api/v1/submissions but deploy/docker/dev-api.mjs only implements /api/skills and health endpoints; mutating submission endpoints are absent in the dev stack.
close_reason: Verified: duplicate of asr-5bx2; dev API approval queue and decision endpoints are implemented and covered by e2e smoke

title: Visual: review dashboard decisions fail against local API
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions load from the local API and Approve/Reject actions render actionable states that record a decision. Actual: /review shows mock rows and a red 'Unable to load pending submissions from the API.' banner because GET http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions?status=pending returns 404; clicking Approve leaves the row pending and shows 'Decision could not be recorded.' Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/review-before.png and .playwright-mcp/review-approve-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: web review UI is wired to spec endpoints that the current dev API container does not implement, but still mixes mock queue data with an API failure state.
close_reason: Duplicate found after broader search during visual review: covered by existing open beads for mock auth banner, filter chips, submission flow, review API/actions, unknown-route error state, and mobile overflow.

title: Visual: submission publish flow is not exposed
description: Flow: Submission / publish at 1280x800. Expected: the web UI exposes a publish/upload flow with form validation states for missing title, oversize zip, and invalid SKILL.md. Actual: there is no publish/upload entry point in the header or browse UI, and direct navigation to /publish renders the browse page with no upload form or route error. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-route-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only routes /review specially and otherwise renders BrowseRegistry; submission UI has not been wired into the SPA.
close_reason: Duplicate found after broader search during visual review: covered by existing open beads for mock auth banner, filter chips, submission flow, review API/actions, unknown-route error state, and mobile overflow.

title: Visual: publish upload flow is not exposed in web UI
description:
Flow: Submission / publish/upload flow on the React SPA.
Expected: A visible publish/upload entry point exists, with form validation states for empty title, oversize zip, and bad SKILL.md as requested by the visual review flow.
Actual: No publish/upload/submission entry point is visible on / or /review, and packages/web/src/App.tsx only routes /review specially; all other paths render BrowseRegistry. The submission validation states cannot be exercised through the UI.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/visual-landing-before-1280x800.png, .playwright-mcp/visual-approval-dashboard-before-1280x800.png
Root cause hypothesis: The current web package implements browse/detail and a mock review dashboard, but not the submitter workflow surface described in specs/api.md and specs/cli-integration.md.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open submission flow beads asr-2jao/asr-4ztf.

title: Visual: approval dashboard calls wrong submissions API
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800 and 390x844.
Expected: Pending submissions load from the local API and approve/reject actions record decisions successfully. specs/api.md lists unversioned /submissions endpoints.
Actual: UI renders an inline "Unable to load pending submissions from the API." banner and clicking Approve adds "Decision could not be recorded". Console/network errors occur because the UI calls /api/v1/submissions?status=pending and /api/v1/submissions/:id/approve against an API that exposes the contract differently.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/visual-approval-dashboard-before-1280x800.png, .playwright-mcp/visual-approval-dashboard-after-approve-1280x800.png, .playwright-mcp/visual-approval-dashboard-mobile-before-390x844.png
Console artefact: .playwright-mcp/visual-approval-console-errors.log
Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard uses hard-coded /api/v1 submission paths that no longer match specs/api.md or the local API implementation.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing approval API/action failure coverage in asr-ydjt and asr-9o50.

title: Visual: approval dashboard shows API error and decisions fail
description:
Flow: approval dashboard at /review on 1280x800.

Expected: pending submissions list should load from the configured dev API or cleanly use mock mode without a visible API failure; approve/reject actions should record a decision or clearly operate in mock mode.

Actual: the dashboard renders mock rows but also shows 'Unable to load pending submissions from the API.' The browser console reports 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/v1/submissions?status=pending. Clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the row unchanged and displays 'Decision could not be recorded. Try again after the API is available.'

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-1280x800.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-after-approve-1280x800.png

Root cause hypothesis: @asr/web calls /api/v1/submissions and /api/v1/submissions/:id/decision, but the local API does not expose those routes; specs/api.md documents POST /submissions/:id/approve and POST /submissions/:id/reject rather than a generic decision endpoint.
close_reason: Duplicate: covered by asr-ydjt for approval queue API failure/mock rows and asr-9o50 for approve/reject missing endpoint.

title: Visual: Review decision actions call a missing endpoint
description: Flow: Approval dashboard approve/reject action at /review. Expected: Approve and Reject actions render and successfully call the API endpoints from specs/api.md: POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/approve and POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/reject. Actual: clicking Approve leaves the row pending and shows 'Decision could not be recorded. Try again after the API is available.' Console/network shows the UI posting a missing decision endpoint. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-after-approve.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard uses POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/decision with { decision }, which does not match the API contract.
close_reason: Implemented: approval dashboard now posts approve/reject actions to the spec endpoints

title: Visual: Review queue shows mock rows after API load failure
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800 and 390x844. Expected: pending submissions reflect the API contract or a clear empty/error state; if the API 404s, the UI should not present mock submissions as actionable real data. Actual: /api/v1/submissions?status=pending returns 404, the page shows 'Unable to load pending submissions from the API.' while still rendering 3 mock rows and enabled Approve/Reject buttons. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-before.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-mobile.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard initializes mockReviewQueue and keeps it after fetchQueue fails instead of clearing or marking it as demo data.
close_reason: Duplicate found after broader visual-label search: asr-av4s covers mock auth on browse; asr-gdj covers non-interactive/missing filter chips; asr-2jao/asr-4ztf cover missing publish/upload flow; asr-ydjt covers mock queue after submissions API failure; asr-a45q covers unknown skill route rendering browse.

title: Visual: Publish/upload flow is not exposed
description: Flow: Submission/publish/upload discovery from the SPA. Expected: a visible publish/upload flow with validation states for required title, oversize zip, and bad SKILL.md. Actual: the SPA exposes only browse and /review; there is no publish/upload button, route, or form to test submission validation. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx has route selection only for /review vs BrowseRegistry and no Submission component.
close_reason: Duplicate found after broader visual-label search: asr-av4s covers mock auth on browse; asr-gdj covers non-interactive/missing filter chips; asr-2jao/asr-4ztf cover missing publish/upload flow; asr-ydjt covers mock queue after submissions API failure; asr-a45q covers unknown skill route rendering browse.

title: Visual: Approval decisions render as inert buttons
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800.
Expected: Approve/Reject actions should open a decision flow, call the approval API, show disabled/loading state, or otherwise visibly acknowledge the action.
Actual: Clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the page unchanged; no modal, toast, loading, status update, or error appears. This makes the core compliance action appear broken.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/review-before.png and .playwright-mcp/review-approve-after.png
Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard buttons are static JSX with no onClick handlers or mutation state.
close_reason: Duplicate found after broader visual-label search; covered by existing open beads asr-2jao/asr-a45q/asr-2soe/asr-ydjt.

title: Visual: Submission upload flow is not exposed
description:
Flow: Submission at 1280x800.
Expected: Publish/upload flow is exposed so a submitter can open the form and see validation for empty title, oversize zip, and invalid SKILL.md.
Actual: The browse header has no Publish/Upload navigation; direct /submit falls through to the browse page with no not-found message or upload form. The submission validation states cannot be exercised.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-missing-after.png
Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx only routes /review specially and otherwise renders BrowseRegistry; no submission component or navigation is wired.
close_reason: Duplicate found after broader visual-label search; covered by existing open beads asr-2jao/asr-a45q/asr-2soe/asr-ydjt.

title: Visual: approval decision buttons do not update state
description: Flow: approval dashboard at /review, viewport 1280x800. Expected: Approve/Reject actions should produce visible feedback, update the pending list/status, or surface an API/error state while disabled during submission. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the row and summary unchanged; the only visual change is focus on the same button. Screenshot before: .playwright-mcp/visual-20260524-review-before.png. Screenshot after: .playwright-mcp/visual-20260524-review-approve-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard renders inert buttons with no onClick handlers, while the queue fetch also calls /api/submissions?status=pending and receives 404 instead of the versioned /api/v1/submissions endpoint.
close_reason: Implemented: approval dashboard decisions now update visible row state, disable completed/non-reviewable actions, and use versioned submissions API with error feedback

title: Visual: awaiting-confirmation submission exposes approval actions
description: Flow: Approval dashboard. Viewports: 1280x800 and 390x800. Expected: the pending submissions list should show actionable approve/reject controls only for submissions actually in pending review; awaiting-confirmation items should not be approvable yet. Actual: test-plan-writer is labelled Awaiting Confirmation but still renders Approve and Reject buttons. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-mobile.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard maps every queue item to the same decision-actions block without gating by submission.status.
close_reason: Implemented: hide approval actions for non-reviewable approval queue submissions

title: Visual: approval actions render but do nothing
description: Flow: Approval dashboard. Viewport: 1280x800. Tested http://localhost:5173/review and clicked the first Approve button. Expected: approve/reject controls should open a decision state, call the approval API, disable while pending, or otherwise provide visible feedback. Actual: clicking Approve leaves the row and page unchanged with no modal, toast, loading state, navigation, or error. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-approve-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx renders buttons without onClick handlers, so core compliance actions are visually exposed but non-functional.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-2soe

title: Visual: unknown skill URL renders browse instead of 404 state
description:
Flow: Empty/error states at /skills/does-not-exist, 1280x800.

Expected: Opening an unknown skill URL should produce a graceful 404/error state with useful copy and retry/back navigation, not a blank screen or unrelated content.

Actual: /skills/does-not-exist renders the normal browse page with two skills and no indication that the requested skill was not found.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-route-skills-does-not-exist-1280x800.png

Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx has no router or not-found branch; every path except /review falls through to BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Verified: unknown /skills/* routes render the skill not-found state with browse and retry actions instead of the browse grid

title: Visual: approval decision buttons are no-ops
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard decision actions at /review, 1280x800.

Expected: Approve and Reject controls should either open the required decision flow, call the approval/rejection API, or show clear disabled/unavailable state. Actionable-looking controls should not silently do nothing.

Actual: Clicking Approve on secure-code-review produces no visible state change, no confirmation, no disabled/loading state, and no API call beyond the existing failed queue fetch. Reject appears wired the same way.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-after-approve-click-1280x800.png

Root cause hypothesis: The buttons in ReviewDashboard are presentational only and have no onClick handlers or route to a submission detail/decision panel.
close_reason: Already implemented: approval dashboard decision buttons open a confirmation flow, show pending/error states, and record approval or rejection through the API when configured.

title: Visual: approval queue silently falls back after submissions API 404
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800.

Expected: If the pending submissions API fails or returns 404/500, the UI should show an inline error with retry and should not present stale/mock data as if it came from the registry. specs/api.md defines GET /submissions as the queue contract.

Actual: The browser console reports 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, but the page still shows three mock submissions and '3 submissions need compliance review' with no error state.

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-1280x800.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-after-approve-click-1280x800.png

Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard fetches /api/submissions?status=pending, ignores non-ok responses, and keeps the mockReviewQueue default state instead of rendering an error/empty state.
close_reason: Implemented: approval queue API failures now render an error state with retry and clear stale mock submissions

title: Visual: publish route has no submission form
description:
Flow: Submission at /publish, 1280x800.

Expected: A publish/upload flow should be exposed for submitters, with validation states for empty title, oversized zip, and invalid SKILL.md per the visual review workflow and specs/api.md POST /submissions contract.

Actual: Navigating to /publish renders the browse page with no upload form, no validation messages, and no submitter workflow entry point visible in the header.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-route-publish-1280x800.png

Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only routes /review specially; all other paths render BrowseRegistry, and there is no submission component or nav affordance.
close_reason: Verified implemented: /publish and /submit render the PublishSkill wizard with archive, SKILL.md, manifest, questionnaire, and review-submit validation states. Required pnpm install/build/test pass; optional recursive tsc is blocked by existing packages/submission test typing errors.

title: Visual: approval actions have no visible result
description: Flow: approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: clicking Approve or Reject should open a decision confirmation/form, call the approval API, disable while pending, or show success/error feedback. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review produces no visible state change, no toast/error, and leaves the row unchanged. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-2009/review-approve-after-1280x800.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx renders approve/reject buttons without onClick handlers or wired POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/approve|reject integration.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open visual approval-actions bead asr-bg0t.

title: Visual: approval queue actions are inert and shown for non-reviewable submissions
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, viewport 1280x800. Expected: approve/reject controls should be wired to the review decision flow and should only render for submissions that are actually in pending review; awaiting-confirmation submissions should not expose compliance decisions. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review produced no modal, status change, validation, loading state, or API call visible in the UI; the awaiting confirmation row also renders Approve and Reject buttons. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-1280x800.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-approve-click-no-feedback-1280x800.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard renders static button elements without handlers and does not gate actions by submission.status.
close_reason: Implemented: approval queue actions are wired with confirmation and pending-review gating

title: Visual: unknown skill route renders browse instead of graceful 404
description: Flow: empty/error states. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: opening /skills/does-not-exist should show a graceful not-found or API error message with retry/back navigation, never stale content. Actual: the route renders the normal browse page and skill list with no indication that the requested skill does not exist. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-route-skills-does-not-exist.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx treats every non-/review path as BrowseRegistry and has no route-aware 404/detail error state.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open unknown skill route/error-state beads asr-d2px/asr-wk03.

title: Visual: submission flow route is not exposed
description: Flow: publish/upload submission. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: a publish/upload flow with form validation for empty title, oversize zip, and invalid SKILL.md is exposed from the SPA. Actual: navigating to /submit renders the generic browse page with no upload form, no required-field states, and no validation controls. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only switches between /review and BrowseRegistry; submission routes/components are absent.
close_reason: Verified: /submit renders the publish upload wizard and exposes required-field, invalid SKILL.md, and oversize archive validation.

title: Visual: approval dashboard silently falls back after submissions API 404
description: Flow: approval dashboard. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: the compliance queue should call the documented submissions API and show an inline error/retry state if the API fails. Actual: /review requests http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, receives 404, logs console errors, and still shows mock queue data as if live. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard uses /api/submissions instead of the documented /api/v1/submissions contract and suppresses failures by retaining mockReviewQueue.
close_reason: Implemented: approval dashboard now uses /api/v1/submissions and shows an inline retryable error instead of mock fallback when the API fails

title: Visual: approval decision buttons have no feedback or action
description: Flow: approval dashboard approve/reject actions. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: Approve should submit or open a confirmation state; Reject should require a reason and submit, with visible loading/error/success feedback. Actual: clicking Approve and Reject on the first submission leaves the UI unchanged with no dialog, validation, status change, toast, or error. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard-after-approve-click.png and .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard-after-reject-click.png. Root cause hypothesis: buttons are rendered without handlers or decision workflow wiring.
close_reason: Implemented: approval dashboard decisions now show modal-local error feedback and success status after recording a decision

title: Visual: approval dashboard silently falls back to mock queue after API 404
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions are loaded from the API contract endpoint and failures render an inline error or empty state. Actual: the UI requests http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, receives 404, logs console errors, then still displays hardcoded mock submissions with no error indication. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/08-review-after-load.png; console: .playwright-mcp/console-2026-05-24T17-40-22-278Z.log. Root cause hypothesis: web uses /api/submissions while specs/api.md defines /api/v1/submissions, and the catch/404 path keeps mockReviewQueue visible.
close_reason: Verified: review dashboard uses /api/v1/submissions and renders an inline API error without mock queue rows on failure

title: Visual: unknown skill URL renders browse page instead of 404
description: Flow: Empty + error states by opening /skills/does-not-exist at 1280x800. Expected: a graceful not-found or API error state with retry/back affordance. Actual: the app silently renders the browse page at /skills/does-not-exist, so users get no indication that the skill route failed or is invalid. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/11-error-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only special-cases /review and sends every other pathname to BrowseRegistry; there is no skill-detail route or not-found state.
close_reason: Verified: current web routes render a skill not-found state for invalid /skills paths

title: Visual: approval decision buttons do nothing
description: Flow: Approval dashboard decision actions at /review, 1280x800. Expected: Approve and Reject actions invoke the approval workflow or show a confirmation/validation state. Actual: clicking Approve and Reject on secure-code-review produces no visible feedback, no status change, and no request/error state. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/09-review-approve-after-click.png and .playwright-mcp/10-review-reject-after-click.png. Root cause hypothesis: buttons are rendered without click handlers or mutation integration.
close_reason: Verified: /review decision buttons open confirmation, submit a mock approval, update the queue, and show a success status.

title: Visual: approval decision buttons have no visible outcome
description: Flow: Approval dashboard action state at 1280x800. Expected: Approve/Reject actions open a confirmation/reason flow, call the API, show disabled/loading state, or render a validation/error message. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the row and page unchanged with no feedback. Screenshot after click: .playwright-mcp/05-review-approve-click-no-response.png. Root cause hypothesis: decision buttons are static controls with no handlers wired to workflow endpoints.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing approval button no-feedback issues asr-5ni/asr-aqsb/asr-i3gc discovered during broader visual label audit.

title: Visual: review dashboard silently falls back after submissions API 404
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800 and 390x844. Expected: pending submissions list is driven by the API contract, or a graceful inline error/empty state appears if the API is unavailable. Actual: /review logs 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, then renders a hardcoded mock queue with no visible error or retry semantics beyond a static Refresh button. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/04-review-dashboard.png and .playwright-mcp/09-review-mobile.png. Root cause hypothesis: web calls /api/submissions while the dev API shim only exposes registry skill endpoints, and ReviewDashboard ignores non-ok responses.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing visual review beads discovered during broader label audit.

title: Visual: publish route falls back to browse instead of submission form
description: Flow: Submission at 1280x800. Expected: publish/upload flow is exposed and supports validation states for empty title, oversize zip, and bad SKILL.md. Actual: opening /publish renders the browse page with skill cards; there is no visible upload form or validation UI to exercise. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/08-publish-route-fallback.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only routes /review specially and all other paths render BrowseRegistry; submission UI is not wired into the SPA.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing visual review beads discovered during broader label audit.

title: Visual: unknown skill URLs render browse instead of an error state
description:
Flow: Empty + error states at /skills/does-not-exist, 1280x800.
Expected: Unknown skill/detail URLs should show a graceful 404-style inline error with retry/back navigation, not a normal registry page.
Actual: /skills/does-not-exist falls through to the browse page, fetches /api/skills, and gives no indication that the requested skill does not exist.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/06-unknown-skill-route.png.
Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx has no router or NotFound/detail route; it renders BrowseRegistry for every path except /review.
close_reason: Verified: unknown /skills/* URLs render the inline skill-not-found state with browse and retry actions, and do not fall through to registry browse.

title: Visual: approval decision buttons have no feedback or state change
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard decision actions at /review, 1280x800.
Expected: Approve/Reject actions should submit the decision or open a confirmation/reason UI, then visibly update status, disable while pending, or show an inline error on failure.
Actual: Clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the row unchanged with no loading state, toast, modal, API error, or success/failure feedback. Reject buttons appear to be the same inert UI.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/04-approval-dashboard-before.png and .playwright-mcp/05-approval-click-after.png.
Root cause hypothesis: The buttons are rendered without onClick handlers or mutation wiring.
close_reason: Verified: approval decision actions open confirmation UI, update status/counts, and show success feedback.

title: Visual: approval dashboard silently falls back to mock data after API 404
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800.
Expected: The dashboard should call the documented submissions endpoint and either render real pending submissions or show a visible inline error with retry when the API returns 4xx/5xx.
Actual: The browser console records 404s for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, but the UI still shows hard-coded pending submissions, making the data look real.
Screenshots/logs: .playwright-mcp/04-approval-dashboard-before.png, .playwright-mcp/console-2026-05-24T17-17-05-229Z.log.
Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard calls /api/submissions instead of the documented /api/v1/submissions endpoint and ignores non-ok responses, preserving mockReviewQueue.
close_reason: Verified: current dashboard uses /api/v1/submissions, clears mocked queue data on API errors, and renders inline retry error state.

title: Visual: publish upload flow is not reachable
description:
Flow: Submission / publish at 1280x800.
Expected: The web UI exposes a publish/upload flow with required-field validation, oversize zip handling, and bad SKILL.md validation states.
Actual: /publish falls through to the browse registry page with no publish form, upload control, validation hints, or submission workflow.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/07-publish-route.png.
Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only routes /review specially; all other paths render BrowseRegistry, and no publish/upload component is mounted.
close_reason: Implemented: publish route already renders the upload wizard with required-field, archive, and SKILL.md validation; verified in browser.

title: Visual: unknown skill routes do not show error state
description: Flow: Empty + error states at 1280x800. Expected: opening /skills/does-not-exist shows a graceful 404/resource-not-found message with retry or navigation, not a silent fallback. Actual: the app renders the normal browse page with no error, so users cannot tell the requested skill was missing. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/route-skills-does-not-exist-1280x800.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only distinguishes /review and otherwise renders BrowseRegistry for every path; skill-detail routing and error boundaries are absent.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-sbr4 (unknown skill route renders browse instead of 404)

title: Visual: approval dashboard calls missing submissions endpoint
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800. Expected: compliance queue fetches the submission API contract and shows real pending submissions or a graceful inline error with retry. Actual: /review renders mock submissions while the console records 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending; user sees no indication the live queue failed. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard-1280x800.png; console: .playwright-mcp/console.log. Root cause hypothesis: web calls /api/submissions but specs/api.md defines submission endpoints under /api/v1/submissions, and errors are swallowed unless mock data is present.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-9cn8 (approval dashboard missing submissions endpoint)

title: Visual: publish route has no submission form
description: Flow: Submission at 1280x800. Expected: publish/upload flow is exposed and supports required validation states for empty title, oversize zip, and bad SKILL.md. Actual: no Publish/Upload control is visible on /, and direct /publish silently renders the browse page with no submission form or validation UI. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/route-publish-1280x800.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only routes /review specially; all other paths render BrowseRegistry and no submission component exists.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-gl9p (publish upload flow not exposed)

title: Visual: approval actions have no user-visible result
description: Flow: Approval dashboard decision actions at 1280x800. Expected: Approve and Reject trigger a decision flow, loading/disabled state, confirmation, validation for reject reason, or an error if the action cannot complete. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the dashboard visually unchanged and shows no feedback. Screenshot before: .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard-1280x800.png; after: .playwright-mcp/review-approve-click-after-1280x800.png. Root cause hypothesis: buttons are presentational only and have no onClick/API wiring.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-i3gc (approval decision buttons have no visible effect)

title: Visual: browse page missing mock auth indicator
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800. Expected: development mock identity is clearly labelled on the main browse UI and login is hidden or marked Mock per AUTH_MODE=mock. Actual: / shows no mock identity banner or auth state; only /review shows 'Mock auth: Compliance'. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-1280x800.png. Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry lacks SessionProvider/MockAuthProvider surface and has no shared topbar with auth state.
close_reason: Implemented: browse header now exposes an explicit development mock auth status banner

title: Visual: approval decision buttons have no user-visible effect
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800 on http://localhost:5173/review. Expected: Approve and Reject actions submit the decision or at least open a confirmation/reason UI, show pending/disabled state, and surface success or error. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the row and page unchanged with no toast, modal, disabled state, or API activity visible to the reviewer. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/05-approval-after-approve-click.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx renders Approve/Reject buttons without onClick handlers or submission lifecycle wiring.
close_reason: Implemented: wired approval detail decisions to visible pending, success, and error states with API submission.

title: Visual: approval dashboard silently falls back after missing submissions endpoint
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800 on http://localhost:5173/review. Expected: pending submissions list is backed by the API contract in specs/api.md (/api/v1/submissions or a documented compatible endpoint), with visible loading/error state if the endpoint fails. Actual: the SPA requests http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, receives 404, logs console errors, and still shows canned mock rows with no inline error, which can mislead compliance reviewers. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/04-approval-dashboard.png and console log .playwright-mcp/console-2026-05-24T16-52-52-221Z.log. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx calls /api/submissions instead of the versioned submission API and keeps mockReviewQueue as fallback data in dev.
close_reason: Implemented: approval dashboard already uses /api/v1/submissions and renders an inline API error without mock fallback

title: Visual: publish upload flow is not exposed
description: Flow: Submission at 1280x800 on http://localhost:5173/. Expected: a publish/upload entry point with validation states for empty title, oversize zip, and bad SKILL.md per the visual review brief and specs/api.md POST /submissions contract. Actual: browse header exposes only logo and search; no Publish, Upload, or submission form is reachable from the SPA, so validation states cannot be exercised. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-browse.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx only implements BrowseRegistry and /review, with no submission route or control.
close_reason: Verified: publish navigation and /publish,/submit upload wizard are already implemented; install, build, and tests pass.

title: Visual: approval actions shown before submitter confirmation
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard (/review), 1280x800 and 390x844 viewports.

Expected: The compliance queue should only expose Approve/Reject actions for submissions that are ready for compliance review. A submission still in 'awaiting confirmation' should be visually separated from pending review, hidden from the actionable queue, or have disabled actions with explanatory copy.

Actual: The test-plan-writer row has status 'awaiting confirmation' but still renders active Approve and Reject buttons alongside pending-review rows, which makes it look approvable before the submitter has confirmed scan findings.

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-1280.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-mobile-390.png

Root cause hypothesis: The review dashboard renders the same decision action block for every row returned/fallback in the queue and does not gate actions by submission.status.
close_reason: Verified: approval dashboard filters the actionable queue to pending-review submissions only, so awaiting-confirmation submissions are not shown with Approve/Reject actions.

title: Visual: Approval queue silently falls back to mock data after API 404
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800 and 390x844.

Expected: The dashboard should call the documented submissions endpoint, show a loading state while fetching, and render an inline error/retry if the API returns 4xx/5xx. Pending rows should represent API data, not silent fallback data.

Actual: The page requests http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, which returns 404. Console shows repeated Failed to load resource errors, but the UI still displays hard-coded pending submissions with active-looking Approve/Reject buttons. Clicking Approve produced no visible state change or confirmation.

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/review-approve-click-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/review-mobile-390.png, .playwright-mcp/console-2026-05-24T16-10-55-827Z.log

Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard fetches /api/submissions?status=pending instead of the spec's /api/v1/submissions path and only updates from API if items.length > 0, leaving mockReviewQueue visible on failures.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open beads asr-xdo (approval dashboard endpoint 404) and asr-5ni (decision buttons have no visible effect)

title: Visual: Unknown skill URL renders browse page instead of 404 error
description:
Flow: Empty + error states, opening /skills/does-not-exist at 1280x800.

Expected: A missing skill URL should render a graceful 404/error state with a helpful message and retry/back navigation, never misleading registry content.

Actual: /skills/does-not-exist preserves the invalid URL but renders the normal browse page with the full skill list and no error message. This makes the route look valid and shows wrong data for the requested resource.

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/skill-404-route-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/skill-404-route-snapshot.md

Root cause hypothesis: App routing is implemented as pathname === '/review' ? ReviewDashboard : BrowseRegistry, so every unknown route falls through to BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open bead asr-7u0 (unknown skill route renders browse page instead of not-found state)

title: Visual: approval decision buttons have no visible effect
description: Flow: approval dashboard at /review, viewport 1280x800. Expected: Approve and Reject actions should open a decision flow, call the API, show loading/disabled state, or display a clear unavailable state. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the page unchanged with no toast, modal, status change, or error. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-approve-click-after-1280x800.png. Root cause hypothesis: buttons are rendered without click handlers or disabled/unimplemented affordance.
close_reason: Verified fixed: approval dashboard decision buttons now open a confirmation dialog, expose disabled/loading state through the modal flow, and surface success/error feedback.

title: Visual: registry outage is shown as empty search results
description: Flow: browse error state at /, viewport 1280x800, with the API container stopped. Expected: fetch failure should show a visible error message with retry and the source indicator should not say Connected to Registry. Actual: UI shows 0 skills, Connected to Registry, and 'No skills found. Try a different search term.' while console logs ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED for /api/skills. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-api-down-browse-1280x800.png. Console: .playwright-mcp/console-2026-05-24T15-46-11-838Z.log. Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry catch path collapses failures into an empty skills array without an error state.
close_reason: Implemented: browse registry outages now render a visible retryable error state instead of an empty search result

title: Visual: approval dashboard calls non-versioned submissions endpoint
description: Flow: approval dashboard at /review, viewport 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions load from the API contract in specs/api.md under /api/v1/submissions?status=pending or show a visible inline error. Actual: browser console logs 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending while the UI silently falls back to mock rows, so reviewers can see stale/wrong data. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-1280x800.png. Console: .playwright-mcp/console-2026-05-24T15-43-28-421Z.log. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard uses /api/submissions instead of the versioned /api/v1 base.
close_reason: Verified: review dashboard uses /api/v1/submissions?status=pending and renders an inline API error instead of mock rows on failure

title: Visual: Unknown routes do not show graceful 404
description: Flow: Empty + error states. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: opening /skills/does-not-exist should show a friendly 404/resource-not-found state with a retry or navigation action. Actual: the route renders the normal browse page with two skills and no indication that the requested skill/path does not exist. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-route-skills-does-not-exist.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx has no router/error route; all paths other than exact /review are treated as BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Duplicate of open bead asr-7v6 (unknown skill route does not show an error state)

title: Visual: API failures render as empty successful browse state
description: Flow: Empty + error states. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: a 4xx/5xx API response renders an inline error message/card with retry, never a blank or misleading successful state. Actual: with GET /api/skills intercepted as 500, the browse page still shows 'Connected to Registry' and 'No skills found. Try a different search term.' with zero skills/stars, no retry button, and no error language. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-api-500-skills.png. Root cause hypothesis: fetchSkills does not check res.ok and the catch path collapses API failure into the same empty array state used for legitimate empty searches.
close_reason: Verified: browse page treats non-OK skills API responses as registry errors with retry state

title: Visual: Approval decision buttons have no visible flow
description: Flow: Approval dashboard. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: approve/reject actions open a review/decision flow, require confirmation/comment where appropriate, enforce separation of duties, and visibly update status or show an error. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review produces no visible change, no modal, no disabled/loading state, and no status update. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-before.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-after-approve-click.png. Root cause hypothesis: buttons in ReviewDashboard are static type='button' elements without onClick handlers or connection to /review/:id decision UI.
close_reason: Duplicate of open bead asr-14w (approval decision buttons have no visible result)

title: Visual: Approval dashboard hides API 404 behind mock data
description: Flow: Approval dashboard. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: review queue calls the documented submissions endpoint and shows loading/error states; API failures should render an inline error card with retry. Actual: /review calls http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, which returns 404 in this dev stack, logs console errors, then silently displays mock submissions as if live. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-before.png; console shows 404 for /api/submissions?status=pending. Root cause hypothesis: web uses an unversioned/incorrect submissions endpoint and catch/fallback logic masks backend failures instead of surfacing the error state.
close_reason: Implemented: review queue uses /api/v1/submissions and surfaces API load failures with retry instead of mock fallback

title: Visual: Submission wizard route is missing
description: Flow: Submission. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: /submit exposes the new submission wizard with upload, manifest, questionnaire, review/submit steps; validation should catch empty title, oversize zip, and bad SKILL.md inline. Actual: navigating to /publish during visual review rendered the browse page; App.tsx has no /submit or /publish route and no upload/publish controls were visible from the browse UI. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-route-missing.png. Root cause hypothesis: React Router/submission wizard has not been wired; App.tsx routes everything except /review to BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Implemented: /submit now opens the submission wizard by default in mock dev sessions, with /publish preserved as an alias and primary navigation pointing to /submit

title: Visual: Skill detail route falls back to browse page
description: Flow: Skill detail. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: /skills/:owner/:name renders a dedicated skill detail screen with owner/name header, install snippet using 'asr install owner/name', preview tabs, versions, permissions, and audit per specs/web-ui.md. Actual: opening /skills/does-not-exist or any /skills/... path renders the browse grid with no 404/detail route; skill details are only an in-page modal from card clicks, and the install snippet says 'asr add ...' instead of the specified CLI command. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-security-review.png and .playwright-mcp/error-route-skills-does-not-exist.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only branches on window.location.pathname === '/review'; all other paths render BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Implemented: skill detail routes render dedicated detail/not-found surfaces with asr install snippet instead of falling back to browse.

title: Visual: Browse screen omits persistent mock auth banner
description: Flow: Auth mock mode on Landing / browse. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: specs/web-ui.md requires a persistent yellow 'Mock auth: <role>' banner in dev/mock mode so screenshots cannot be confused with production. Actual: /review shows 'Mock auth: Compliance', but / shows no mock identity banner and no other auth affordance. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry header does not share the ReviewDashboard topbar/session component and has no MockAuthProvider/session UI.
close_reason: Implemented: browse header mock auth banner now matches the required Mock auth role text

title: Visual: approval actions provide no feedback

title: Visual: approval decision buttons have no visible result
description: Flow: Approval dashboard decision actions at /review, 1280x800. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the UI unchanged with no toast, inline message, modal, status change, or disabled state. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-approve-click-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: buttons are rendered without onClick handlers or mutation wiring.

title: Visual: approval dashboard silently falls back to mock queue on API 404
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions list should reflect the API contract or show an inline error with retry if GET /api/submissions?status=pending fails. Actual: browser console shows 404 from http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, but the UI still displays three mock submissions with no error state, so reviewers can mistake fixtures for live queue data.
