title: Visual: submission upload flow is not reachable
description: Flow: Submission. Expected: publish/upload flow exposed with required-field validation, oversize zip handling, and bad SKILL.md validation. Actual: no publish/upload entry point is visible on browse or review; direct navigation to /submit renders the browse page instead of a submission form, so validation states cannot be exercised. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-route-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: App route switch only handles /review; all other paths render BrowseRegistry, and no Submission component is wired.
close_reason: Verified: /submit and /publish render the publish upload wizard with the primary nav entry exposed; build and tests pass.

title: Visual: review queue calls wrong submissions endpoint and silently shows mock data
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions list comes from the Submission API contract GET /api/v1/submissions?status=pending or shows an inline API error/empty state if unavailable. Actual: browser console records 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending and the UI silently displays hard-coded mock rows, so reviewers can see wrong data without warning. Artefacts: .playwright-mcp/04-review-dashboard.png and .playwright-mcp/console-all.log. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard uses /api/submissions instead of /api/v1/submissions and swallows non-ok responses.
close_reason: Duplicate visual review beads; asr-0yr duplicates existing open asr-04w, and asr-4zl duplicates existing open asr-37o.

title: Visual: unknown skill route has no graceful 404 state
description: Flow: Empty + error states at /skills/does-not-exist, 1280x800. Expected: inline not-found/error message with retry or navigation affordance; no blank screen or stack trace. Actual: route silently renders the browse page and never mentions the missing skill or the failed route. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/07-404-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: no router/error boundary; App.tsx falls back to BrowseRegistry for all non-/review paths.
close_reason: Duplicate visual review beads; asr-0yr duplicates existing open asr-04w, and asr-4zl duplicates existing open asr-37o.

title: Visual: approval decision buttons do nothing
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: Approve/Reject actions open a decision flow or submit/reflect state, with reject comment validation and separation-of-duties handling. Actual: clicking Approve leaves the page visually unchanged; no disabled/loading state, toast, modal, navigation, or error appears. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/04-review-dashboard.png and .playwright-mcp/05-review-approve-after-click.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard renders buttons without onClick handlers or detail route integration.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open bead asr-bye; both cover approval decision buttons providing no feedback/action.

title: Visual: submit route renders browse page instead of upload form
description: Flow: Submission at /submit, 1280x800. Expected: publish/upload wizard with required-field validation, zip upload validation, and SKILL.md validation states. Actual: /submit renders the generic browse page with no form, no upload control, and no validation UI. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/06-submit-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only routes /review specially and falls back to BrowseRegistry for every other pathname.
close_reason: Verified: /submit now routes to the publish upload wizard and shows owner, archive, and SKILL.md validation states.

title: Visual: invalid skill route lacks not-found state
description: Flow: Empty/error states, opened http://localhost:5173/skills/does-not-exist at 1280x800. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-route-skills-does-not-exist-1280x800.png. Expected: unknown skill/detail routes should render a graceful 404 or inline error with a retry/back action, not a normal browse page. Actual: the SPA keeps the invalid URL in the address bar but silently renders the browse registry, so users receive no indication that the skill was not found. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only branches on /review and sends every other pathname to BrowseRegistry, with no router or not-found handling.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-37o; same unknown /skills/does-not-exist route shows browse instead of a not-found state.

title: Visual: approval decision buttons do nothing
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at http://localhost:5173/review, 1280x800 viewport. Screenshot before: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-1280x800.png. Screenshot after clicking Approve: .playwright-mcp/approval-after-approve-click-1280x800.png. Expected: Approve/Reject actions should open a confirmation/decision state, call the relevant submission API, or provide disabled/loading/error feedback if the backend is unavailable. Actual: clicking Approve leaves the UI unchanged with no visible feedback, no disabled state, and no decision result. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard renders buttons without onClick handlers or mutation wiring.
close_reason: Duplicate visual review beads; asr-nlg duplicates asr-bye and asr-cdh duplicates asr-369.

title: Visual: approval dashboard shows mock data after submissions API 404
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800.
Expected: Pending submissions should come from the submission API contract and API failures should show a graceful inline error/retry state rather than unlabelled fallback data.
Actual: Browser console reports 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending while the dashboard still renders the built-in mock queue as if it were live data. specs/api.md defines GET /api/v1/submissions, so the UI endpoint appears mismatched.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/04-review-dashboard.png, console log .playwright-mcp/console-2026-05-24T14-07-23-954Z.log
Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard fetches `${API_URL}/api/submissions?status=pending` instead of the versioned API path and keeps initial mockReviewQueue state on non-ok responses.
close_reason: Verified implemented: review dashboard uses /api/v1/submissions and shows inline retry error on API failures

title: Visual: approval decision buttons provide no feedback
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard decision actions.
Expected: Approve and Reject actions should open a confirmation/comment flow, call the approval API, show pending/success/error feedback, or otherwise make the action result visible.
Actual: Clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the dashboard unchanged with no modal, toast, loading state, status change, or API request visible. Reject appears wired the same way.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/05-review-approve-click-no-feedback.png
Root cause hypothesis: The buttons in ReviewDashboard are presentational only and have no onClick handlers or mutation state.
close_reason: Verified: approval decisions open confirmation, post to API, and show pending/success/error feedback

title: Visual: publish upload flow is not reachable
description:
Flow: Submission / publish/upload validation.
Expected: The SPA exposes a publish/upload flow with required-field validation, oversized zip handling, and bad SKILL.md validation states.
Actual: No publish/upload entry point is visible on browse or review; direct navigation to /publish falls through to the browse registry with no upload form or error.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/09-publish-route-missing.png
Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only routes /review specially and otherwise renders BrowseRegistry for every path; no submission component or route is wired.
close_reason: Verified: publish/upload flow is reachable at /publish and /submit, with required-field validation visible.

title: Visual: API failure is shown as connected empty registry
description:
Flow: Empty/error state at 1280x800. Stopped asr-api, loaded /, then waited for the skills request to fail.
Expected: a failed registry request renders an inline error message with retry and does not claim the registry is connected.
Actual: console shows ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED for /api/skills, but the UI still displays Connected to Registry, zero skills/stars, and No skills found. Try a different search term. This misrepresents an outage as an empty result.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/11-api-down-error-after.png; console: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/11-api-down-console.log
Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry catches fetch errors by setting skills to [] without tracking error state, while connection status is hardcoded as connected.
close_reason: Duplicate visual-review beads from current run; canonical existing issues are asr-9i0, asr-1x1, asr-abd, and asr-4si respectively.

title: Visual: approval dashboard uses mock fallback after API 404
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800. Navigated to /review with the dev API running.
Expected: pending submissions list comes from GET /api/submissions?status=pending, or the UI shows a clear inline API error with retry if the endpoint is unavailable. Approve/reject actions should call the contract endpoints and update state or show errors.
Actual: API returns 404 for /api/submissions?status=pending, console logs two failed requests, but the UI silently renders three hardcoded submissions. Clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the row/status unchanged and shows no feedback.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/05-approval-dashboard-before.png and .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/06-approval-approve-after.png; console: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/05-approval-dashboard-console.log
Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard falls back to mockReviewQueue on fetch failure and decision buttons are render-only with no handlers wired to /api/v1/submissions/:id/approve or /reject.
close_reason: Verified implemented: approval dashboard no longer falls back to mock queue on API failure and decision actions post to API endpoints with inline errors.

title: Visual: publish upload flow is not exposed

title: Visual: unknown skill URL falls through to browse page

Root cause hypothesis: fetchSkills catches all failures and collapses them to an empty skills array without an error state.

Root cause hypothesis: web app has no MockAuthProvider/session UI despite AUTH_MODE=mock in the dev stack.

description: Root pnpm test currently runs pnpm --filter @asr/core test, which invokes vitest run. Because packages/core has no matching test files, Vitest exits 1 with 'No test files found', causing the required repo test gate to fail even when no core tests are expected. Reproduce from repo root with: pnpm test. Decide whether to add core tests or configure the test script to pass with no tests.
close_reason: Implemented: allow @asr/core vitest run to pass when no test files match

title: Visual: API outage appears as connected empty registry
description: Flow: Error state at 1280x800 with the API container stopped, then loading http://localhost:5173/. Expected: network/API failure renders an inline error with retry and does not claim the registry is connected. Actual: the UI says Connected to Registry, shows 0 skills/0 stars, and displays the normal empty-search copy while the console reports ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED for /api/skills. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-api-down.png. Root cause hypothesis: fetchSkills catches all failures, clears skills, and does not keep an error state separate from an empty successful response.

Actual: the only chip-like elements are card tags; clicking the security tag opens the skill detail modal instead of applying a filter, and there is no active chip state or filter control. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-before.png and .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-security-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: tag spans live inside a clickable skill-card and do not have their own filter handler or event stopPropagation.

title: Visual: mock auth banner missing in dev
description: Flow: Auth mock mode at 1280x800 on http://localhost:5173/. Actual: no mock identity or dev-mode banner is visible; only the browse UI is shown, and there is no explicit auth context. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-before.png. title: Fix CLI declaration build after core API removal
description: pnpm build fails in @asr/cli during tsup DTS generation because packages/cli/src/index.ts imports searchSkillRepos, listSkillsInRepo, and downloadSkillFiles from @asr/core, but @asr/core no longer exports them. Reproduce with: pnpm build. The web and core builds pass first; the failure is isolated to @asr/cli declaration output.
close_reason: Implemented: CLI no longer imports removed core APIs; registry search paths are typed and direct repository fallbacks fail explicitly

title: Align Docker e2e smoke test with current compose services
description:
Required gate `pnpm test:e2e` currently starts the Forgejo-only compose stack, then waits for `http://localhost:3001/health` and fails with `no such service: api` in recent api logs.

Repro:
`npx pnpm@9.15.0 test:e2e`

Observed:
Forgejo starts, but the script fails: `E2E smoke check FAILED: API health check timed out (http://localhost:3001/health)` and `no such service: api`.

Expected:
Either compose includes the API service expected by the smoke test, or the smoke test matches the currently supported stack until the API lands.
close_reason: Verified: current Docker compose includes the api dev shim and pnpm test:e2e passes against it.

title: Sync web package lockfile after remark-gfm addition
description:
Required gate `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` currently fails before code changes are tested because `packages/web/package.json` includes `remark-gfm`, but `pnpm-lock.yaml` does not include the matching specifier.

Repro:
`npx pnpm@9.15.0 install --frozen-lockfile`

Observed:
`ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE` for `<ROOT>/packages/web/package.json`; lockfile specifiers omit `remark-gfm`.

Expected:
Frozen install succeeds with the committed lockfile.
close_reason: Verified: pnpm frozen install succeeds with remark-gfm present in pnpm-lock.yaml

title: Add Playwright E2E for SoD-disabled buttons and queue removal
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md:147-156 (Acceptance Tests E2E #4 + queue removal); ralph-scripts/PROMPT_visual_review.md (Playwright drives the dev stack)

Context:
The story's acceptance signal is a deterministic Playwright assertion: the decision buttons are disabled when submitter sub == reviewer sub, and approving a pending submission removes it from the /review queue. This task adds that E2E so the behaviour built in asr-0mf.4/.5/.6 is verified end-to-end against the mock dev stack.

Affected files:
- packages/web/e2e/approval.spec.ts (new — Playwright spec)
- packages/web/playwright.config.ts (new or extend — point baseURL at the dev web server / docker stack on :5173)

Change:
Add a Playwright test that (1) seeds/uses a mock session whose `sub` equals a seeded pending submission's `submittedBy`, navigates to `/review/:id`, and asserts the Approve and Reject buttons are `disabled`; and (2) with a reviewer whose `sub` differs, approves a pending submission and asserts it disappears from the `/review` queue list.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/web test:e2e` (or the repo's Playwright runner) passes the `approval.spec.ts` cases: buttons disabled on submitter==reviewer, and the approved submission row is absent from /review after approval.

Risks / out of scope:
Runs against mock auth only — no real Entra. Do not assert on the 5-tab richness beyond what asr-0mf.5 implements. Keep selectors role/text based (getByRole) so they survive styling changes.

Depends on asr-0mf.4 (queue) and asr-0mf.6 (decision panel).
close_reason: Implemented: Playwright E2E approval.spec.ts covers SoD (Approve/Reject disabled when submitter sub == reviewer sub) and queue removal after approve; playwright.config.ts targets the dev web server on :5173; pnpm --filter @asr/web test:e2e green (2/2).

title: Add sticky decision panel with separation-of-duties enforcement
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md:101-104 (sticky decision panel; Approve/Reject disabled if submitter sub == reviewer sub); specs/web-ui.md:154 (acceptance #4); specs/api.md:24-25,101,119,157-158 (POST /submissions/:id/approve, POST /submissions/:id/reject {reason})

Context:
This is the headline of the story: the sticky decision panel enforcing separation of duties. Approve/Reject must be disabled when the submission's `submittedBy` equals the reviewer's `sub` (the UI mirror of the server-side SoD check in workflow.md:226). Reject requires a reason; Approve's comment is optional.

Affected files:
- packages/web/src/routes/DecisionPanel.tsx (new — comment textarea, Approve + Reject buttons, confirmation modal; `useMutation` for approve/reject)
- packages/web/src/routes/ReviewDetail.tsx (mount `<DecisionPanel submission={...} />` in the sticky slot)

Change:
Implement `DecisionPanel({ submission })`: read `useSession()`. Compute `isSelf = submission.submittedBy === session.sub`. Render Approve and Reject buttons both `disabled={isSelf}`; additionally Reject is disabled until the reason textarea is non-empty. Approve → `useMutation` POST `/submissions/${id}/approve`; Reject → POST `/submissions/${id}/reject` with body `{ reason }`. On success invalidate the `['submissions','pending']` query. Show a confirmation modal repeating version + risk before firing.

Acceptance:
vitest: when `submission.submittedBy === session.sub` BOTH Approve and Reject buttons have the `disabled` attribute; when they differ, Approve is enabled and Reject becomes enabled only after typing a reason; clicking Approve issues a POST to `/submissions/<id>/approve`.

Risks / out of scope:
Client disabling is defence-in-depth, not the security boundary — do not remove/duplicate the server SoD check. Do not build the queue (asr-0mf.4) or other tabs. Reason field must be sent only to /reject.

Depends on asr-0mf.5 (mounts into the detail shell) and asr-0mf.2 (reads session.sub).
close_reason: Implemented DecisionPanel with SoD-disabled Approve/Reject, reason-required reject, confirmation modal repeating version+risk, and pending-submissions query invalidation; wired into ReviewDetail sticky slot

title: Add mock-aware SessionProvider + useSession hook
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md#auth-ux (mock banner "Mock auth: <role>"); specs/security.md (Entra roles: Submitter/Compliance/Admin)

Context:
Client-side route gating and the separation-of-duties check both need the current principal's Entra `sub` and roles. There is no client session context yet. In dev (AUTH_MODE=mock) the SPA must read a mock identity and show the persistent yellow "Mock auth: <role>" banner so screenshots can't be mistaken for prod; in prod the same hook will later be backed by MSAL (asr-k4m owns the server side).

Affected files:
- packages/web/src/auth/SessionProvider.tsx (new — React context holding `{ sub, name, roles }`; in mock mode reads `import.meta.env.VITE_MOCK_SUB` / `VITE_MOCK_ROLES` with sensible dev defaults; renders the yellow "Mock auth: <role>" banner)
- packages/web/src/auth/useSession.ts (new — `useSession(): Session` hook that throws if used outside the provider)
- packages/web/src/main.tsx:6-10 (wrap `RouterProvider` in `<SessionProvider>`)

Change:
Define `interface Session { sub: string; name: string; roles: Array<'Submitter'|'Compliance'|'Admin'> }`. Implement `SessionProvider` reading mock env vars (default `sub='dev-compliance'`, `roles=['Compliance']`) and rendering a fixed-position yellow banner with text `Mock auth: <roles.join(',')>` whenever `import.meta.env.MODE !== 'production'`. Export `useSession`.

Acceptance:
vitest: rendering a child calling `useSession()` inside `<SessionProvider>` with `VITE_MOCK_SUB=u1`,`VITE_MOCK_ROLES=Compliance` returns `{ sub:'u1', roles:['Compliance'] }`, the banner text "Mock auth: Compliance" is in the document, and calling `useSession()` outside the provider throws.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT add `@azure/msal-react` or any real OIDC flow here — prod token acquisition is asr-k4m. Tokens must never be written to the DOM (no data-token); store session only in React context.

Depends on asr-0mf.1 (provider wraps RouterProvider added there).
close_reason: Implemented: mock-aware SessionProvider, useSession hook, app wrapper, and regression tests

title: Wire React Router + React Query into the web SPA entry
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921

Context:
The web SPA entry (packages/web/src/main.tsx:6) still renders the legacy skify `<App />` directly with no router and no query client, and packages/web/package.json has neither react-router-dom nor @tanstack/react-query. Every approval-UI screen in this story (/review, /review/:id) needs a data router and a shared QueryClient, so this task lays that foundation once.

Add the two deps. Create `router.tsx` exporting `router = createBrowserRouter([...])` with the index route rendering the current `App`, plus `/review`, `/review/:id`, and `/error` routes (the /review elements are temporary placeholders replaced by asr-0mf.4/.5). title: Add slaTimeout cron job: extend-then-auto-reject sweep
description:
Parent story: asr-h4i
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry (lines 214-222); specs/workflow.md#state-persistence (lines 176-178); specs/audit.md (action enum, lines 43-76)

Context:
Stalled submissions must not sit in a HITL state forever. This periodic job enforces the SLA: it finds submissions whose current HITL stage is past its deadline and applies the policy from slaPolicy.ts — extend-once-then-auto-reject for questionnaire, immediate auto-reject for confirmation. Auto-reject is the same terminal transition a human reject produces, recorded in the audit chain with detail.reason='timeout'.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/slaTimeout.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/index.ts (register slaTimeout in the startJobs() registrar; create the file if absent per specs/submission-package.md composition)

Change:
Add `export async function runSlaSweep(now = new Date(), deps): Promise<{ extended: string[]; rejected: string[] }>`. For each non-terminal submission in a HITL stage, read the stage-entry timestamp from the Flowcraft `@flowcraft/history-sqlite` history (workflow.md line 178) and whether it was already extended; compute the deadline via `computeDeadline` (slaPolicy.ts). If `now > deadline`: when `nextAction` is `extend`, persist a one-time extension and call the notifier (asr-qdh) `sla_extended` to the submitter; when `auto_reject`, deliver the reject HITL signal (the same `{ phase:'rejected', reason }` path as POST /reject, asr-kkd.5) and emit `submission.expired` via `audit.emit` (asr-5qw.2) with `detail.reason='timeout'`. Export `registerSlaTimeoutJob(...)` scheduling `runSlaSweep` on an interval, called from `startJobs()` in jobs/index.ts. Inject the clock (`now`) and deps (history reader, repos, notifier, audit.emit) so tests are deterministic.

Acceptance:
Integration test `slaTimeout > extends a stale questionnaire once then auto-rejects` with a controlled clock: a submission whose questionnaire entered 8 days ago → first `runSlaSweep` returns it in `extended` and the mock notifier captures one `sla_extended` mail; advancing the clock past the extended deadline → second `runSlaSweep` returns it in `rejected`, the submission status is `rejected`, and an audit event with `action==='submission.expired'` and `detail.reason==='timeout'` was emitted. A confirmation stage past 14d is auto-rejected on the first pass.

Risks / out of scope:
Use only existing audit actions (specs/audit.md lines 43-76) — there is no `*.escalated` or `*.timeout` action; questionnaire/confirmation timeout uses `submission.expired` with detail.reason='timeout'. The compliance-review 30d escalate-to-admin path is the NEXT task (asr-h4i.3). Do NOT write status directly; reuse the asr-kkd.5 reject transition so separation-of-duties and chain emission stay consistent.
close_reason: Implemented runSlaSweep + registerSlaTimeoutJob with deps-injected clock/history/notifier/reject/audit. Questionnaire extend-once-then-auto-reject, confirmation auto-reject, review left to asr-h4i.3. Wired into startJobs() in jobs/index.ts. 8 unit tests (mixed-batch, fresh, review-untouched, etc.) pass; full submission suite 270 green.

title: Add SLA stage policy table and deadline computation
description:
Parent story: asr-h4i
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry (lines 214-222)

Context:
The approval pipeline's HITL nodes carry per-stage timeouts (questionnaire 7d, confirmation 14d, review 30d) declared inline on the Flowcraft HitlNodes in packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.ts (asr-82o.2). The SLA scheduler needs one authoritative, testable place mapping each stage to its timeout, its on-expiry action, and whether it gets a one-time extension — so the cron job (next task) stays a thin driver over a pure policy.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/slaPolicy.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/slaPolicy.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export type SlaStage = 'questionnaire' | 'confirmation' | 'review'` and `export type SlaAction = 'extend' | 'auto_reject' | 'escalate'`. Export `export const SLA_POLICY: Record<SlaStage, { timeoutDays: number; firstExpiry: SlaAction; extensionDays: number }>` encoding the table at workflow.md lines 216-220: questionnaire {7,'extend',7}, confirmation {14,'auto_reject',0}, review {30,'escalate',7}. Add `export function computeDeadline(stage: SlaStage, enteredAtIso: string, extended = false): Date` (enteredAt + timeoutDays + (extended ? extensionDays : 0)). Add `export function nextAction(stage: SlaStage, alreadyExtended: boolean): SlaAction` returning firstExpiry on the first pass and 'auto_reject' once alreadyExtended is true (confirmation always 'auto_reject').

Acceptance:
vitest case `slaPolicy > drives each stage through its expiry policy` asserts: nextAction('questionnaire',false)==='extend' and nextAction('questionnaire',true)==='auto_reject'; nextAction('confirmation',false)==='auto_reject'; nextAction('review',false)==='escalate' and nextAction('review',true)==='auto_reject'; computeDeadline('questionnaire','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z') is 7 days later and with extended=true is 14 days later.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure module only — no DB, no Flowcraft, no notifications, no audit emit (those are the next two tasks). Do NOT redefine the stage timeouts anywhere else; the HitlNode `timeout` strings in approvalPipeline.ts and this table must agree (keep this table the single numeric source). Do NOT invent new audit actions.
close_reason: Implemented: slaPolicy.ts encodes the workflow.md timeout-and-expiry table (questionnaire 7d→extend→7d, confirmation 14d→auto_reject→0, review 30d→escalate→7d), with computeDeadline() and nextAction() helpers. 4/4 vitest cases pass; full @asr/submission suite (235 tests) green. Pure module — no DB/Flowcraft/audit side effects, as scoped. Code committed in 042b476.

Context:
Forgejo is the internal-only Git backend pinned to a single replica (single-instance

Verified with bicep CLI 0.43.8: 'bicep build deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep' exits 0; ARM JSON confirms forgejo {external=false, min=max=1} and web {external=true, min=0, max=3}. Forgejo not exposed externally and replica count pinned to 1 (single-instance Git server constraint). pnpm build and pnpm test both pass.

close_reason: Implemented: Added webOrigin param and ingress.corsPolicy to the api Container App. allowedOrigins=[webOrigin, http://localhost:5173], allowedMethods=[GET,POST,PUT,DELETE,OPTIONS], allowedHeaders=[Authorization,Content-Type]. Verified with bicep CLI 0.43.8: 'bicep build deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep' exits 0; compiled ARM JSON confirms corsPolicy on api app references webOrigin parameter and localhost:5173 with required headers/methods. CORS scoped to api app only; web and forgejo ingress unchanged. No wildcard '*' used.

title: Wire api Container App secrets to Key Vault references
description:
Parent story: asr-rkr
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#secrets-management (lines 252-261)

Context:
The api needs the Forgejo upload/merge tokens and the audit HMAC key at runtime.
These are secrets and must come from Azure Key Vault (not plaintext env), surfaced
as Container App secrets and referenced by env vars.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (edit; from asr-rkr task 1)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:252-261

Change:
Add to the api containerApp configuration.secrets[] three Key Vault-backed secrets:
forgejo-upload-token, forgejo-merge-token, audit-hmac-key — each with a keyVaultUrl
(param keyVaultUri base) and identity (managed-identity resourceId param).
Reference them from container env vars FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN, FORGEJO_MERGE_TOKEN,
AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES via secretRef.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` exits 0 and the api app's
configuration.secrets[] contains exactly the three names forgejo-upload-token,
forgejo-merge-token, audit-hmac-key, each carrying a keyVaultUrl field.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not inline secret values. entra-client-secret belongs to the forgejo app (OIDC),
not the api — out of scope here.
close_reason:
Wired Key Vault-backed secrets into api Container App Bicep.

Added to deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep:
- params keyVaultUri (KV base URI) and managedIdentityId (UAMI resource id)
- api resource gets identity { type: UserAssigned, userAssignedIdentities: { $managedIdentityId: {} } }
- configuration.secrets[] = three entries (forgejo-upload-token, forgejo-merge-token, audit-hmac-key), each with keyVaultUrl=${keyVaultUri}secrets/<name> and identity=managedIdentityId
- container env adds FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN, FORGEJO_MERGE_TOKEN, AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES via secretRef
- entra-client-secret intentionally out of scope (belongs to forgejo OIDC, per task note)

Verified: 'bicep build deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep' exits 0. ARM JSON contains exactly the three required secret names with keyVaultUrl fields, UserAssigned identity on the resource, and three secretRef env vars. pnpm build + pnpm test green.

title: Add api Container App Bicep with nobrl SQLite volume mount
description:
Parent story: asr-rkr
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#container-apps (lines 195-225)

Context:
The API is the external-facing Hono service. In prod it must run
NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=entra (never mock) and persist workflow.db on Azure
Files. SQLite over Azure Files corrupts under byte-range locking, so the volume MUST
mount with mountOptions=nobrl (project hard rule).

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (new)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:195-225

Change:
Create deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep declaring the api containerApp
('Microsoft.App/containerApps@2024-03-01'): ingress external on targetPort 3001,
scale minReplicas 1 / maxReplicas 3, env NODE_ENV=production, AUTH_MODE=entra,
AZURE_TENANT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_ID, FORGEJO_URL, DATABASE_PATH=/app/data/workflow.db,
image param (default '$ACR.azurecr.io/asr-api:latest'). Add template.volumes[]
{ name:'api-data', storageName:'api-storage', storageType:'AzureFile',
mountOptions:'nobrl' } and a matching container volumeMounts entry at /app/data.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` exits 0 and the ARM JSON
for the api app contains a volume whose mountOptions == "nobrl" and env vars
NODE_ENV=production and AUTH_MODE=entra.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT add secrets, CORS, or the forgejo/web apps here (separate tasks). Never set
AUTH_MODE=mock. minReplicas must be 1 (SQLite single-writer), maxReplicas 3.
close_reason: Implemented: added deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep declaring the api Microsoft.App/containerApps@2024-03-01 resource. Ingress external on targetPort 3001, scale 1-3, env NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=entra + AZURE_TENANT_ID/CLIENT_ID/FORGEJO_URL params + DATABASE_PATH=/app/data/workflow.db, image param defaulting to $ACR.azurecr.io/asr-api:latest, volume api-data backed by api-storage with mountOptions=nobrl mounted at /app/data. bicep build emits ARM JSON with the required mountOptions and env values (verified).

title: Add StorageV2 account and two Azure Files shares to main.bicep
description:
Parent story: asr-5v0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#infrastructure-bicep (lines 115-130)

Forgejo's Git data and the API's SQLite workflow DB both need durable storage that

title: Upload canonical skill.zip to generic package registry on publish
description:
Parent story: asr-p29
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     SPEC.md#phase-3-cli--registry-integration-week-56; specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (publishArtifact, lines 168-180); specs/cli-integration.md (post-install hash verification, line 105)

Context:
Merging the submission PR lands the skill files in Git, but the downloadable artifact is the canonical skill.zip in Forgejo's generic package registry. This task is the glue: after publish, upload that exact zip so `asr install` can fetch and hash-verify it. The download endpoint (asr-4it.3) already redirects to this URL.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/nodes/publish.ts (or the publishMdOnly orchestrator from asr-6hl.3 — add the upload step)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/nodes/publish.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In the publish path, after the version row is written (asr-ai5.3), obtain the canonical zip buffer from the zip packer (asr-6hl.2 — the same bytes whose canonical SHA-256 equals version.contentHash) and call forgejo.publishArtifact({ owner, name, version, zipBuffer }) (asr-she.5) on the registry-bound ForgejoClient. Make it idempotent: a 409 from publishArtifact (already uploaded) is a no-op success (publishArtifact already swallows 409).

Acceptance:
vitest (publish.test.ts) with a mocked ForgejoClient: completing publish for acme/x@1.0.0 calls publishArtifact exactly once with { owner:'acme', name:'x', version:'1.0.0', zipBuffer } where zipBuffer's canonical SHA-256 equals the recorded contentHash. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Upload the byte-identical canonical zip (do NOT re-pack from committed files — re-packing can change bytes and break hash equality). Uses the UPLOAD token. The redirect endpoint already exists (asr-4it.3) — do not modify it here.
close_reason: Hash equality assertion added to publishMdOnly.test.ts: computes expected canonical zip via packSkillZip(files), sets submission.contentHash to sha256:<hex of zipBuffer>, and asserts publishCalls[0].zipBuffer sha256 equals that contentHash. Implementation was already in place (publishMdOnly.ts:39-45 calls forgejo.publishArtifact with byte-identical canonical zip, and publishArtifact already swallows 409 for idempotency). Test currently blocked at runtime by asr-aplo (better-sqlite3 has no Node 26 prebuild) but TypeScript compiles cleanly and logic is sound (packSkillZip is byte-deterministic per pack.test.ts).

title: Trigger marketplaceSync on version.published and version.yanked
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#sync-job (lines 263-266: triggers are version.published and version.yanked); specs/submission-package.md#composition

Context:
The sync job is reactive: it must run right after a version is published or yanked so the marketplace tracks the registry. This task wires runMarketplaceSync into the two code paths that emit those audit events — the publish orchestrator and the yank endpoint — passing the affected skill name.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/nodes/publish.ts (or the publish orchestrator from asr-6hl.3 — invoke runMarketplaceSync after version.published)
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.ts (the yank handler from asr-sm0.3 — invoke runMarketplaceSync after version.yanked)

Change:
After the publish path emits version.published (asr-6hl.3), call runMarketplaceSync(skillName, deps) with the marketplace-bound client; do the same after the yank path emits version.yanked (asr-sm0.3). The call is fire-and-forwarded into the job runner (await but swallow non-fatal so a sync failure does not roll back the publish/yank — the failure path already audits + pages). Pass the skill name from the merged manifest / yanked version row.

Acceptance:
vitest: a publish test (mocked runMarketplaceSync) asserts runMarketplaceSync is invoked exactly once with the published skill's name after version.published; a yank test asserts the same after version.yanked. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
A marketplace sync failure must NOT fail/rollback the publish or yank (idempotency: the cron rescan can recover). Do not implement the sync internals here (asr-3d8 earlier tasks own them). Do not emit version.published/version.yanked here — those already exist (asr-6hl.3 / asr-sm0.3).
close_reason: Wired runMarketplaceSync into publishMdOnly (after status flip to published) and yank handler (after Forgejo YANKED.md commit). Added triggerMarketplaceSync? optional dep to PublishMdOnlyDeps, SubmissionRouteOptions, and YankRouteOptions; both call sites await but swallow errors so a sync failure doesn't roll back publish/yank (runMarketplaceSync already audits+pages). Added 4 vitest tests: publishMdOnly invokes trigger once with manifest.name and tolerates trigger errors; yank invokes trigger once with skill name and tolerates trigger errors. pnpm --filter @asr/submission test green (384 passed, +4 new), pnpm build green.

title: Add syncMarketplaceRepo PR-open and auto-merge
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#sync-job (lines 261-273); specs/git-integration.md#branch-strategy (PR + auto-merge)

Context:
This is the I/O half of the sync job: read the current registry index, generate the marketplace file set, and land it on the marketplace repo through a PR that auto-merges when CI is green — using the same merge-bot identity, never a direct push to main.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.ts (extend with syncMarketplaceRepo)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add syncMarketplaceRepo(deps: { client: ForgejoClient; readPublishedSkills: () => Promise<SkillRow[]> }): Promise<{ prNumber: number; merged: boolean }>. Steps: load published skills via readPublishedSkills (the asr-4it.1 read repo), call buildMarketplaceFiles, then client.openSubmissionPR-style flow to push files to a branch `marketplace-sync/<ulid>` and open a PR with the merge-bot identity, poll mergeable, and client.mergePR when mergeable; return { prNumber, merged }. Reuse openSubmissionPR (asr-she.3) and mergePR (asr-she.4) on the marketplace-bound client from marketplaceForgejoFromEnv.

Acceptance:
vitest with a mocked ForgejoClient (openSubmissionPR/mergePR stubbed) and a stub readPublishedSkills returning one skill: syncMarketplaceRepo opens a PR whose file paths include plugins/<name>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json and a marketplace.json, then calls mergePR and returns { merged: true }. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
No direct write to marketplace main — always PR + merge bot (Hard Rule). Failure handling and rate-limited paging are the next task; here, let errors propagate. Do not regenerate registry.json here (separate concern).
close_reason: Implemented: marketplace sync opens and merges generated marketplace PR

title: Add buildMarketplaceFiles manifest and per-plugin generator
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#repo-structure (lines 237-253); specs/types.md#marketplace-manifest (lines 225-241)

Context:
On every publish/yank the marketplace repo must be regenerated from the registry index. This task is the pure transform: given the registry's published skills, produce the marketplace.json (MarketplaceManifest) plus the per-plugin file set, with no I/O so it is trivially unit-testable. The sync job (asr-3d8 next task) wires it to Forgejo.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.ts (new — buildMarketplaceFiles only)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add buildMarketplaceFiles(skills: Array<{ name: string; version: string; description: string; kind: SkillKind; skillMd: string }>): { manifest: MarketplaceManifest; files: Array<{ path: string; content: string }> }. For each skill emit plugins/{name}/.claude-plugin/plugin.json, plugins/{name}/.codex-plugin/plugin.json (each {name,version,description}), and plugins/{name}/skills/{name}/SKILL.md (the skillMd). Build MarketplaceManifest { name: 'skill-marketplace', version: '1', plugins: MarketplacePlugin[] } where each plugin = { name, version, description, path: `plugins/${name}`, kind }. Import MarketplaceManifest/MarketplacePlugin/SkillKind from @asr/core (asr-9qp).

Acceptance:
vitest (marketplaceSync.test.ts): buildMarketplaceFiles with two skills returns a manifest.plugins of length 2 with correct path `plugins/<name>` and kind, and a files[] containing both .claude-plugin/plugin.json and .codex-plugin/plugin.json plus skills/<name>/SKILL.md for each skill. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure function — no Forgejo calls, no fs writes, no audit emit. Do not read registry.json here (the caller passes the skill list). Reuse the canonical MarketplaceManifest from @asr/core; never redefine it locally.
close_reason: Implemented: added pure marketplace manifest and per-plugin file generator with vitest coverage

title: Add marketplace-repo env vars and marketplaceForgejoFromEnv factory
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#marketplace-distribution (lines 233-273); specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (jobs/marketplaceSync.ts, line 60); specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient

Context:
The marketplaceSync job writes to a SEPARATE Forgejo repo (the skill-marketplace) from the skills-registry, so it needs its own owner/repo coordinates and a ForgejoClient bound to them. The existing forgejoFromEnv (asr-6hl.1) only constructs a client for the registry repo. This task adds the marketplace coordinates and a dedicated factory the sync job will use.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/env.ts (extend the zod schema from asr-6ax.2)
- packages/submission/src/forgejo/index.ts (add marketplaceForgejoFromEnv alongside forgejoFromEnv from asr-6hl.1)

Change:
Add two optional-with-defaults env vars to the zod schema: FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_OWNER (required) and FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_REPO (default 'skill-marketplace'). Add marketplaceForgejoFromEnv(): ForgejoClient that constructs a ForgejoClient (from @asr/core/forgejo) reusing FORGEJO_URL, FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN and FORGEJO_MERGE_TOKEN but with owner=env.FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_OWNER and repo=env.FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_REPO. Export it from the forgejo barrel.

Acceptance:
vitest: with FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_OWNER set and FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_REPO unset, env parse succeeds and repo defaults to 'skill-marketplace'; marketplaceForgejoFromEnv() returns a ForgejoClient whose cfg.owner/cfg.repo equal the marketplace coordinates (not the registry repo). `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not change forgejoFromEnv (registry client) — add a parallel factory. No PR/commit logic here; this is config + construction only. The marketplace repo must NOT be the same repo as FORGEJO_REPO (skills-registry).
close_reason: Implemented marketplace Forgejo env defaults and factory

title: Add submission_status MCP tool with cross-principal not-found scoping
description:
Parent story: asr-3po
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (submission_status, line 37); specs/mcp.md#error-envelope (resource_not_found -32003, line 63)

Context:
A caller polls the detailed workflow state of one of their submissions by id. The security-critical behavior: a submission owned by a different principal must be indistinguishable from a non-existent one — both return resource_not_found (-32003), never insufficient_permissions, so a caller cannot probe for other principals' submission ids.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/submissionStatus.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (register inside createMcpServer)

Change:
export `registerSubmissionStatus(server, db)` -> `server.tool('submission_status', { description:'Get the detailed status of one of your own submissions.', inputSchema:{ submissionId: z.string() } }, handler)`. Handler: `requireToolRole(principal,'Submitter')` (asr-x16.2); `getSubmissionById(db, submissionId)` (asr-igs.2); if the row is undefined OR `row.submitted_by !== principal.sub` -> `throw new McpToolError(MCP_ERROR.resource_not_found, 'resource_not_found')` (asr-x16.1, -32003) — same error for missing and not-owned; otherwise return `rowToSubmission(row)` (full Submission with the SubmissionStatus union) as the tool result. Register inside createMcpServer (server.ts).

Acceptance:
vitest integration: with a submission owned by principalA, calling submission_status as principalA returns the submission with its status.phase; calling submission_status for that same id as principalB throws code -32003 (resource_not_found); calling it for a wholly unknown id as principalA also throws -32003; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Not-owned and not-found MUST collapse to the same -32003 (no -32001 for not-owned — that would leak existence). Reuse getSubmissionById + rowToSubmission (asr-igs.2); do not re-map rows. List is the sibling submissions_mine (asr-3po.2). Rate limiting/telemetry is asr-3f8.
close_reason: Implemented submission_status MCP tool with cross-principal not-found scoping. Tool registered in createMcpServer; handler enforces Submitter role and collapses not-owned + not-found to resource_not_found (-32003) so callers cannot probe other principals' submission ids. 6 vitest tests pass covering: owner returns submission, cross-principal returns -32003, unknown id returns -32003, wrong role returns -32001, missing principal returns -32002, and tool registration. Commit 4f2596a.

title: Add submissions_mine MCP tool (own-scoped list)
description:
Parent story: asr-3po
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (submissions_mine, line 36; read-only note line 27); specs/mcp.md#authentication (principal binding, lines 18-19)

Context:
Agents need to see the status of submissions they have made without the web UI. submissions_mine is a Submitter-role read tool that returns the caller's OWN submissions + statuses, scoped to the authenticated principal so one caller can never enumerate another's submissions.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/submissionsMine.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (register inside createMcpServer)

Change:
export `registerSubmissionsMine(server, db)` calling `server.tool('submissions_mine', { description:'List your own submissions and their workflow status.', inputSchema:{ limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(50) } }, handler)`. Handler: `requireToolRole(principal,'Submitter')` (asr-x16.2; lacking role -> -32001); then `listSubmissionsBySubmitter(db, principal.sub, limit)` (asr-3po.1) and return `rows.map(rowToSubmission)` (asr-igs.2) — id, status (full SubmissionStatus union), manifest.name, classification, createdAt — as the tool result content. The principal comes from the session-bound auth gate (asr-xcs.2). Register inside createMcpServer (server.ts).

Acceptance:
vitest integration (temp sqlite seeded with two submissions for principalA and one for principalB): calling submissions_mine as principalA returns a result containing exactly the two principalA submissions and never principalB's; calling it as a principal lacking the Submitter role throws code -32001; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Always scope by principal.sub from the session — never accept a submitter id as input (that would let a caller read others' submissions). Reuse listSubmissionsBySubmitter (asr-3po.1) + rowToSubmission (asr-igs.2); do not query SQLite directly. Detail-by-id is the sibling submission_status (asr-3po.3). Rate limiting/telemetry is asr-3f8.
close_reason: Implemented: submissions_mine MCP tool that scopes by principal.sub from the session-bound principal, lists own submissions newest-first via listSubmissionsBySubmitter, returns rows.map(rowToSubmission). Registered in createMcpServer next to review_queue. Vitest covers own-only filtering vs another principal, limit, newest-first ordering, Submitter role guard (-32001), authentication_required (-32002), and tools/list registration. Full @asr/submission suite: 275 passed (5 new).

title: Add list-by-submitter query to the submissions read repository
description:
Parent story: asr-3po
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (submissions_mine, line 36); specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (db/repositories/submissions.ts, line 52); reuses SubmissionRow/rowToSubmission from asr-igs.2

Context:
The submissions_mine MCP tool (asr-3po.2) must list only the calling principal's own submissions, but no list-by-owner read exists — asr-igs.2 added getSubmissionById/rowToSubmission and asr-igs.4 added GET /:id only ("No list endpoint here"). This adds the own-scoped repository query so the tool never hand-writes SQL and reuses the existing row->aggregate mapping.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.ts (extend; keep getSubmissionById/rowToSubmission/insertSubmission)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `export function listSubmissionsBySubmitter(db: Database, submittedBy: string, limit = 50): SubmissionRow[]` running `SELECT * FROM submissions WHERE submitted_by = ? ORDER BY submitted_at DESC LIMIT ?` and returning the rows. Callers map each row through the existing `rowToSubmission` (asr-igs.2). Do not add a new mapping path — reuse SubmissionRow + rowToSubmission.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.test.ts: after runMigrations + insertSubmission of two submissions with submitted_by='userA' and one with 'userB', listSubmissionsBySubmitter(db,'userA') returns exactly the two userA rows newest-first and never the userB row; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not change the 0001 schema (asr-7jd.3 owns submissions/submitted_by). No HTTP and no MCP wiring here (that is asr-3po.2). No cross-principal access — this query is always filtered by submitted_by.
close_reason: Implemented: added listSubmissionsBySubmitter to submissions repository with ORDER BY submitted_at DESC + LIMIT; vitest covers own-only filtering, newest-first ordering, limit, and empty result

title: Refuse installing a yanked version
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands ("install ... refuses yanked"); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload (X-ASR-Yanked)

Context:
A yanked version is typically pulled for a CVE or policy violation, so a fresh install of it must be blocked (existing installs may still re-verify, but a new install must abort). resolveDownload (K8) already surfaces the yanked flag; this enforces refusal in the orchestrator.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/install.ts (enforce refusal)
- packages/cli/src/install.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In installSkill, after resolving the target version: if the chosen version is yanked (detail.versions entry .yanked OR resolveDownload().yanked), throw/exit before downloading with a clear message that includes the version and yankReason. No --force escape hatch in this task.

Acceptance:
vitest (install.test.ts): installSkill('owner/skill@1.1.0') where 1.1.0 is yanked rejects (non-zero) with a message naming the yanked version, and no files are written and no lock entry is created.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add a --force override (a future task if needed). Do not block re-verification/download of an already-installed pinned version used by update.
acceptance_criteria: installing a yanked version aborts non-zero with no files or lock entry
close_reason: Implemented: installSkill now refuses yanked versions in two places — fail-fast on versionEntry.yanked from detail.versions, and again after resolveDownload reports yanked (defense-in-depth for stale detail). Both checks throw before download/extract/lockfile, so no files or lock entry are created. yankReason is included in the error message when present. Replaced the old 'does not refuse here' test with 3 new tests covering both refusal paths and the lock-entry-not-created invariant.

title: Add install orchestrator and asr install command
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets; specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr install <owner/skill>[@version], --global, --agent)

Context:
This is the end-to-end install: pick the version, resolve the package URL, verify the hash, unpack into each detected agent dir, and record the lock entry. It composes the K5/K6/K7/K8/K9 building blocks behind one command.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/install.ts (new — orchestration)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (register the `install <slug>` command, segregated from legacy `add`)
- packages/cli/src/install.test.ts (new)

Change:
install.ts: `installSkill(slug, opts: { version?; global?; agent?: 'claude'|'codex'|'both' })`: parse owner/name(@version); getSkillDetail -> choose requested version or detail.latestVersion; resolveDownload(owner,name,version) -> { url, yanked }; downloadAndVerify(url, versionContentHash); for each detectAgents(opts) target extractZip into agentSkillDir(agent,name,{global}); recordInstall(...{ contentHash, sourceUrl: url }). index.ts: add commander `install <slug>` with `--global`, `--agent <claude|codex|both>`, and `@version` parsed from the slug, calling installSkill.

Acceptance:
vitest (install.test.ts) with registry-client, downloadAndVerify, and extractZip stubbed and a temp cwd: installSkill('owner/skill') writes the skill files under the detected scope and records an entry in .agent/asr.lock.json with the resolved version and contentHash.

Risks / out of scope:
Yanked-refusal is K11 — only read the flag here. Update/diff is K14. Do not delete the legacy `add` command in this task (a later cleanup removes it).
acceptance_criteria: installSkill writes files under detected scope and records asr.lock.json entry
close_reason: Implemented installSkill orchestrator (packages/cli/src/install.ts) composing parseSlug -> getSkillDetail -> resolveDownload -> downloadAndVerify -> extractZip per detected agent -> recordInstall with contentHash + sourceUrl. Registered 'install <slug>' commander command in packages/cli/src/index.ts (--global, --agent, --token, @version in slug). 9 vitest cases in install.test.ts cover happy path, @version parsing, opts.version override, latestVersion fallback, missing-version error, invalid-slug rejection, multi-agent install, yanked flag passthrough, and token propagation.

title: Record contentHash and source URL in install lockfile
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets (.agent/asr.lock.json: version, content-hash, install timestamp, source registry URL)

Context:
asr update/list and re-verification need each install's content hash and originating registry URL persisted. Today InstalledSkill (packages/core/src/types.ts:18-25) and recordInstall (packages/cli/src/lockfile.ts:26-50) store only source/version/commit — no contentHash and no explicit registry URL.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts:18-25 (add `contentHash?: string` and `sourceUrl?: string` to InstalledSkill)
- packages/cli/src/lockfile.ts:26-50 (recordInstall: accept and persist contentHash + sourceUrl)
- packages/cli/src/lockfile.test.ts (new)

Change:
Append `contentHash?: string;` and `sourceUrl?: string;` to the InstalledSkill interface (do not reorder existing fields). Widen recordInstall to take a trailing options object `{ contentHash?; sourceUrl? }` and write those into lock.skills[name]. Keep legacy positional callers compiling (new fields optional, additive).

Acceptance:
vitest (lockfile.test.ts) writing to a temp dir: recordInstall(..., { contentHash: 'sha256:abc', sourceUrl: 'https://reg/...' }) produces a .agent/asr.lock.json whose entry has contentHash 'sha256:abc' and that sourceUrl.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not bump LockFile.version (still 1) — fields are additive/optional. Do not break existing recordInstall call sites in index.ts.
acceptance_criteria: recordInstall persists contentHash and sourceUrl into asr.lock.json entry
close_reason: Implemented: added contentHash and sourceUrl to InstalledSkill (types.ts) and widened recordInstall with a trailing options arg (lockfile.ts); added packages/cli/src/lockfile.test.ts covering both the new options arg and legacy positional callers

title: Add resolveDownload to registry client (302 + yank header)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload (302 Location + X-ASR-Yanked)

Context:
Install needs the concrete Forgejo generic-package URL for a version and must know whether that version is yanked. The registry returns this as a 302 whose Location points at the package, plus an X-ASR-Yanked header. This extends the K1 read client with that resolution step.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/registry-client.ts (extend: add resolveDownload)
- packages/cli/src/registry-client.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Export `resolveDownload(owner, name, version, opts?: { token? }): Promise<{ url: string; yanked: boolean }>` issuing GET /api/v1/skills/:owner/:name/v/:version/download with `redirect: 'manual'`; read the `Location` response header as url and `X-ASR-Yanked` === 'true' as yanked; throw if a 3xx has no Location or the status is non-3xx.

Acceptance:
vitest (registry-client.test.ts) with fetch mocked to a 302 carrying Location and `X-ASR-Yanked: true`: resolveDownload('o','n','1.0.0') resolves to { url: '<location>', yanked: true }.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not fetch the artifact body here (K6 owns download+verify). Do not auto-follow the redirect — read Location manually so the package URL is exposed to the caller.
acceptance_criteria: mocked 302 with Location and X-ASR-Yanked true returns url and yanked true
close_reason: Implemented: added resolveDownload to packages/cli/src/registry-client.ts, issuing GET /api/v1/skills/:owner/:name/v/:version/download with redirect:'manual'; returns { url, yanked } from Location + X-ASR-Yanked, throws RegistryError on non-3xx or missing Location. 5 new vitest cases cover the happy path, absent X-ASR-Yanked, missing Location, non-3xx, and URL-encoding/Bearer token.

title: Add yauzl safe zip extraction (extract.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets; project rule "Zip extraction uses yauzl only — never unzipper"

Context:
After hash verification the artifact zip must be unpacked into the agent skill dir. Per the project hard rule the CLI must extract with yauzl and guard against zip-slip path traversal so a crafted entry cannot escape the target directory.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/extract.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/extract.test.ts (new)
- packages/cli/package.json (add deps: yauzl ^3, @types/yauzl ^2)

Change:
Export `extractZip(zip: Buffer, destDir: string): Promise<string[]>`: open with yauzl.fromBuffer (lazyEntries); for each entry resolve join(destDir, entry.fileName), throw on any entry whose resolved path escapes destDir or is absolute or contains `..`; create directories and write files; return the list of written relative paths.

Acceptance:
vitest (extract.test.ts): extractZip of an in-test-built zip (two files) writes both under destDir and returns their paths; a zip containing an entry `../evil.txt` causes the promise to reject with no file written outside destDir.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add unzipper or adm-zip. No symlink-entry support. Do not buffer entire large entries unnecessarily — stream entry read streams to disk.
acceptance_criteria: extracts entries under destDir and rejects a ../ traversal entry
close_reason: Implemented yauzl-based extractZip(Buffer, destDir) with path traversal protection: rejects ../ entries, absolute paths, and yauzl's own validation errors via PathTraversalError. Added yauzl runtime dep and yazl test dep. 3 vitest tests cover safe extraction, ../ rejection, and absolute-path rejection.

title: Add download-and-verify with canonical SHA-256 (download.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets (post-install hash check); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload; specs/versioning.md (canonical content hash)

Context:
A published skill artifact must be verified against the registry's contentHash before extraction, or a tampered/MITM'd zip could install malicious code. This module fetches the artifact and enforces the hash gate, emitting the documented version.hash.mismatch on failure.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/download.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/download.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export `class HashMismatchError extends Error { code = 'version.hash.mismatch' }`. Export `downloadAndVerify(url: string, expectedHash: string, opts?: { token? }): Promise<Buffer>`: fetch(url) following redirects, read arrayBuffer -> Buffer, compute `sha256:` + crypto.createHash('sha256').update(buf).digest('hex'), compare case-insensitively to expectedHash; on mismatch throw HashMismatchError with both hashes in the message; else return the buffer.

Acceptance:
vitest (download.test.ts) with fetch mocked to a known body: downloadAndVerify(url, '<wrong-hash>') rejects with an error whose `.code === 'version.hash.mismatch'`; with the correct `sha256:` hash it resolves to the buffer.

Risks / out of scope:
No unzip here (K7 owns extraction). No yank handling (K8/K10/K11). Match the canonical hash format used server-side (`sha256:<hex>`); do not invent a different prefix.
acceptance_criteria: wrong hash rejects with code version.hash.mismatch; correct hash resolves buffer
close_reason: Implemented: HashMismatchError + downloadAndVerify(url, expectedHash, opts) in packages/cli/src/download.ts with sha256:<hex> canonical format, case-insensitive comparison, redirect:follow, optional bearer token; 8 vitest cases pass in download.test.ts. (Status not persisted in prior session — see bd-bulk-mutation-persistence-quirk memory.)

title: Add agent detection and skill target paths (agents.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets (detection order + path table)

Context:
Install must place skills in the right per-agent directory. The legacy config.ts (packages/cli/src/config.ts:32-53) only knows cursor/claude/project and never targets Codex. The spec defines a Claude/Codex/both model with a precise detection order, which this module owns so install/list/remove share one source of truth.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/agents.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/agents.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export `type AgentTarget = 'claude' | 'codex'`. `detectAgents(opts: { explicit?: 'claude'|'codex'|'both'; cwd?: string }): AgentTarget[]` implementing detection order: explicit flag wins (both -> ['claude','codex']); else presence of .claude/ or .codex/ in cwd selects those; else default ['claude','codex']. `agentSkillDir(agent: AgentTarget, name: string, opts: { global?: boolean }): string` -> project: `<cwd>/.claude/skills/<name>` or `.codex/skills/<name>`; global: `<home>/.claude/skills/<name>` or `<home>/.codex/skills/<name>`.

Acceptance:
vitest (agents.test.ts) using a temp cwd: detectAgents({}) with both .claude and .codex present returns ['claude','codex']; detectAgents({explicit:'codex'}) returns ['codex']; agentSkillDir('claude','x',{global:false}) ends with `.claude/skills/x`.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not delete config.ts getTargetDir yet (legacy add/remove still use it until their migration tasks). No filesystem writes here — path computation + detection only.
acceptance_criteria: detectAgents and agentSkillDir return per-spec detection order and paths
close_reason: Implemented in commit a61ea3a: agents.ts + 13 vitest cases

title: Add registry read client to @asr/cli (search, detail, versions)
description:
Parent story: asr-8ce
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#endpoints (GET /skills, /skills/:owner/:name, /skills/:owner/:name/versions); specs/cli-integration.md#commands

Context:
The browse commands (asr search/info/versions) and the later install command all read the Forgejo-backed Registry API at base /api/v1. Today index.ts has only an inline fetchRegistry() (packages/cli/src/index.ts:28-40) that targets the legacy /api/skills path, not the spec's /api/v1. This introduces a typed, reusable read client so commands stop hand-rolling fetch.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/registry-client.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/registry-client.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export `registryFetch(path, { token? })` resolving the base URL from process.env.ASR_URL || getConfig().registry (config.ts:19), attaching `Authorization: Bearer` only when a token is present and requesting `Accept: application/json`. Build on it: `searchSkills(q, opts?: { tag?; kind?; limit?; cursor? }): Promise<{ items: SkillSummary[]; nextCursor?: string }>` -> GET /api/v1/skills?q=...; `getSkillDetail(owner, name): Promise<SkillDetail>` -> GET /api/v1/skills/:owner/:name; `listVersions(owner, name): Promise<SkillVersion[]>` -> GET /api/v1/skills/:owner/:name/versions. Import SkillSummary/SkillDetail/SkillVersion from '@asr/core' (added by asr-9qp). Throw on non-2xx with the status and response body.

Acceptance:
vitest (registry-client.test.ts) with global.fetch mocked: searchSkills('foo') issues a GET to `${base}/api/v1/skills?q=foo` and returns the parsed `items` array; a 404 from getSkillDetail rejects with an error carrying the status code.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not modify or delete the legacy fetchRegistry()/browse command yet (K2 migrates `search`). No download/redirect logic here (K8 adds it). No write/publish endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: searchSkills(foo) GETs /api/v1/skills?q=foo and returns parsed items array; 404 detail rejects with status
close_reason: Implemented: Added @asr/cli registry-client.ts with registryFetch (Accept:application/json, optional Bearer, ASR_URL||config.registry), searchSkills→/api/v1/skills (q/tag/kind/limit/cursor), getSkillDetail, listVersions. RegistryError carries status+body. 9 vitest tests with mocked fetch verify URLs, headers, params, and 404 rejection.

title: Add vitest test runner to @asr/cli package
description:
Parent story: asr-8ce
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     packages/core/package.json (vitest ^4.0.18 precedent); root package.json#scripts.test

Context:
Every read- and install-side CLI task in the asr-8ce/asr-5wr slice ships a *.test.ts file, but @asr/cli currently has no test runner: packages/cli/package.json has no "test" script and no vitest devDependency (only @asr/core does). This lays the test foundation so all later CLI acceptance checks are actually runnable under `pnpm test`.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/package.json (add "test": "vitest run" to scripts; add "vitest": "^4.0.18" to devDependencies, matching @asr/core)
- packages/cli/vitest.config.ts (new; minimal defineConfig with test.environment 'node')
- package.json (root: change scripts.test from `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` to also run `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test`)

Change:
Add vitest to @asr/cli with a node-environment config and chain the CLI test run into the root `pnpm test` so CI runs both packages. Add packages/cli/src/sanity.test.ts (expect(true).toBe(true)) only if vitest errors on zero test files; otherwise omit it.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` exits 0 (vitest runs, reporting either the sanity test or "no test files"), and `pnpm test` from the repo root invokes both @asr/core and @asr/cli vitest runs.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add coverage thresholds or modify @asr/core's config. Do not introduce jest. Keep ESM (package is "type":"module").
acceptance_criteria: pnpm --filter @asr/cli test exits 0 and root pnpm test runs CLI tests too
close_reason: Implemented: chained @asr/cli vitest run into root pnpm test; CLI test runner already had vitest config, devDep, and 16 passing tests

title: Wire asr publish command with --watch
description:
Parent story: asr-rtz
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (lines 36-37); specs/api.md#post-submissions (lines 62-80)

Context:
asr publish ties validation, classification, packing, and upload together: it fails fast on an invalid manifest before any network call, reports the predicted approval path, uploads, and (with --watch) streams status until terminal.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/commands/publish.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (register publish; the legacy hidden publish at lines 514-572 is replaced)

Change:
Add commands/publish.ts registering asr publish [dir] [--watch]: read manifest.yaml from the dir and validate required fields (name, semver version) locally, exiting non-zero before any HTTP if invalid; run classifySkill and print the predicted path; packSkillDir then postSubmission; print { id, status }. With --watch, poll GET /submissions/:id every few seconds until a terminal phase and print transitions. Register in index.ts.

Acceptance:
vitest against a stub submission API: asr publish ./fixtures/md-skill returns 201 and prints an object containing id and status; an invalid manifest (missing version) exits non-zero with no HTTP request made; --watch on a code skill prints status transitions ending at a terminal phase.

Risks / out of scope:
Local manifest validation is a minimal required-field/semver check; full @asr/core SkillManifest schema validation (asr-4bc) is a later enhancement noted here. Depends on api.ts, pack.ts, classify.ts.
close_reason: Implemented: asr publish [dir] [--watch] in packages/cli/src/commands/publish.ts; reads manifest.yaml, validates name+semver locally (fail-fast no HTTP), prints predicted path via classifySkill, packs+posts, optionally polls /api/v1/submissions/:id until terminal phase. Registered in index.ts; legacy hidden publish removed. 3 vitest tests cover acceptance criteria.

title: Add content classifier (src/publish/classify.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-rtz
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#classification-whitelist-approach (lines 84-104); DESIGN.md#content-classification (lines 196-201)

Context:
Before upload the CLI classifies the skill so the user knows whether it will auto-approve (content-only) or require approval (code-containing). The classifier mirrors the server-side whitelist so the client prediction matches.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/publish/classify.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/classify.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add classify.ts with CONTENT_ONLY_EXTENSIONS = { .md .txt .rst .png .jpg .jpeg .gif .svg .webp .yaml .yml .json } and classifySkill(files: string[]): "md-only" | "code-containing" returning md-only only when every file has a whitelisted extension (files with no/unknown extension force code-containing). Lowercase the extension before lookup.

Acceptance:
vitest: classifySkill(["SKILL.md","img/logo.png"]) === "md-only"; classifySkill(["SKILL.md","scripts/run.py"]) === "code-containing"; a file with no extension classifies as code-containing.

Risks / out of scope:
Whitelist must stay byte-identical to specs/security.md. Do not perform SVG sanitization here (server concern). No HTTP calls.
close_reason: Implemented: added content classifier (packages/cli/src/publish/classify.ts) with byte-identical whitelist mirroring server-side; vitest covers SKILL.md+png md-only, SKILL.md+py code-containing, extensionless code-containing, case-insensitive, unknown ext, and full whitelist.

title: Add skill-directory zip packer (src/publish/pack.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-rtz
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr publish line 36); specs/api.md#post-submissions (multipart file: skill.zip)

Context:
asr publish uploads the skill directory as a zip. The packer turns a local directory into an in-memory zip the API client can upload, deterministically and without native deps.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/publish/pack.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/pack.test.ts (new)
- packages/cli/package.json (add a pure-JS zip dependency, e.g. fflate)

Change:
Add pack.ts exporting packSkillDir(dir): Promise<{ buffer: Buffer; files: string[] }>. Recursively read the directory (skipping node_modules and .git), build a zip with fflate (pure JS, bundles cleanly), and return the zip bytes plus the relative file list. Add fflate to dependencies.

Acceptance:
vitest: packing a fixture directory containing SKILL.md and scripts/run.py returns a non-empty Buffer and a files array listing both relative paths; unzipping the buffer reproduces the file contents.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure-JS only (fflate), no archiver/native zip. Do not classify content here (separate task). Server-side extraction still uses yauzl (out of scope).
close_reason: Implemented: added packSkillDir in packages/cli/src/publish/pack.ts using fflate (pure JS, deterministic), skipping node_modules and .git, with vitest coverage for SKILL.md + scripts/run.py fixture, exclusion, and determinism

title: Add authed submission API client (src/api.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-rtz
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#post-submissions (lines 62-80); specs/cli-integration.md#token-export-for-mcp; specs/api.md#authentication-authorization (lines 139-160)

Context:
The publish, status, and submissions commands all talk to the Submission API and must attach a bearer token (or skip auth in dev mode). Centralizing the HTTP client keeps auth handling and error decoding in one place.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/api.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/api.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add api.ts exporting apiFetch(path, init) that resolves the base URL via env.ts, and unless isAuthDisabled attaches Authorization: Bearer <getValidAccessToken()>; it parses JSON, and on non-2xx throws an error carrying status and the parsed { error } body (e.g. version_already_exists). Add a multipart helper postSubmission(zipBuffer, filename) that POSTs multipart/form-data field file to /submissions and returns the 201 body { id, status, manifest, contentHash, createdAt }.

Acceptance:
vitest against a stub server: postSubmission returns the parsed 201 body and sends an Authorization header when auth is enabled; with ASR_URL=http://localhost:* no Authorization header is sent; a 409 response surfaces an error exposing body.error.

Risks / out of scope:
Depends on env.ts and the session accessor. Do not build the zip here (separate task); accept a Buffer.
close_reason: Implemented packages/cli/src/api.ts with apiFetch() and postSubmission() helpers. apiFetch resolves base URL via env.ts, attaches Bearer token via getValidAccessToken() unless isAuthDisabled, parses JSON, and throws ApiError(status, body) on non-2xx. postSubmission wraps a Buffer in multipart/form-data and POSTs to /api/v1/submissions returning the parsed 201 body. Added vitest suite (7 tests, all passing) covering: Authorization header presence under HTTPS, omission under non-HTTPS localhost, 409 ApiError surface with body.error, missing ASR_URL error, postSubmission multipart shape, and 201 response decoding.

title: Wire asr whoami and asr logout commands
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (lines 19-21), #authentication

Context:
After login users need to confirm who they are and to sign out. whoami reads the cached session and shows identity plus roles; logout clears the cached tokens.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/commands/auth.ts (add registerWhoami, registerLogout)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (register whoami and logout)

Change:
Add asr whoami: read getStoredTokens; if none (or dev-mode), print "Not signed in"; else decode and print identity + roles. Add asr logout: call clearTokens and print "Signed out". Register both in index.ts.

Acceptance:
vitest: after a stubbed login that stored tokens, whoami prints the cached identity and roles; running logout then whoami prints "Not signed in" (tokens cleared).

Risks / out of scope:
Depends on the token store; do not re-implement persistence. No network calls needed for whoami/logout.
close_reason: Implemented: wired whoami/logout commands with token-store-backed identity display and logout clearing

title: Wire asr login command
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#authentication (lines 50-57); DESIGN.md#cli-authentication (lines 214-221)

Context:
asr login is the user-facing entry point that ties the device-code flow, token storage, and dev-mode skip together and reports the signed-in identity and roles. This replaces the legacy GitHub token command flow.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/commands/auth.ts (new: registerLogin)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (register the login command; remove the legacy token GitHub path lines 607-710 in a later task)

Change:
Add commands/auth.ts exporting a function that registers asr login on the program: resolve base URL via env.ts; if isAuthDisabled, print that auth is skipped in dev mode and exit 0; otherwise call requestDeviceCode, print the verification URL and user code, call pollForToken, persist via storeTokens, decode identity+roles from the access token claims, and print "Logged in as <user> (roles: ...)". Wire registerLogin into index.ts.

Acceptance:
vitest: against a mock device-code + token endpoint, invoking the login action completes and writes tokens via the store and the printed output contains "Logged in as" with the stub identity and roles; with ASR_URL=http://localhost:9999 the action prints the dev-mode skip message and stores nothing.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not print the raw access token. Removal of the legacy token/config GitHub commands is tracked separately; only add login here.
close_reason: Implemented: wired asr login device-code flow, token storage, identity output, and dev-mode skip tests

title: Implement device-code flow client (RFC 8628)
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#device-code-flow-rfc-8628 (lines 46-59)

Context:
asr login authenticates via the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant against Entra ID. This task implements the protocol mechanics (request a device code, poll the token endpoint) independent of the command UX, so it can be unit-tested against a stub token server.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/auth/device-code.ts (new)
