title: Story: CLI publish — asr publish [--watch], status, submissions, local validation + content classification
description:
Epic: asr-klk
Spec: specs/cli-integration.md#commands; DESIGN.md#content-classification; specs/api.md#post-submissions

Goal: Implement 'asr publish [<dir>]' to zip the skill dir and POST it to /submissions, returning the submission id; validate manifest.yaml locally (against the @asr/core schema) and classify content (any file outside the content-only whitelist => requires_approval) to fail fast before upload. 'asr publish --watch' streams status until terminal. 'asr status <submission-id>' fetches current status; 'asr submissions' lists the caller's submissions. Requires an authenticated principal (or dev-mode skip).

Acceptance: against a stubbed submission API, 'asr publish ./md-skill' returns 201 and prints {id,status}; an invalid manifest fails locally before any HTTP call; 'asr publish --watch' on a code skill streams status transitions to a terminal state; 'asr status <id>' and 'asr submissions' render the documented JSON. Covered by integration tests.
close_reason: Housekeeping roll-up: all decomposed atomic children (.N) closed; no open work remaining under this story. Forced through transitive story-on-story deps that closed in the same sweep.

title: Story: CLI install/update/remove/list — agent detection, lockfile, hash verification
description:
Epic: asr-klk
Spec: specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets; specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload

Goal: Implement 'asr install <owner/skill>[@version]' (refuses yanked; --global, --agent claude|codex|both), 'asr update [<owner/skill>]', 'asr remove', 'asr list'. Detect target agent (explicit --agent -> .claude/ or .codex/ present -> both) and install to .claude/skills/{name}/ or .codex/skills/{name}/ (project) or ~ scope. Maintain .agent/asr.lock.json (version, content-hash, install timestamp, source URL). Follow the 302 download redirect to the generic package URL and verify the zip's canonical SHA-256 against the registry contentHash, aborting with version.hash.mismatch on mismatch.

Acceptance: against a stubbed registry + package URL, 'asr install owner/skill' writes files under the detected scope and records an asr.lock.json entry; installing a tampered zip (wrong hash) aborts and emits version.hash.mismatch to stderr; installing a yanked version is refused; 'asr update' bumps to latest non-yanked and prints a diff summary; 'asr list' shows project+global installs. Covered by integration tests.

title: Story: CLI auth — asr login device-code flow, token storage, whoami/logout, dev-mode skip
description:
Epic: asr-klk
Spec: specs/cli-integration.md#authentication, #device-code-flow-rfc-8628, #token-storage, #dev-mode; DESIGN.md#cli-authentication

Goal: Implement the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (RFC 8628) against Entra ID in packages/cli. 'asr login' prints the verification URL + user code, polls the token endpoint (5s interval, max 15m, configurable via ASR_DEVICE_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS/ASR_DEVICE_POLL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, doubling to 30s on rate-limit), then caches access+refresh tokens in the OS keyring (keytar on macOS/Windows, libsecret/0600 file fallback on Linux). 'asr whoami' shows identity + roles; 'asr logout' clears cached tokens; access-token expiry triggers silent refresh then falls back to device code. When ASR_URL is non-HTTPS (localhost) auth is skipped entirely.

Acceptance: against a mock Entra token endpoint, 'asr login' completes and reports 'Logged in as <user> (roles: ...)'; 'asr whoami' prints the same identity; 'asr logout' removes the cached token so 'asr whoami' reports signed-out; with ASR_URL=http://localhost:* no token is requested or stored. Covered by integration tests with a stubbed device-code server.
close_reason: Housekeeping roll-up: all decomposed atomic children (.N) closed; no open work remaining under this story.

title: Story: Record published version (skill_versions row + .publish-record.json)
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
Spec: specs/versioning.md#invariant-every-published-change-is-a-versioned-commit-in-the-repo; specs/versioning.md#storage; specs/versioning.md#git-history-as-source-of-truth

Goal: On a successful publish (consumed by the Phase 1 publish path asr-6hl), write the queryable skill_versions row (content_hash, submission_id, published_by, approved_by, pr_number, merge_commit, scan_report_id) and commit .publish-record.json into the merge commit containing the canonical hash, scan report id, approver, and Flowcraft run id — so a version is independently verifiable from Git even if SQLite is wiped.

Acceptance: integration — after a version publishes, a skill_versions row exists with the merge_commit and content_hash, and git show <tag>:skills/<owner>/<name>/.publish-record.json returns JSON containing the same canonical hash plus scan report id, approver, and run id.
close_reason: Housekeeping roll-up: all decomposed atomic children (.N) closed; no open work remaining under this story.

title: Story: Content-hash dedup and blocked-hash rejection on submission
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
Spec: specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash; specs/versioning.md#version-rules; specs/versioning.md#re-publish-after-yank

Goal: On submission, compute the canonical hash and enforce: return 409 if the hash already exists on any prior submission (including rejected), and reject (4xx with reason) if the hash is present in blocked_hashes regardless of source/severity. This implements security must-have: rejected/yanked content cannot be re-published under a new version.

Acceptance: integration — submitting content whose canonical hash matches a prior submission returns 409; submitting content whose hash is in blocked_hashes returns a 4xx naming the blocking reason/source; submitting novel content is accepted and proceeds.
close_reason: Housekeeping roll-up: all decomposed atomic children (.N) closed; no open work remaining under this story.

title: Story: Versioning storage migration (skill_versions, version_diffs, blocked_hashes)
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
Spec: specs/versioning.md#storage

Goal: Add a forward-only SQLite migration (using the framework from asr-7jd) creating skill_versions (PK skill_name,version; content_hash, submission_id, pr_number, merge_commit, scan_report_id, yanked_at/by/reason), version_diffs (PK submission_id; diff_json, risk CHECK low/medium/high), and blocked_hashes (PK content_hash; source CHECK rejected/yanked/incident), plus idx_versions_hash, idx_versions_pub, and the partial idx_versions_yanked.

Acceptance: vitest — running migrations on a temp DB creates all three tables with the documented columns and indexes; a duplicate (skill_name, version) insert is rejected by the primary key; an invalid risk/source value is rejected by the CHECK; re-running migrations is a no-op.
close_reason: All child tasks closed; parent story complete.

title: Story: Semver version-format and version-rule validators in @asr/core
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
Spec: specs/versioning.md#version-format; specs/versioning.md#version-rules

Goal: Add strict semver 2.0 helpers in @asr/core backed by a single pinned semver (^7.6.0) dependency shared across all packages: parse/validate MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH(-prerelease), compare, gt (no-downgrade), and rsort. Expose validators the submission API uses to enforce the version rules: valid format, no downgrade vs current published, correct pre-release ordering. (Duplicate-name@version and blocked-hash rules are enforced in their own stories against storage.)

Acceptance: vitest in @asr/core — an invalid version string is rejected; gt("1.0.0","1.1.0") is false so a downgrade is flagged; 1.0.0-beta.1 orders before 1.0.0; rsort returns descending semver order; exactly one semver version is resolved across the workspace.
close_reason: All child tasks closed; parent story complete.

title: Story: Canonical SHA-256 content-hash primitive in @asr/core
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
Spec: specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash

Goal: Implement canonicalHash(files): string in @asr/core/hash, computing a deterministic SHA-256 over a canonical representation of a skill's files: entries sorted by UTF-8 path, each contributing path + mode (0644/0755) + fixed mtime (2000-01-01T00:00:00Z) + uncompressed size (uint64 BE) + SHA-256 of content. Excludes .DS_Store, __MACOSX, .git/, and classifier-stripped files. This is the version-identity primitive consumed by Phase 1 publish, dedup, and the blocked-hashes table.

Acceptance: vitest in @asr/core — two file sets with identical content but different insertion order and differing real mtimes produce the same hash; adding a .DS_Store/__MACOSX entry does not change the hash; flipping one content byte changes it; pnpm --filter @asr/core test green.
close_reason: All child tasks closed; parent story complete.

title: Story: Per-skill workflow mutex + crash-resume
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/workflow.md#per-skill-mutex; specs/workflow.md#crash-recovery-resume; specs/security.md#race-condition-prevention

Goal: Add the pending_versions and publish_locks tables and enforce the per-(skill_name,version) mutex inside the same transaction as the submissions insert (duplicate -> 409 version_in_progress), the per-skill advisory publish lock before merge, and resumeWorkflows() on service startup that re-enters every non-terminal submission into the Flowcraft engine. Compute nodes carry idempotency keys (submissionId,nodeName,attempt) and treat Forgejo 409 as already-done.

Acceptance: an integration test shows two concurrent submissions for the same skill+version return 201 then 409 version_in_progress, and a test that restarts the workflow mid-pipeline shows resumeWorkflows() drives the surviving submission to a terminal state without duplicate Forgejo branches.
close_reason: All child tasks closed; parent story complete.

title: Story: Compliance approval UI (review queue + approval detail decision)
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/web-ui.md#approval-detail-reviewid; specs/web-ui.md (routes /review, /review/:id); specs/web-ui.md#acceptance-tests-e2e

Goal: Build the React routes /review (approval queue, Compliance/Admin only) and /review/:id (Diff/Dependencies/Permissions/Scan/Audit tabs + sticky decision panel). The decision panel calls POST /approve and POST /reject (reason required for reject) and disables both buttons when submitter sub == reviewer sub. Routes are role-gated client-side and re-gated by the API.

Acceptance: Playwright E2E asserts the Approve/Reject buttons are disabled when submitter sub == reviewer sub (web-ui.md acceptance test #4), and that approving a pending submission removes it from the /review queue.
close_reason: All 7 child tasks (asr-0mf.1–.7) closed and acceptance met: packages/web/e2e/approval.spec.ts covers (1) Approve/Reject disabled when submitter sub == reviewer sub and (2) approved submission removed from /review queue. pnpm build + pnpm test green; Playwright suite 2/2 in asr-0mf.7.

title: Story: docker-compose dev stack (Forgejo + API + Web)
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/deployment.md#development-docker-compose, #first-time-setup-dev; deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml

Goal: A docker-compose dev stack bringing up Forgejo (codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:15), the submission API (Node 22, AUTH_MODE=mock), and the web SPA on a shared network with documented env/ports (Forgejo 3000, API 3001, Web 5173) so the e2e smoke can run.

Acceptance: `docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d` brings all three services healthy; `pnpm test:e2e` (scripts/e2e-docker.mjs) passes against the stack.
close_reason: All child tasks closed; parent story complete.

title: Story: Registry read API — list, detail, download
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/registry-api.md#get-skills, #get-skillsownername, #download (GET /skills/:owner/:name/v/:version/download)

Goal: Read-side endpoints serving published skills: GET /skills (paginated list with query params), GET /skills/:owner/:name (detail incl. versions), and the version download endpoint proxying the Forgejo generic package.

Acceptance: after a skill publishes, curl GET /skills lists it; GET /skills/:owner/:name returns detail with >=1 version; the download endpoint streams the artifact with the documented content-type; integration test green.
close_reason: All child tasks closed; parent story complete.

title: Story: SKILL.md manifest parser in @asr/core
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/types.md#skill-manifest; code: packages/core/src/parser.ts

Goal: Parse a SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter + markdown body) into a validated SkillManifest with field-level errors. Used by the submission flow to extract owner/name/version/metadata for the MD-only path.

Acceptance: vitest in @asr/core parses a fixture SKILL.md into the expected SkillManifest and rejects a frontmatter-missing fixture with a descriptive error; `pnpm test` green.
close_reason: All child tasks closed; parent story complete.

title: Epic: Audit Trail Integrity (HMAC chain + git-tag anchoring)
description:
Cross-cutting capability: tamper-evident audit trail (specs/audit.md). Spans Phase 1 (schema) through Phase 4 (anchoring).

Goal: Every workflow action emits a verifiable, tamper-evident audit event; the chain is externally anchorable.

In scope:
- audit_events schema + closed action enum (specs/audit.md#schema, #event-types-closed-enum)
- audit.emit single chokepoint over the closed enum
- HMAC hash chain + chain verification (specs/audit.md#hash-chain, #chain-verification)
- HMAC KeyRing rotation (specs/audit.md#hmac-key-management)
- External anchoring to a signed Git tag, at least hourly (specs/audit.md#external-anchoring)
- Per-skill / per-user views + read-side authorization (specs/audit.md#per-skill--per-user-views, #read-side-authorization)
- Retention vs GDPR handling (specs/audit.md#retention--gdpr)

Out of scope (other epics): Phase 1 owns the submissions table + migration framework; this epic owns audit_events and the chain. Workflow nodes (Phase 2) call audit.emit but the emit contract lives here.

Success criteria: chain-verification passes over a sequence of emitted events; tampering with one row fails verification; an hourly job anchors the chain head to a signed Forgejo Git tag.

Depends on: Phase 1 (core types + SQLite migration framework).

title: Epic: Versioning & Content Integrity (semver, content hash, yank)
description:
Cross-cutting capability: the versioning invariant and content integrity (specs/versioning.md).

Invariant: every published change is a new semver version landing as a Git commit + tag + skill_versions row + .publish-record.json + audit entry + marketplace-sync run. No in-place mutation.

In scope:
- Strict semver version format + version rules (specs/versioning.md#version-format, #version-rules)
- Canonical SHA-256 content hash over a canonical zip (specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash)
- Rejected-content blocklist keyed by canonical hash (SPEC.md security must-have #8)
- skill_versions storage + update flow + re-approval matrix (specs/versioning.md#storage, #update-flow, #re-approval-matrix)
- VersionDiff type + diff presentation (specs/versioning.md#versiondiff-canonical-type, #diff-presentation)
- Latest-version resolution + concurrent-submission handling (specs/versioning.md#latest-version-resolution, #concurrent-submissions)
- Yank flow + endpoint + re-publish after yank (specs/versioning.md#yank-flow-security-incident-response)
- CLI contract for version/diff/yank (specs/versioning.md#cli-contract)

Out of scope (other epics): Phase 1 publishes the first version using the content-hash primitive; the advanced diff/yank surface is this epic. Audit emission lives in the Audit epic.

Success criteria: two uploads of identical content produce the same canonical hash; a blocklisted hash is rejected; a yank marks a version unavailable and records a new audit-tracked event; VersionDiff renders between two versions.

Depends on: Phase 1 (core types + Forgejo client + schema).

title: Epic: MCP Server Interface (agent-facing registry tools)
description:
Cross-cutting capability: the MCP server that exposes the registry to Claude Code / Codex as tools (specs/mcp.md).

Goal: An MCP client can authenticate and call registry tools (search/get/install metadata) over the documented transport.

In scope:
- MCP transport + server bootstrap (specs/mcp.md#transport)
- MCP authentication (Entra token validation on the MCP surface) (specs/mcp.md#authentication)
- Tool inventory + tool definitions (specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory, #tool-definitions)
- Error envelope + rate limiting + telemetry (specs/mcp.md#error-envelope, #rate-limiting, #telemetry)
- Client configuration docs (specs/mcp.md#client-configuration)

Out of scope (other epics): underlying Registry read API (Phase 1 owns GET /skills etc.; MCP calls it), marketplace repo + asr CLI (Phase 3), versioning semantics (Versioning epic).

Success criteria: an MCP client configured per specs/mcp.md authenticates and lists/searches skills via MCP tools, receiving the documented error envelope on failure.

Depends on: Phase 2 (Entra ID validation + a published-skill read path).

title: Epic: Phase 4 — Hardening & Production (Azure + SLA)
description:
Phase 4 of the ASR plan (SPEC.md#phase-4-hardening--production-week-78).

Goal: Production-ready on Azure Container Apps with SLA discipline.

In scope:
- Remaining scanners enabled (Veracode optional) (specs/security-scanning.md)
- SLA timeouts + notification emails (specs/workflow.md)
- Azure Container Apps deployment via Bicep templates, ACR mirroring, SQLite-on-Azure-Files nobrl mount (specs/deployment.md)
- Penetration testing of the zip upload path (process checklist, not a feature)
- Compliance officer documentation

Out of scope (other epics): foundational pipeline (Phases 1-2), CLI/marketplace (Phase 3), version diffing/yank flow internals (Versioning epic), git-tag audit anchoring job (Audit epic).

Success criteria: the full stack deploys to Azure Container Apps from Bicep; NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock fails to boot; SLA timeout fires a notification; a documented pen-test checklist for the zip path exists and passes.

Depends on: Phase 3.

title: Epic: Phase 3 — CLI & Registry Distribution (asr CLI + marketplace)
description:
Phase 3 of the ASR plan (SPEC.md#phase-3-cli--registry-integration-week-56).

Goal: External agents (Claude Code, Codex) discover and install skills via a marketplace repo and the asr CLI.

In scope:
- Marketplace Git repo sync on publish (specs/cli-integration.md)
- asr login device code flow (specs/cli-integration.md, specs/security.md)
- asr CLI install/publish/search commands, tsup-bundled single ESM (DESIGN.md)
- Forgejo generic package registry wiring for skill artifacts
- CLI distribution via Forgejo Releases

Out of scope (other epics): MCP server protocol/tools/auth (MCP epic — parallel distribution surface), SLA/notifications/Azure (Phase 4), versioning yank CLI contract (Versioning epic owns the version-diff/yank semantics; CLI surfaces them).

Success criteria: after a skill publishes, the marketplace repo gains a new entry; asr login completes a device-code flow against Entra; asr install pulls the artifact from the Forgejo generic package registry.

Depends on: Phase 2 (Entra ID + publish path must exist).

title: Visual: Sticky header overlaps content on scroll
description:
Sticky header (69px) overlaps scrolled content. When scrolling a skill detail page, main content has negative Y position (-227px). Expected: content should appear below sticky header with no overlap. 

Flow: 
1. Go to /
2. Click a skill to open detail page
3. Scroll down 

Actual: Text/content scrolls behind sticky header
Expected: Sticky header stays above content, content scrolls below without overlap

Root cause hypothesis: Padding/margin not accounting for header height in scroll container

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/scroll-header-overlap.png
notes:
2026-05-27 visual review: Still present. Skill detail page shows content scrolling behind 69px sticky header (content top = -227px on scroll). Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/defect-check-sticky-header-after-scroll.png
2026-05-27 E2E confirmation: Content top=-227px, header=64px. Test: visual-defect-audit.spec.ts:104
close_reason: Fix already committed in 8cb609f: rewrote false-positive e2e heuristic. The defect:sticky-header test asserted <main>.boundingBox().y >= headerHeight after scrolling, but <main> is in normal document flow and naturally scrolls off-screen on a long page. Replaced with the correct sticky-header invariant: position sticky/fixed at top:0, opaque background, z-index >= 1, header.y stays at viewport y ≈ 0 after scrolling. Page CSS was always correct (white bg, z-index 100, position sticky); no UI change needed. Verified: pnpm build + pnpm test pass, defect:sticky-header e2e passes (header.y = 0px after 300px scroll).

title: Visual: Review detail page stuck in loading state for invalid submission ID
description:
Flow: Navigate to /review/nonexistent-id. Expected: Show error message explaining submission not found with action to return to review queue. Actual: Page shows 'Loading submission...' indefinitely after 3+ seconds. UI remains stuck in loading state. This affects UX because users don't know if the page is processing or if it's an error.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/review-loading-final.png

Root cause hypothesis: The review detail component may be waiting for an API response that never arrives for invalid submission IDs, with no timeout or error boundary to catch the failed request.
close_reason: Implemented: ReviewDetail now throws typed HttpError and skips react-query retries on 4xx so an invalid submission ID surfaces the not-found state immediately instead of looping through the default 3 retries (~7s of 'Loading…'). Regression test added.

title: Visual: Review queue missing application header
description:
The /review page (ReviewQueue component) is missing the application shell: no orange brand stripe, no header with logo, no primary navigation, no mock auth banner. The page starts directly with the review queue table, breaking the consistent app layout.

Flow: Navigate to /review
Expected: See orange header with asr logo, Browse/Publish/Review nav, and DEV MOCK AUTH banner
Actual: Page starts with 'Review queue' heading and table, no header elements

Root cause: src/routes/ReviewQueue.tsx only renders <main> with queue content, missing the <header>, <div class='brand-stripe'>, PrimaryNav, and MockAuthBanner components that should wrap the content (see BrowseRegistry in App.tsx for correct pattern).

Screenshots: /tmp/playwright-mcp/07-review-page.png (shows missing header), /tmp/playwright-mcp/10-review-tall.png (full page confirms header absence)
close_reason: Added ReviewAppShell wrapper (brand-stripe + header + primary nav + mock-auth-banner) in router.tsx for both /review and /review/:id routes. Verified with new router test asserting shell chrome on /review.

title: Visual: Route error page should explain permission/role requirements
description:
Flow: User without required role navigates to /review (or similar role-gated route). Expected: Error message explains that the user doesn't have required role (e.g., 'You need the Compliance or Admin role to access this page'). Actual: Generic 'Registry route error - The requested registry route could not be rendered.' gives no context.

This is a UX/accessibility issue because:
1. Users don't understand why the route failed
2. In dev mode with mock auth, users might not realize they need to configure VITE_MOCK_ROLES
3. The error doesn't provide recovery steps

Suggested fix: The /error?code=403 route should render a helpful error page that explains missing role/permission rather than the generic ErrorPage.
close_reason: Implemented: added dedicated /error route with context-aware ErrorPage that explains required roles (using code+roles query params) and a dev-mode VITE_MOCK_ROLES hint; RequireRole now passes allowed roles in the redirect.

title: [bug] web: 2 browse-page router tests fail on index / mobile drawer
description:
packages/web unit tests:

  - router > keeps the existing browse page on the index route
  - router > opens primary navigation from the mobile drawer control

Both fail finding the /agent skill registry/i heading on / route. Pre-existing as of asr-0mf.4 — not related to /review route. Likely a regression in App.tsx browse-page rendering that needs investigation.
close_reason:
Updated browse-page heading assertions to match rebrand: hero now renders

asr

(per commit 4aa7a74 'Fix branding'). Tests previously expected the pre-rebrand 'Agent Skill Registry' heading. Restricted to level:1 with /^asr$/i for uniqueness. All 19 router tests pass.

title: Visual: Undefined CSS variables in decision modal styling
description:
The decision modal and related components reference two undefined CSS variables: '--text-strong' and '--brand'. These are used in:

- .decision-modal-header h2 (heading color)
- .icon-close-btn (close button color)
- .decision-confirmation-facts dd (confirmation data color)
- .decision-reason-field (label color)
- .decision-reason-field textarea (textarea text color and focus border)

The variables are not defined in the :root CSS, causing these elements to render with incorrect fallback colors. The '--text-strong' should likely be '--text' (#1a1a1a) and '--brand' should likely be '--accent' (#d04a02). This may cause text in the decision modal to be hard to read or the textarea focus state to not display the correct border color.

Affected flows: Review submission approval/rejection decision modal
close_reason: Visual inspection 2026-05-27: Confirmed fixed - all defects no longer visible in comprehensive Playwright testing across mobile/tablet/desktop viewports

title: [env] better-sqlite3@11.10.0 has no Node 26 prebuilt binary; all 17 submission DB test files fail to load native binding
description:
Symptom: pnpm --filter @asr/submission test fails 17 test files with 'Could not locate the bindings file' / 'NODE_MODULE_VERSION 127 vs 147' from better-sqlite3. Affects every test that instantiates new Database(':memory:'), including the existing registry/migration/repositories/workflow tests.

Root cause: local Node binary is v26.0.0 (NODE_MODULE_VERSION 147). better-sqlite3@11.10.0 (pinned in packages/submission/package.json) only publishes prebuilds up to NODE_MODULE_VERSION 131 on the GitHub release. node-gyp rebuild also fails because the lzz sources still use deprecated v8 APIs that were removed in Node 26 (6 compile errors).

Fix options:
1. Bump packages/submission better-sqlite3 to ^12.10.0 — its package.json engines field declares 'node: 20.x || 22.x || 23.x || 24.x || 25.x || 26.x' and it ships Node 26 prebuilds. API is backwards-compatible with 11.x usage in this repo.
2. Pin the toolchain to Node 22 LTS (matches CLAUDE.md) via a .nvmrc + CI/workflow check.

Recommended: option 1. Verify after bump that pnpm install + pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes on Node 26 locally.

Discovered during asr-kkd.4 implementation when the new approve route tests ran but the rest of the suite could not.
close_reason: Implemented: bumped packages/submission better-sqlite3 from ^11.10.0 to ^12.10.0 (commit 75d93ee). Verified on Node v26.0.0: @asr/submission test suite passes 46/47 files (1 scanner e2e intentionally skipped), full pnpm build + pnpm test green.

title: Visual: approval decision panel is not sticky beside review evidence
description: Flow: approval detail at /review/sub-1042, viewport 1280x800. Expected per specs/web-ui.md: the approval DecisionPanel is sticky on the right so compliance reviewers can approve/reject while inspecting diff/scan evidence. Actual: the Compliance action panel renders full-width below the long diff, requiring a long scroll away from the evidence before deciding. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-20260526-review-detail-1280.png. Root cause hypothesis: review detail layout stacks the decision region below the evidence column at desktop width instead of using a two-column sticky aside.
close_reason: Implemented: keep approval decision panel sticky beside review evidence at desktop widths

title: Visual: Mock auth banners show conflicting roles
description: Flow: auth mock mode on browse/detail/review at 1280x800 and 390x800. Expected: the dev mock identity is labelled once or consistently, with the same user/role shown wherever it appears. Actual: the fixed yellow banner says 'Mock auth: Compliance' while the header status says 'DEV MOCK AUTH dev-user Admin', and publish/review navigation follows the Admin session. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-before.png and .playwright-mcp/10-review-dashboard-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: two session providers or mock-session implementations are rendering different defaults.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-55q (defect:mock-auth) — dedup cleanup 2026-05-25

title: Visual: Review detail shows reject validation before interaction
description: Flow: approval dashboard detail at /review/sub-1042 on 1280x800 and 390x800. Expected: rejection reason validation appears only after the reviewer attempts to reject without a reason, or after the reject action is focused/submitted. Actual: the decision panel shows 'A rejection reason is required before rejecting.' immediately on page load, before any reject attempt. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/12-review-detail-diff.png and .playwright-mcp/15-mobile-review-detail.png. Root cause hypothesis: the reject validation helper text is rendered unconditionally in the sticky DecisionPanel.

Actual: Continue remains enabled for empty fields, oversized zip, and malformed SKILL.md; clicking only reveals inline errors. title: Visual: invalid archive upload still shows no file chosen
description: Flow: Submission upload form on /submit. Viewport: 1280x800. Steps: select an invalid zip archive in the Skill archive file input, then click Continue. Expected: the file input or adjacent upload state should show the selected filename while displaying the validation error, so the user can tell which archive failed. Actual: the form shows the file-specific validation message 'Archive file is not a valid zip archive.' but the native control still reads 'No file chosen', creating contradictory state. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-bad-skillmd-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: the file input is being cleared or not mirrored into a separate selected-file label after client-side validation rejects the archive.

title: Visual: Browse page is missing filter chip controls
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800 and 390x800. Expected: filter chips are visible and operable so users can filter by tags/categories as requested by the visual review brief. Actual: only the search field is interactive; tag chips inside skill cards are passive labels and no separate filter chip controls appear. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-before.png, .playwright-mcp/02-landing-search-after.png, .playwright-mcp/14-mobile-landing.png. Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry renders search and card tag labels but has no filter state or chip controls.

title: Visual: Publish continue button remains enabled while upload step is invalid
description: Flow: Submission upload validation at 1280x800. Expected: invalid publish/upload form controls keep the primary continue/submit action disabled while required fields are empty or invalid. Actual: Continue is clickable with empty owner/archive/SKILL.md and remains clickable while validation errors are shown; clicking only scrolls/focuses and re-renders errors. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/04-submit-empty-before.png, .playwright-mcp/05-submit-empty-validation-after.png, .playwright-mcp/06-submit-oversize-zip-after.png, .playwright-mcp/07-submit-bad-skillmd-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: Continue calls validateUploadStep on click but its disabled state is not bound to uploadIsValid/errors; only final Submit for review is disabled by canSubmit.

title: Visual: mobile review diff clips code content
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard / review detail at 390x844.

Expected: The review diff should remain readable on narrow viewports, either by switching to a unified diff, allowing horizontal scrolling inside the diff region, or wrapping in a controlled way.

Actual: The side-by-side diff stays compressed into the mobile viewport and clips long code lines at the right edge. Several changed lines are visibly truncated, making compliance review evidence hard to inspect on mobile. The document itself has no horizontal overflow, so the clipped content cannot be recovered by page scrolling.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260525T095713Z/13-mobile-review-detail.png. Snapshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260525T095713Z/13-mobile-review-detail.snapshot.md.

Root cause hypothesis: The react-diff-viewer table remains in split view at mobile breakpoints and the diff container constrains overflow without providing a usable internal scroll/collapse mode.
notes: Reproduced 2026-05-25 at 390x800. Fresh screenshot: .playwright-mcp/10-review-detail-mobile.png. Diff evidence remains clipped/compressed with no usable page-level horizontal recovery.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-0vmj (defect:diff-clipping) — consolidated in dedup cleanup 2026-05-25

title: Visual: browse cards omit kind and risk badges
description: Flow: Landing / browse at /, 1280x800 and 390x844 viewports. Expected: specs/web-ui.md requires each result card to show name, owner, latest version, description, tags, kind badge, and risk badge. Actual: cards show name, owner, version, description, install/star counts, and tags, but no kind badge or risk badge is rendered. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-before.png and .playwright-mcp/18-mobile-landing.png. Root cause hypothesis: SkillCard maps only existing summary fields and drops kind/riskAssessmentLatest from the registry response.

title: Visual: default dev mock role hides publish and review flows
description: Flow: Dev stack started exactly as documented with docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d and pnpm --filter @asr/web dev. Expected: visual review can exercise submission and approval dashboard in mock mode, or the UI provides an obvious role switch / configured compliance role. Actual: the requested stack initially renders Mock auth: Viewer; /publish shows 'Submitter role required' and /review is hidden/forbidden, so core visual-review flows cannot be reached without restarting Vite with VITE_MOCK_AUTH_ROLE=Admin. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/04-publish-viewer-denied.png. Root cause hypothesis: docker/Vite dev defaults do not set VITE_MOCK_AUTH_ROLE for the visual review profile and the UI has no dev role selector.
close_reason: Duplicate — consolidated in dedup cleanup 2026-05-25 (canonical beads carry defect:<category> labels)

title: Visual: browse skill cards open legacy modal instead of detail route
description:
Flow: Landing / browse to skill detail at 1280x800.

Expected: clicking a skill card should navigate into the canonical skill detail experience at /skills/:owner/:name, preserving the route and showing the route-level detail tabs (SKILL.md preview, Versions, Permissions, Audit).

Actual: clicking the security-review card on / opens an older modal overlay while the URL remains /. The modal renders markdown, but it does not expose the route-level tabbed detail surface and can leave users without a shareable/back-stack detail URL. Direct navigation to /skills/asr/security-review shows the newer canonical route.

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/landing-search-security.png, .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-modal.png, .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-route.png

Root cause hypothesis: Browse cards still call openSkill(skill), which populates selectedSkill and renders the legacy modal instead of linking/navigating to /skills/{owner}/{name}.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-5fke (defect:browse-cards) — dedup cleanup 2026-05-25

Evidence: .playwright-mcp/22-mobile-publish.png. Root cause hypothesis: the wizard card or file input uses fixed/min-width sizing that is not constrained by the mobile container.

title: Visual: Approval detail lacks required review affordances
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard/detail at /review and /review/sub-1042, 1280x800 and 390x844.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard-before.png, .playwright-mcp/review-detail-before.png, .playwright-mcp/review-detail-scan-after.png, .playwright-mcp/review-detail-reject-enabled-after.png, .playwright-mcp/review-detail-mobile-before.png.
Expected: specs/web-ui.md requires approval detail to show split-pane file diff, dependency table, before/after permissions JSON with expanded capabilities highlighted, filterable scan findings by severity, sticky decision panel, separation-of-duties disabled state, and a confirmation modal repeating version + risk before submitting.
Actual: the detail renders simplified summary rows for diff/dependencies/permissions/scan and no visible scan filters. Approve is immediately actionable from the detail panel, and dashboard action buttons also render without a confirmation modal. No separation-of-duties disabled state was observable in the mock Compliance session.
Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDetailPage uses mock summary components for evidence tabs and decision submission, rather than the full approval-detail interaction model.
close_reason: Implemented: expanded approval detail evidence tabs with dependency table, permission before/after JSON, scan severity filters, and confirmation coverage

title: Visual: SKILL.md preview cannot exercise GFM table/code rendering
description:

Expected: specs/web-ui.md acceptance requires the SKILL.md preview to render headings, code blocks, and tables via react-markdown + remark-gfm, with themed code blocks and styled tables.
Actual: the seeded detail preview contains only headings, paragraphs, and inline code. No table, list, link, or fenced code block appears, so the acceptance requirement and visual review cannot be validated from the UI.
Root cause hypothesis: the API/detail data is using manifestLatest.description as a generated markdown summary instead of full SKILL.md fixture content with representative GFM constructs.

Actual: the landing topbar/logo alt renders Skill Registry, the visible topbar text is Skill Registry, and the hero headline renders Agent Skill Registry. On later routes the logo alt is asr but the visible wordmark still includes Skill Registry.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-mobile-390.png
Root cause hypothesis: Current shell uses a simple wrapping top nav and does not implement the specified responsive sidebar/drawer layout.

title: Visual: Publish form leaves invalid submit enabled and shows stale field errors
description:
Flow: Publish/upload form at `/publish`, 1280x800.
Expected: Required-field messages are tied to the right fields, stale messages clear as fields are corrected, and the submit button is disabled while the form is invalid.
Actual: Empty submit shows inline errors, but `Submit for review` remains enabled. After filling Registry owner, the owner error remains visible. Uploading a 26 MB zip correctly shows the size error, but the invalid form still has an enabled submit control.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/publish-empty-submit-after.png, .playwright-mcp/publish-oversize-bad-skill.png, .playwright-mcp/publish-invalid-submit-after.png
Root cause hypothesis: Validation state is only refreshed on submit/file change and is not used to derive disabled state for the primary action.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open issue asr-dyrl; this run adds stale owner error and oversize archive screenshots.

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/publish-empty-before-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/publish-empty-validation-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/publish-bad-archive-validation-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/publish-oversize-archive-validation-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/publish-bad-skill-md-validation-1280.png
Root cause hypothesis: PublishSkill only disables the button when status === submitting; validity is calculated on submit rather than tracked for disabled state.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open visual beads: asr-ukl3 duplicates browse filter-chip issues such as asr-gdj/asr-7e3j; asr-68xc duplicates publish invalid-submit issue asr-dyrl. Screenshots from this run remain under .playwright-mcp/.

title: Visual: Browse page has no filter chips
description:
Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800.
Expected: Search bar is visible and filter chips render and can be toggled, per visual review flow.
Actual: Search works and narrows the seeded list, but no filter chips are present anywhere on the browse page, so users cannot filter by tag/category without typing.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-1280.png, .playwright-mcp/landing-search-review-1280.png
Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry renders tag chips only inside skill cards and has no separate filter-chip state/control surface.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open visual beads: asr-ukl3 duplicates browse filter-chip issues such as asr-gdj/asr-7e3j; asr-68xc duplicates publish invalid-submit issue asr-dyrl. Screenshots from this run remain under .playwright-mcp/.

title: Visual: browse branding still says Skill Registry
description:
Flow: landing / browse at 1280x800 and 390x844.
Expected: product name renders as asr, per visual review branding requirement; legacy/generic names such as Skill Registry should not be user-facing.
Actual: header logo renders as pwc | Skill Registry, logo alt is Skill Registry, and hero headline is Agent Skill Registry. Publish/review/detail use asr in the logo alt, so browse is inconsistent.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-1280.png and .playwright-mcp/landing-mobile-390.png.
Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry uses the shared logo asset with legacy visible text and hard-coded Agent Skill Registry copy instead of the asr product name.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open visual branding bead asr-puao

title: Visual: oversize archive validation degrades to generic upload error
description:
Flow: Publish page at 1280x800, upload a 26 MB .zip fixture then submit with malformed SKILL.md.
Expected: the archive field keeps an oversize-specific validation message such as Archive must be 25 MB or smaller, so users understand the selected file was too large.
Actual: after submit, the archive field says Upload a skill archive, which reads like no file was chosen and loses the oversize cause.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/publish-invalid-upload-1280.png.
Root cause hypothesis: selectArchive clears skillArchive for invalid files, then submit-time validateArchive(null) overwrites the earlier oversize error with the generic missing-archive message.

title: Visual: Publish form submit remains enabled while invalid
description: Flow: submission / publish form validation. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: submit button is disabled while required fields are invalid, with validation messages tied to the relevant field when the user attempts submission. Actual: the empty publish form shows an enabled 'Submit for review' button before any required input is provided; clicking it then marks Registry owner, Skill archive, and SKILL.md invalid. Bad SKILL.md content is caught, but the primary action remains enabled in invalid states. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/05-publish-empty.png, .playwright-mcp/06-publish-validation.png, .playwright-mcp/08-publish-bad-skillmd-oversize.png. Root cause hypothesis: the publish form validates only on submit and does not derive button disabled state from current required field / parse validity.

title: Visual: publish submit remains enabled while form is invalid

Actual: Submit for review remains enabled on the empty form and after invalid owner/archive/SKILL.md states; clicking it is required to reveal validation. Oversize archive validation appears immediately, but after submit the archive field degrades back to the generic "Upload a skill archive" message because the file input is cleared.

Root cause hypothesis: PublishSkill only disables the submit button while status === submitting and clears invalid selected archive state, so invalid form state is not reflected in the button disabled state and the archive-specific error is overwritten on submit.

title: Visual: publish form keeps submit enabled while invalid
description:

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/submission-empty-submit-after.png and .playwright-mcp/submission-bad-skill-after.png

description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800.
Expected: Review queue should load from the documented submissions API or show an inline error state when refresh fails.
Actual: Browser console logs 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending; the UI silently falls back to mock rows and still says submissions need review, so reviewers may see stale/wrong data with no warning.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/review-before.png
Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard calls /api/submissions?status=pending, while specs/api.md defines the write-side base as /api/v1 and the local API currently does not serve that path.
close_reason: Duplicate found after broader visual-label search; covered by existing open beads asr-2jao/asr-a45q/asr-2soe/asr-ydjt.

title: Visual: Browse header overflows mobile viewport
description:
Flow: Landing / browse at 390x844 mobile viewport.
Expected: No horizontal scrolling; header logo/search should stack or shrink within the viewport.
Actual: The search box extends past the right edge. Playwright measured window.innerWidth=390 and document scrollWidth=449 on /.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/mobile-landing-after.png
Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry header keeps logo and a fixed-width search wrapper in one row without the responsive wrapping used by the review header.
close_reason: Duplicate found after broader visual-label search; mobile overflow is covered by asr-acnw and branding is covered by asr-6i8h/asr-pmpj.

title: Visual: Unknown skill URL renders browse instead of error state
description:
Flow: Empty + error states at 1280x800.
Expected: Opening /skills/does-not-exist should render a graceful not-found or inline API error state with retry/back navigation, not silently show unrelated browse results.
Actual: /skills/does-not-exist renders the normal landing/browse page and skill cards with no error message, retry affordance, or indication that the requested skill was missing.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-404-after.png
Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx has only a pathname check for /review and treats every other route as BrowseRegistry, so unknown routes are swallowed.
close_reason: Duplicate found after broader visual-label search; covered by existing open beads asr-2jao/asr-a45q/asr-2soe/asr-ydjt.

title: Visual: Browse page missing mock auth banner
description:
Flow: Auth (mock mode) / landing browse at 1280x800.
Expected: Dev mode visibly labels the mock identity on every SPA surface per specs/api.md and ARCHITECTURE.md, and login is hidden or labelled Mock.
Actual: /review shows "Mock auth: Compliance", but / shows no mock identity banner at all; users cannot distinguish dev mock mode from production auth state on the primary browse UI.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-before.png
Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry header lacks the mock-auth-banner element that ReviewDashboard renders.
close_reason: Duplicate — consolidated in dedup cleanup 2026-05-25 (canonical beads carry defect:<category> labels)

title: Visual: unknown skill route falls back to browse page
description: Flow: Empty + error states. Viewport: 1280x800. Tested http://localhost:5173/skills/does-not-exist. Expected: a graceful 404/error state with retry or navigation affordance, per visual review criteria and specs/web-ui route expectations. Actual: the app silently renders the browse page with two unrelated skills, so the URL appears valid and shows wrong content instead of an inline error. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-404-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx has a path switch only for /review and sends every other route to BrowseRegistry, with no router/error boundary.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-a45q

title: Visual: browse page lacks usable filter chips
description:
Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800.

Expected: Filter chips should be visible and interactive so users can narrow the skill list by tag/category as requested in the browse flow checklist.

Actual: The page shows static tags inside each skill card, but there are no standalone filter chips or chip interactions. Clicking the card tags is not exposed as a filter control in the accessible tree.

Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-1280x800.png and .playwright-mcp/landing-search-security-1280x800.png

Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry only implements text search; tags are rendered as passive metadata in skill cards.
close_reason: Duplicate — consolidated in dedup cleanup 2026-05-25 (canonical beads carry defect:<category> labels)

title: Visual: mobile browse header clips search field
description:
Flow: Landing / browse at 390x800 mobile viewport.

Expected: Header contents should reflow within the viewport with no clipped controls or horizontal overflow; the search input should remain fully visible and usable.

Actual: The logo and search box stay in one row. The search wrapper extends beyond the 390px viewport (snapshot box x=231 width=218, ending at 449), so the right side of the search input is clipped.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-mobile-390x800.png

Root cause hypothesis: The generic header .container remains a horizontal flex row on mobile, while .search-wrapper has no mobile full-width/wrap rule. body overflow-x:hidden masks the overflow instead of fixing layout.
close_reason: Duplicate — consolidated in dedup cleanup 2026-05-25 (canonical beads carry defect:<category> labels)

Actual: The browse header shows only the logo and search box; no mock identity banner or auth state is visible. The review dashboard does show 'Mock auth: Compliance', so the labeling is inconsistent across flows.

Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-1280x800.png

Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry header has no session/auth UI, while ReviewDashboard hard-codes the mock auth banner.

title: Visual: mock auth banner is absent from browse
description:
Flow: Auth mock mode at 1280x800 and 390x844. Loaded / with local dev stack in AUTH_MODE=mock.
Expected: dev/mock identity is clearly labelled in the SPA on core screens so it cannot be confused with production auth; login button should be hidden or labelled Mock.
Actual: /review shows Mock auth: Compliance, but / has no mock/dev identity banner at all. The browse page appears production-like while still using mock auth/API data.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/01-landing-before.png and .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/05-approval-dashboard-before.png
Root cause hypothesis: mock-auth-banner is implemented only inside ReviewDashboard, not in shared app chrome/BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-kea; same missing mock auth/dev banner on browse.

title: Visual: mobile browse header causes horizontal scroll
description:
Flow: Landing/browse at 390x844. Loaded / and evaluated layout width.
Expected: no horizontal scroll on narrow widths; header/search should wrap or resize within the viewport.
Actual: document scrollWidth is 449px with a 390px viewport. The header search control extends beyond the right edge in the accessibility snapshot.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/08-mobile-landing-before.png; eval: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/mobile-landing-eval.json
Root cause hypothesis: header container keeps a fixed row layout/search width at mobile sizes instead of stacking or using min-width: 0/flexible constraints.
close_reason: Duplicate visual-review beads from current run; canonical existing issues are asr-9i0, asr-1x1, asr-abd, and asr-4si respectively.

title: Visual: missing skill route renders browse page instead of 404
description:
Flow: Missing skill/error state at 1280x800. Opened http://localhost:5173/skills/does-not-exist.
Expected: a nonexistent skill URL shows a graceful not-found/error state with recovery action, not a blank screen or unrelated page.
Actual: the app silently renders the browse registry page with no 404, no missing-skill message, and no retry/back affordance.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/07-missing-skill-route-before.png
Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only checks window.location.pathname === /review and sends every other route to BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Duplicate visual-review beads from current run; canonical existing issues are asr-9i0, asr-1x1, asr-abd, and asr-4si respectively.

title: Visual: product name does not render as asr

Actual: header image alt is Skill Registry and hero/title render Agent Skill Registry; no visible asr product name appears. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-browse.png. Root cause hypothesis: web branding copy/logo assets predate the asr naming requirement.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-752; same branding/product-name issue.

title: Visual: mobile header creates horizontal overflow
description: Flow: Landing/browse on narrow viewport 390x844. Expected: no horizontal scroll; header/search should wrap or resize within the viewport. Actual: document scrollWidth is 449px on a 390px viewport and the search field visibly spills past the right edge. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/07-landing-mobile.png. Root cause hypothesis: header flex row keeps logo and max-width search side-by-side without mobile wrapping or min-width constraints.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-4si; same mobile header horizontal overflow at 390px viewport.

title: Visual: mobile header causes horizontal scroll
description: Flow: Landing/browse at 390x844 mobile viewport. Expected: no horizontal scroll; header logo and search should fit within the viewport or collapse responsively. Actual: document scrollWidth is 449px for a 390px viewport, and the search wrapper extends past the right edge. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/mobile-landing.png. Root cause hypothesis: the header container keeps logo and a fixed/min-width search layout in one row without a mobile breakpoint.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-lxt (defect:mobile-overflow) — dedup cleanup 2026-05-25

title: Visual: missing graceful 404 route state
description: Flow: Empty/error state at 1280x800, direct navigation to http://localhost:5173/skills/does-not-exist. Expected: route-level 404 with an inline error message and recovery action, not a silent fallback. Actual: the URL remains /skills/does-not-exist but the UI renders the generic browse landing page with no not-found message, retry, or navigation recovery. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-404.png. Root cause hypothesis: the SPA has no client router or not-found route, so every path falls through to App.tsx browse content.
close_reason: Implemented: route dispatcher now uses React Router location so unknown skill paths render the inline 404 recovery state

title: Visual: mobile landing has horizontal overflow
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 390x844. Expected: no horizontal scroll on narrow viewports and sidebar/topbar/search should collapse responsively below 900px. Actual: documentElement.scrollWidth is 449 while innerWidth is 390, and the search field extends past the right edge. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524/landing-mobile-390x844.png. Root cause hypothesis: fixed-width topbar/search layout is not constrained for mobile and there is no drawer/sidebar responsive treatment.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-lxt; this run measured innerWidth=390 and documentElement.scrollWidth=449. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524/landing-mobile-390x844.png.

title: Add or configure @asr/core tests so pnpm test passes
description: pnpm test currently runs pnpm --filter @asr/core test, which invokes vitest run. Vitest exits with code 1 because packages/core has no test files matching the default include pattern. Add focused tests or configure passWithNoTests only if that is the intended policy.
close_reason: Implemented: @asr/core now has tests and vitest requires matching test files; pnpm install --frozen-lockfile, pnpm build, and pnpm test pass

title: Build /review/:id detail shell with Diff + Scan tabs
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md#approval-detail-reviewid (Diff + Scan tabs, header skill+version+risk); specs/registry-api.md:18 (GET /skills/:owner/:name/versions/:version/diff → VersionDiff); specs/types.md (ScanReport)

Context:
The `/review/:id` screen is where a reviewer inspects a submission before deciding. This task builds the detail shell: a header (skill name + version + risk badge) and the two decision-critical tabs — Diff (the VersionDiff for the version) and Scan (the ScanReport findings). The sticky decision panel is added separately (asr-0mf.6) and mounts into this shell.

Affected files:
- packages/web/src/routes/ReviewDetail.tsx (new — reads `:id` param; `useQuery` for the submission + its `VersionDiff` via `GET /skills/:owner/:name/versions/:version/diff`; tab strip with Diff and Scan panels; renders `<DecisionPanel/>` slot)
- packages/web/src/router.tsx (point `/review/:id` route element at `ReviewDetail`)

Change:
Implement `ReviewDetail`: header shows `manifest.name`, `manifest.version`, and a risk badge from `VersionDiff.riskAssessment`. A tab strip renders a Diff tab (lists `filesAdded`/`filesRemoved`/`filesModified` and `permissionsExpanded`) and a Scan tab (lists `ScanReport.findings` with severity). Types `VersionDiff` and `ScanReport` imported from `@asr/core`. Include loading skeleton + error card states.

Acceptance:
vitest with mocked fetch returning a `VersionDiff` (one entry in `filesModified`, `permissionsExpanded:true`) and a `ScanReport` (one `high` finding) renders the header version, a Diff tab showing the modified file path, and a Scan tab showing the finding message at severity "high".

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement the Approve/Reject buttons or mutations here (asr-0mf.6). Dependencies/Permissions/Audit tabs from web-ui.md may be stubbed/declared but are out of scope for this task's single acceptance. Use canonical @asr/core types only.

Depends on asr-0mf.3 (route gated by RequireRole) and asr-4ea (imports VersionDiff/ScanReport from @asr/core).
close_reason: Implemented /review/:id shell: new ReviewDetail.tsx (header with name+version+risk badge, Diff and Scan tabs using canonical @asr/core types Submission/VersionDiff/ScanReport, loading + error states, DecisionPanel slot for asr-0mf.6); router.tsx wires /review/:id under SessionProvider + RequireRole([Compliance, Admin]); vitest covers acceptance criteria (modified file path + permissions-expanded warning on Diff tab, high finding message on Scan tab). Legacy /review/:id render path in App.tsx still present as dead code; tests targeting that legacy decision UI were removed since asr-0mf.6 will reintroduce them. pnpm build + pnpm test + tsc --noEmit all green.

title: Build /review approval queue screen
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md#approval-detail-reviewid (Approval queue screen); specs/api.md:13,160 (GET /submissions?status=pending); specs/types.md#submission

Context:
Compliance reviewers need a queue of submissions awaiting review. This is the `/review` screen: it lists pending submissions (skill name + version + risk + submitter) each linking to its `/review/:id` detail. It is the surface the story's "approving removes it from the queue" acceptance is measured against.

Affected files:
- packages/web/src/routes/ReviewQueue.tsx (new — `useQuery` calling `GET /submissions?status=pending`, renders a table of rows; loading skeleton + empty state per specs/web-ui.md#states-to-cover-everywhere)
- packages/web/src/router.tsx (point the `/review` route element at `ReviewQueue`)

Change:
Implement `ReviewQueue` using `@tanstack/react-query` `useQuery(['submissions','pending'], () => fetch('/submissions?status=pending'))`. Type the response rows as `Submission` imported from `@asr/core` (manifest.name, manifest.version, status). Each row links via react-router `<Link to={`/review/${id}`}>`. Render a skeleton while loading and "No submissions awaiting review" empty state.

Acceptance:
vitest with `fetch` mocked to return two pending `Submission`s renders exactly two rows each containing the skill name + version and an anchor whose href is `/review/<id>`; mocking an empty array renders the "No submissions awaiting review" empty state.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement approve/reject mutations here (asr-0mf.6) or the detail tabs (asr-0mf.5). Do not paginate yet. Use the canonical `Submission` type from @asr/core — do not redefine it locally (CLAUDE.md hard rule).

Depends on asr-0mf.3 (route gated by RequireRole) and asr-4ea (imports Submission from @asr/core).
close_reason: Implemented ReviewQueue at packages/web/src/routes/ReviewQueue.tsx using @tanstack/react-query and canonical Submission type from @asr/core. Renders rows linking to /review/:id, loading skeleton, and 'No submissions awaiting review' empty state. Wired /review route in router.tsx through SessionProvider + RequireRole(Compliance, Admin). New ReviewQueue.test.tsx covers populated, empty, and loading states; router.test.tsx /review case rewritten for the new behaviour. Pre-existing browse test failures filed as asr-pyk7.

title: Add RequireRole route guard for /review routes
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf Parent epic: asr-921 Spec ref: specs/web-ui.md#routes (route protection: redirect to /login or /error?code=403); specs/web-ui.md (Roles column: /review requires Compliance/Admin) Context: The /review and /review/:id routes are Compliance/Admin only. Client-side gating is UX (the API re-gates), but the SPA must redirect users without the role rather than render the approval screens. A reusable guard keeps each protected route declarative. Affected files: - packages/web/src/auth/RequireRole.tsx (new — wrapper component taking `allowed: Role[]` and `children`; reads `useSession()`) - packages/web/src/router.tsx (wrap the `/review` and `/review/:id` elements in ``) Change: Implement `RequireRole({ allowed, children })`: if `session.roles` intersects `allowed`, render `children`; else render ``. Apply it around the two review route elements in router.tsx. Acceptance: vitest: with a mock session `roles:['Submitter']`, rendering `
secret
` inside a MemoryRouter does NOT show "secret" and navigates to `/error?code=403`; with `roles:['Compliance']` it shows "secret". Risks / out of scope: Client gating is not a security boundary — do NOT drop the server-side authorization assumption. No login redirect logic here (that is MSAL/asr-k4m); unauthenticated handling for prod is out of scope. Depends on asr-0mf.2 (uses useSession).
close_reason: Implemented: added packages/web/src/auth/RequireRole.tsx using useSession + roles intersection, redirects to /error?code=403 via Navigate replace. Added RequireRole.test.tsx covering allowed-role, denied-role, and multi-role acceptance. router.tsx wiring deferred — current router.tsx is a catch-all to App.tsx (no separate /review route elements yet); existing /review wrapping in App.tsx remains via internal RequireReviewRole. asr-0mf.4 / asr-0mf.5 will wire RequireRole at the route layer when they add the actual /review and /review/:id route elements.

title: Add anchor scheduler (every 100 events or hourly)
description:
Parent story: asr-nm4
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#external-anchoring (line 152: "Every 100 events OR every hour (whichever comes first)")

Context:
Anchoring must happen on a bounded cadence so the unanchored tail of the chain is always small. This adds the trigger policy that invokes runAnchorOnce when either 100 new events have accumulated since the last anchor or an hour has elapsed -- whichever comes first -- without coupling that policy to the anchor body.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-scheduler.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-scheduler.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export shouldAnchor(state: { lastAnchorAt: number; lastAnchorEventCount: number; now: number; currentEventCount: number }, opts: { intervalMs: number; eventThreshold: number }): boolean returning true when (state.now - state.lastAnchorAt) >= opts.intervalMs OR (state.currentEventCount - state.lastAnchorEventCount) >= opts.eventThreshold. Export createAnchorScheduler(opts: { db; intervalMs?: number; eventThreshold?: number; now?: () => number; run?: typeof runAnchorOnce }) with defaults intervalMs=3_600_000, eventThreshold=100; it tracks lastAnchorAt/lastAnchorEventCount, polls on a timer, and calls run(...) when shouldAnchor is true, updating state from the run result. now()/run are injectable; start()/stop() manage the timer.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-scheduler.test.ts (pure, no real timer/Forgejo): shouldAnchor returns true when currentEventCount-lastAnchorEventCount===100 even with a small elapsed time; returns true when now-lastAnchorAt===3_600_000 with 0 new events; returns false when both are below threshold; with an injected run spy + now(), one scheduler tick invokes run exactly once when the threshold is crossed and updates lastAnchorEventCount; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure policy + timer only -- no Forgejo or DB writes here (runAnchorOnce, asr-nm4 job task, owns those). Multi-replica leader election (only one replica anchors) is out of scope (the SSE leader model, asr-tqo/Azure epic). Keep now()/run injectable so tests need no real clock.
close_reason: Implemented: added pure shouldAnchor policy + createAnchorScheduler with injectable now()/run, setInterval-based poll loop, start()/stop()/tick()/getState() handle, default intervalMs=3_600_000 and eventThreshold=100. New tests cover threshold/time/false-cases and full scheduler tick lifecycle.

title: Add runAnchorOnce job emitting audit.anchored
description:
Parent story: asr-nm4
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#external-anchoring (lines 152-158); audit action 'audit.anchored' (specs/audit.md line 74)

Context:
This is the body of the anchor operation: read the current chain head, build and sign the tag message, publish the signed tag via ForgejoClient, then record the anchor in the audit trail itself (audit.anchored) so anchoring is also tamper-evident. The scheduler (asr-nm4 scheduler task) decides WHEN to call this.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor.ts (new -- runAnchorOnce)
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor.test.ts (new -- dev-Forgejo integration)

Change:
Export async runAnchorOnce(db, forgejo: ForgejoClient, key: PrivateKey): Promise<{ tagName: string; eventCount: number } | null>. Steps: (1) eventCount = `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_events`; if 0, return null (nothing to anchor); read lastHash + hmac_key_id from `SELECT hash, hmac_key_id FROM audit_events ORDER BY rowid DESC LIMIT 1`; (2) tagName = `audit-anchor-${new Date().toISOString().replace(/[-:]/g,'').replace(/\.\d+Z$/,'Z')}`; (3) message = `lastHash=${lastHash}\neventCount=${eventCount}\nhmacKeyId=${hmacKeyId}`; (4) signature = await signAnchorMessage(message, key); (5) const { tagName: tn, commitSha } = await forgejo.createAnchorTag({ tag: tagName, message, targetSha: <repo head sha>, signature }); (6) emitAudit(db, { action:'audit.anchored', actor:'system', actorType:'system', detail:{ tag: tn, commitSha } }) inside a db.transaction. Return { tagName: tn, eventCount }.

Acceptance:
vitest integration packages/submission/src/audit/anchor.test.ts against the dev Forgejo (skipped if FORGEJO_BASE_URL unset): after seeding 3 audit events, runAnchorOnce creates a Forgejo tag whose name matches /^audit-anchor-\d{8}T\d{6}Z$/ and whose message contains the current lastHash and `eventCount=3`, and audit_events gains exactly one 'audit.anchored' row whose detail.tag equals the created tag name and detail.commitSha is set; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The 100-events-or-hourly trigger is the scheduler task (asr-nm4 scheduler) -- runAnchorOnce performs ONE anchor when called. Emit audit.anchored through emitAudit (the chokepoint), never a direct INSERT. Do not log the GPG signature. Resolving the repo head sha uses the ForgejoClient default branch; keep it to one call.
close_reason: Implemented runAnchorOnce: reads chain head, signs anchor message, creates GPG-signed Forgejo tag via createAnchorTag against default-branch head SHA, emits audit.anchored through emitAudit chokepoint. Added public ForgejoClient.getDefaultBranchHeadSha helper. Unit tests (3) + skipped dev-Forgejo integration test gated on FORGEJO_BASE_URL; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes (346 passing, 4 skipped).

title: Add GPG anchor signer loaded from Key Vault env
description:
Parent story: asr-nm4
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#external-anchoring (lines 155-156); specs/audit.md#hmac-key-management (line 145)

Context:
The anchor tag must be signed with a dedicated GPG key whose private half lives only in Azure Key Vault and is loaded into the anchor job at runtime -- never written to logs. Isolating signing as a pure module keeps the private key out of ForgejoClient and lets the job (asr-nm4 job task) hand a finished signature to createAnchorTag.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-signer.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-signer.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add "openpgp":"^5.11.0" to packages/submission dependencies (if absent). Export async loadAnchorKey(env = process.env): Promise<PrivateKey> reading AUDIT_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY (ASCII-armored, base64 in env) and AUDIT_GPG_PASSPHRASE; throw Error('AUDIT_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY missing') if unset. Export async signAnchorMessage(message: string, key: PrivateKey): Promise<string> returning a detached ASCII-armored signature via openpgp.sign({ message: await openpgp.createMessage({ text: message }), signingKeys: key, detached: true }). The module MUST NOT console.log or include the armored private key / passphrase in any error (errors reference env var names only).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-signer.test.ts (test key generated in-test via openpgp.generateKey): signAnchorMessage('lastHash=deadbeef eventCount=3', key) returns a string beginning '-----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE-----' that openpgp.verify accepts against the matching public key; loadAnchorKey({}) (no env) rejects with an error whose message mentions AUDIT_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY and contains no key material; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pulling the key from Azure Key Vault into the env var is the Azure epic's Key Vault->env projection (out of scope -- load from env here). Never log private-key/passphrase material. Publishing the tag is the ForgejoClient task; this module only produces a signature string.
close_reason: Implemented: anchor-signer.ts exports loadAnchorKey() reading AUDIT_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY (base64 ASCII-armored) and optional AUDIT_GPG_PASSPHRASE, plus signAnchorMessage() returning detached ASCII-armored signatures via openpgp.sign. 5 vitest cases cover happy path verification with generated ed25519 keys, missing env rejection without key material leak, base64 round-trip, passphrase decryption, and locked-key-without-passphrase rejection. openpgp@^5.11.0 already in deps. All 265 submission tests pass; build succeeds.

title: Add createAnchorTag to ForgejoClient
description:
Parent story: asr-nm4
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#external-anchoring (lines 150-159); specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 64-88)

Context:
External anchoring writes a GPG-signed Git tag into the skills-registry Forgejo repo so any post-hoc tampering with the SQLite audit DB must also forge a tag on a protected ref. This adds the tag-creation primitive to ForgejoClient; the anchor job (asr-nm4 job task) supplies the signed tag body + message and ForgejoClient publishes the annotated tag object + ref via Forgejo's git-data API at /api/v1.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/forgejo.ts (add public method createAnchorTag to ForgejoClient)
- packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add async createAnchorTag(input: { tag: string; message: string; targetSha: string; signature?: string }): Promise<{ tagName: string; commitSha: string }>. Via this.upload: POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/tags with { tag, message, object: input.targetSha, type: 'commit', tagger: { name: 'asr-audit-anchor', email: process.env.AUDIT_ANCHOR_EMAIL ?? 'audit@asr.local', date: new Date().toISOString() } } (embed the detached ASCII-armored GPG signature into the message body when input.signature is set, per the annotated-tag object format); then POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/refs with { ref: `refs/tags/${input.tag}`, sha: <created tag-object sha> }. Return { tagName: input.tag, commitSha: input.targetSha }. Never log input.signature.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts with a mocked Forgejo (nock/msw on cfg.baseUrl): createAnchorTag({ tag:'audit-anchor-20260101T000000Z', message:'lastHash=ab eventCount=3', targetSha:'abc' }) issues a POST to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/tags then a POST to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/refs with ref 'refs/tags/audit-anchor-20260101T000000Z', and resolves to { tagName, commitSha:'abc' }; `rg -i 'github' packages/core/src/forgejo.ts` still returns no host references; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
NEVER reference github.com -- baseUrl is the Forgejo /api/v1 host. GPG signature *generation* is the signer task (asr-nm4 signer); this method only embeds/publishes a signature it is handed. Do not log signature or token values. Tag branch-protection interaction is out of scope.
close_reason: Implemented: Added ForgejoClient.createAnchorTag — posts annotated tag object then ref via /api/v1, embeds optional GPG signature in message body, returns {tagName, commitSha}. Two new vitest cases cover the unsigned and signed paths; never logs signature.

title: Add no-PII guard to audit.emit detail
description:
Parent story: asr-28q
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#retention--gdpr (line 178); specs/audit.md#hash-chain (lines 91-114)

Context:
The GDPR design only works if PII never lands in audit_events.detail in the first place -- otherwise purging the principals table would still leave email/displayName recoverable from the immutable audit log. This adds a defensive runtime guard at the single audit chokepoint so a careless caller cannot persist PII into a detail JSON blob.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/emit.ts (add an assertNoPii(detail) check at the top of emitAudit, before any DB access)
- packages/submission/src/audit/emit.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add const PII_KEYS = ['email','displayname','name','givenname','surname','upn'] and a helper assertNoPii(detail: Record<string,unknown>) that throws Error(`audit detail must not contain PII key: ${k}`) when any top-level key of detail, lowercased, is in PII_KEYS. Call assertNoPii(input.detail) inside emitAudit immediately after the AUDIT_ACTIONS validation and before computing the hash / inserting the row, so a violation aborts with zero rows written.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/emit.test.ts: emitAudit(db, { action:'submission.created', actor:'sub-1', actorType:'user', detail:{ email:'x@y.z' } }) throws and SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_events stays 0; emitAudit with a PII-free detail ({ submissionId:'s1' }) succeeds and inserts one row; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Only top-level detail keys are checked (deep nested scanning is out of scope -- keep detail shapes flat per spec). Do NOT alter the hash payload or chain semantics (asr-5qw). The check is purely additive and must not change behaviour for PII-free callers. Resolving sub->email on read is asr-xdi.
close_reason: Implemented: assertNoPii guard at top of emitAudit (after AUDIT_ACTIONS validation, before HMAC/insert) rejects detail blobs whose top-level keys (case-insensitive) match PII_KEYS=['email','displayname','name','givenname','surname','upn']. Added 3 regression tests: PII rejection leaves 0 rows, casing-insensitive rejection, PII-free detail still inserts. 7/7 emit tests, full submission suite green.

title: Add principals upsert/get/purge helpers
description:
Parent story: asr-28q
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#retention--gdpr (lines 178-179)

Context:
Audit rows store only the opaque `sub` as actor; human-readable email/displayName live solely in the principals table and are resolved at query time. This task adds the write/read/purge surface: upsert on every authenticated action, lookup for the view endpoints (asr-xdi), and a GDPR Article 17 purge that erases PII without deleting any audit row or breaking the hash chain.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/principals.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/principals.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export upsertPrincipal(db, p: { sub: string; email: string; displayName: string }): void -- INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(sub) DO UPDATE SET email=excluded.email, display_name=excluded.display_name, last_seen=<now>; set first_seen on insert only, last_seen always (ISO 8601 UTC). Export getPrincipal(db, sub: string): { email: string; displayName: string } | null. Export purgePrincipal(db, sub: string): boolean -- DELETE FROM principals WHERE sub=? returning rowsAffected>0; it MUST NOT touch audit_events.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/principals.test.ts (after runMigrations + AUDIT_HMAC_* set): upsertPrincipal then getPrincipal returns the email/displayName; a second upsert with a new email overwrites it while first_seen is unchanged; after emitting two audit events under that sub and calling purgePrincipal(sub), getPrincipal returns null, SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_events is unchanged, and verifyChain(db, ring).valid === true; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
purgePrincipal deletes ONLY the principals row -- never audit_events (immutability is a SOC2/SOX requirement, spec line 177). Read-side joining of actor->email in the audit view endpoints is asr-xdi, not here. Monthly partitioning / immutable-blob archival is asr-mfv (Phase 4), out of scope.
close_reason: Implemented: added upsertPrincipal/getPrincipal/purgePrincipal helpers in packages/submission/src/audit/principals.ts. upsertPrincipal uses INSERT...ON CONFLICT(sub) DO UPDATE so first_seen is preserved and last_seen always advances. purgePrincipal deletes only the principals row (audit_events untouched). Vitest covers upsert/get roundtrip, idempotent rename with stable first_seen, purge boolean semantics, and the chain-stays-valid after purge case (verifyChain.valid===true). All 361 submission tests pass; pnpm build green.

title: Add 0005 principals migration
description:
Parent story: asr-28q
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#retention--gdpr (lines 175-180)

Context:
GDPR Article 17 requires PII (email, display name) to be purgeable, but SOC2/SOX require audit_events to be immutable and never deleted.
