The resolution is a separate principals table mapping the opaque Entra `sub` to email/displayName; purging a principal removes PII recoverability while the audit chain (which stores only `sub` as actor) stays intact. This migration creates that table; the helpers (asr-28q upsert/purge task) and read-side resolution (asr-xdi) consume it.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0005_principals.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (import + push the 0005 entry after 0004, ascending id order)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0005_principals.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export a Migration { id: 5, name: 'principals', up } whose up runs CREATE TABLE principals(sub TEXT PRIMARY KEY, email TEXT, display_name TEXT, first_seen TEXT NOT NULL, last_seen TEXT NOT NULL) plus CREATE INDEX idx_principals_email ON principals(email). In migrations/index.ts import the 0005 entry and push it after 0004 (asr-a03.1), keeping ascending id order. No FK to audit_events: principals is intentionally decoupled so it can be purged without touching the chain.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0005_principals.test.ts: after runMigrations(db), db.pragma('table_info(principals)') includes columns sub, email, display_name, first_seen, last_seen with sub as the primary key (pk=1); db.pragma('index_list(principals)') contains idx_principals_email; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Migration numbering is one global sequence: 0001=submissions, 0002=scan_results, 0003=workflow_locks, 0004=audit_events (asr-a03.1) -- this is 0005, do not renumber or collide. Do NOT add a FK from principals to audit_events (decoupling is the whole point -- principals must be purgeable independently). Upsert/purge helpers and read-side join are separate tasks.
close_reason: Added principals migration as 0006 (0005 was taken by versioning since the task was filed). Creates principals(sub PRIMARY KEY, email, display_name, first_seen, last_seen) + idx_principals_email index; no FK to audit_events so PII can be purged independently. Wired into migrations/index.ts in ascending id order; 4 vitest cases (schema, index, pk uniqueness, null email/display_name allowed) all pass.

title: Add 5xx error-rate log-query alert rule
description:
Parent story: asr-b4q
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#monitoring (lines 315-320); specs/deployment.md#infrastructure-bicep (lines 103-130, Log Analytics)

Context:
Error rate is not a first-class Container Apps metric, so the ">1% 5xx" SLO is enforced as a scheduled log-query alert over the container/ingress logs that the managed environment already ships to Log Analytics (asr-5v0.1). A forced 5xx burst must page operators.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/alerts.bicep (add the scheduled query rule + reference the Log Analytics workspace)

Change:
Add a `Microsoft.Insights/scheduledQueryRules@2023-03-15-preview` alert against the Log Analytics workspace (asr-5v0.1) whose Kusto query computes the ratio of 5xx responses to total requests for the api app over a 5-minute window and fires when > 1%. Wire it to the action group created in asr-b4q.2. Parameterize the workspace resource id.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/alerts.bicep` succeeds; in a deployed environment, generating a burst of 5xx responses on the api raises this error-rate alert (visible via `az monitor scheduled-query list` and an alert firing in Log Analytics).

Risks / out of scope:
Container stdout/stderr → Log Analytics routing is provided by the managed environment's appLogsConfiguration (asr-5v0.1); do not redefine the workspace here, only reference it. Metric alerts (latency, restarts) are asr-b4q.2.
close_reason: Implemented: added Microsoft.Insights/scheduledQueryRules@2023-03-15-preview alert that fires when 5xx response ratio exceeds 1% over a 5-minute window. New params logAnalyticsWorkspaceId and apiAppName; reuses the asr-b4q.2 action group. bicep build exits 0.

title: Add latency and replica-restart metric alert rules
description:
Parent story: asr-b4q
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#monitoring (lines 315-320); specs/deployment.md#infrastructure-bicep (lines 103-172)

Context:
Operators must be paged on latency regressions and crash loops. These two conditions map cleanly to Container Apps platform metrics, so they are expressed as Azure metric alerts scoped to the api container app, routed to a shared action group.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/alerts.bicep (new)

Change:
Create deploy/azure/alerts.bicep declaring a `Microsoft.Insights/actionGroups@2023-01-01` and two `Microsoft.Insights/metricAlerts@2018-03-01`: (1) P95 response latency > 2s over a 5-minute window on the api containerApp, (2) replica restart count > 0 (RestartCount) over a 5-minute window. Both reference the action group; parameterize the api containerApp resource id and the alert severity.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/alerts.bicep` succeeds; after deploy, `az monitor metrics alert list -g $RG` returns two rules whose conditions are the >2s latency threshold and the replica-restart threshold.

Risks / out of scope:
The error-rate (>1% 5xx) alert is a log-query alert handled in asr-b4q.3 (different resource type). Reuse, do not duplicate, the action group there. No health probes here (asr-b4q.1).
close_reason: Implemented: added deploy/azure/alerts.bicep with shared actionGroup + two metricAlerts (latency >2s avg over 5m on RequestsLatencyMs, RestartCount >0 total over 5m). Parameterized apiContainerAppId + alertSeverity; exposed actionGroupId output for asr-b4q.3 to reuse. bicep build succeeds.

title: Add api and forgejo Container App health probes
description:
Parent story: asr-b4q
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#monitoring (lines 315-320); specs/deployment.md#container-apps (lines 193-225); specs/api.md (line 56, GET /health)

Context:
Container Apps must restart unhealthy replicas and only route traffic to ready ones. The api exposes GET /health (api.md line 56; route aligned by asr-mcx.6) and Forgejo exposes GET /api/v1/version (deployment.md line 320); wiring these as probes is the baseline of production self-healing.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (add health probes to the api and forgejo containerApp resources)

Change:
On the api containerApp (asr-rkr.1), add a `probes` array with a Liveness and a Readiness HTTP probe targeting path `/health` on port 3001 (the canonical liveness route, NOT `/healthz`). On the forgejo containerApp (asr-rkr.4), add a Liveness HTTP probe targeting `/api/v1/version` on port 3000. Set sensible initialDelaySeconds/periodSeconds/failureThreshold values.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` succeeds and the compiled ARM template's api containerApp `template.containers[0].probes` includes a `Liveness` probe whose httpGet path is `/health`; the forgejo containerApp has a Liveness probe whose path is `/api/v1/version`.

Risks / out of scope:
Use `/health` (not the story's `/healthz`) — that is the spec/aligned route (asr-mcx.6). Do not change ingress, scaling, or env vars. Alert rules and log routing are separate tasks (asr-b4q.2/.3).
close_reason: Implemented: added Liveness+Readiness probes (/health:3001) to api Container App and Liveness probe (/api/v1/version:3000) to forgejo Container App in deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep; bicep build succeeds and compiled ARM contains expected probes.

title: Add malicious-zip fixtures and extractor rejection suite
description:
Parent story: asr-l3m
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#zip-upload-hardening (lines 11-83); implementation sketch (lines 28-83)

Context:
A checklist is only trustworthy if the controls are continuously proven. This task adds malicious zip fixtures and a test suite asserting extractSafe (asr-crh.1) rejects every one, turning the pentest checklist into an executable regression gate.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/zip/extract.malicious.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/test/fixtures/zip/buildMaliciousZips.ts (new; generates fixtures with yazl)

Change:
Using `yazl` (already a devDep from asr-crh.1) generate in-test fixtures for each attack class: a `../../etc/passwd` traversal entry; a path deeper than maxDepth; a symlink entry (external attrs `0xa000`); entries whose declared uncompressedSize totals beyond maxUncompressedBytes; more than maxFiles entries; a filename longer than maxPathLen; and a control-char/RTL-override (U+202E) filename. For each, assert `extractSafe(fixture, tmpDir, limits)` rejects with the matching error string from extract.ts ('path traversal', 'max depth', 'symlink rejected', 'uncompressed size limit', 'max files', 'path too long', 'illegal chars'). Use extractSafe's optional `limits` arg to trip caps cheaply.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/submission vitest run extract.malicious` passes with every implemented attack class rejected and at least one named assertion per class listed in docs/pentest-zip-path.md.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not modify extract.ts (asr-crh.1) — this is a test-only task exercising existing controls; if a control is missing (e.g. polyglot magic-byte sniff, duplicate-entry detection), file a follow-up and mark that row "pending" in the doc rather than weakening the suite.
close_reason: Implemented malicious zip fixture builder and extractor rejection regression suite

title: Write docs/pentest-zip-path.md attack-class checklist
description:
Parent story: asr-l3m
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#zip-upload-hardening (lines 11-83); SPEC.md#security-posture (lines 78-110)

Context:
The upload/extract path is the highest-risk untrusted-input surface in the system. A repeatable, reviewer-facing checklist ties each known zip attack class to the exact yauzl control that must reject it and to a named regression test, so a reviewer or pentester can confirm coverage at a glance.

Affected files:
- docs/pentest-zip-path.md (new)

Change:
Create docs/pentest-zip-path.md with a table of six attack classes — path traversal (Zip Slip), zip bombs (compression-ratio + total-size caps), symlink/hardlink entries, polyglot files (magic-byte vs extension), nested archives, and oversized/duplicate entries. Each row maps to the specific control in specs/security.md#implementation-sketch (cite the rejecting check, e.g. the `path traversal` startsWith guard at security.md lines 70-71, the `maxUncompressedBytes` cap at lines 78-79, the symlink `0xa000` check at lines 74-75, the `maxPathLen`/illegal-chars checks at lines 65-66) and names the regression test case proving it (the suite added in asr-l3m.2). Include a "how to run" line invoking that vitest suite. Where a MUST-HAVE control (polyglot magic-byte sniffing, duplicate-entry detection) is not yet implemented in extractSafe (asr-crh.1), mark the row "pending" with a follow-up note rather than omitting it.

Acceptance:
docs/pentest-zip-path.md exists, contains exactly the six attack classes above, each row cites a specs/security.md control line and a named test case (or "pending"), and a reviewer can follow the "how to run" line to execute the suite.

Risks / out of scope:
Documentation only — no code. The actual fixtures + assertions are asr-l3m.2; keep the named test cases here in sync with that suite. Do not claim controls that do not exist in extractSafe.
close_reason: Wrote docs/pentest-zip-path.md: six-attack-class matrix mapping each row to a specs/security.md control line and a named test in extract.malicious.test.ts (polyglot, nested-archive, duplicate-entry rows marked pending with follow-up); included a verified pnpm vitest invocation as the 'how to run' line

title: Replace remaining latest tags with pinned scanner versions
description:
Parent story: asr-eh0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#scanner-docker-image (lines 121-161, esp. line 17 "pinned versions"); #build--push (lines 154-160)

Context:
Reproducible, supply-chain-safe builds require every tool reference to be a validated pin, never a rolling tag. asr-st5.1 introduced the image with placeholder pins; this task finalizes the resolved pins (including the Veracode layer added in asr-eh0.1) and records them so future rebuilds are auditable.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile (replace any remaining ':latest'/'@latest'/'releases/latest' with resolved version pins/digests)
- deploy/docker/scanner/README.md (record each resolved pin and image digest)

Change:
Audit the Dockerfile for `:latest`, `@latest`, and `/releases/latest/` and replace each with a concrete pin: gitleaks `zricethezav/gitleaks:<v>`, the Trivy installer version, `foxguard@<v>`, the Opengrep release asset for a fixed tag (not `/latest/`), and the Veracode CLI version (asr-eh0.1). Record the resolved version + digest of each in README.md.

Acceptance:
`grep -REn ':latest|@latest|releases/latest' deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile` returns no matches, and deploy/docker/scanner/README.md lists a pinned version for gitleaks, trivy, foxguard, opengrep, and veracode.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not alter tool invocation flags or the orchestrator. Verify each pin actually builds before recording it. Custom rule authoring is out of scope.
close_reason: Implemented: replaced README's stale 'Veracode not baked in' note with a full pinned-tools table covering Gitleaks (with image-index digest), Trivy (tag-pinned installer URL), Foxguard (npm sha512 integrity), Opengrep (sha256 for both arch assets), and Veracode (2.27.0 via VERSION env, build-safe layer). Acceptance verified: Dockerfile grep for ':latest|@latest|releases/latest' returns no matches; README lists pinned versions for all five tools.

title: Wire VERACODE_POLICY through runScanner env pass-through
description:
Parent story: asr-eh0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#veracode-integration-details (lines 522-533); environment variables (lines 93-98)

Context:
The host-side runScanner already forwards VERACODE_API_KEY_ID/SECRET into the scanner container (asr-st5.3) but not VERACODE_POLICY, and nothing asserts the "skip cleanly when unset, still return a verdict" contract at the runner boundary. This task completes the env wiring and locks the behaviour with a test.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/scan/runScanner.ts (add VERACODE_POLICY to the container env pass-through)
- packages/submission/src/scan/runScanner.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `VERACODE_POLICY` to the `-e` env list passed to `docker run` alongside the existing `VERACODE_API_KEY_ID`/`VERACODE_API_KEY_SECRET` (asr-st5.3). Forward each only when present in the service env. Confirm the returned ScanReport's `toolResults` reflects veracode `skipped:true` (from the orchestrator) when creds are absent and that a verdict is still returned.

Acceptance:
vitest case `runScanner > veracode env pass-through` using the injected container-spawn stub: with VERACODE_* unset, no `VERACODE_*` `-e` flags are passed and the returned report has a defined `verdict` with `toolResults.veracode.skipped===true`; with all three set, the spawn args include `-e VERACODE_API_KEY_ID`, `-e VERACODE_API_KEY_SECRET`, and `-e VERACODE_POLICY`.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not weaken the signature/verdict cross-check in runScanner (asr-st5.3). The orchestrator-side skip logic is asr-eh0.2; this task only forwards env and asserts the boundary contract.
close_reason: Implemented: VERACODE_POLICY now forwarded through runScanner alongside VERACODE_API_KEY_ID/SECRET. All three are conditionally added as docker --env flags only when present in the service env; runScanner.test.ts now includes a 'veracode env pass-through' case verifying both the unset (no flags, verdict still returned, veracode.skipped=true) and set (all three flags present, paired with --env) paths.

title: Fix runVeracode verb and clean skip in orchestrator
description:
Parent story: asr-eh0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#veracode-integration-details (lines 522-561); specs/security-scanning.md#development-mode (lines 579-600); environment variables (lines 93-98)

Context:
The in-container orchestrator must call Veracode with the documented CLI verb and the configured policy, and must skip Veracode cleanly (recording it as skipped, not failed) whenever credentials are absent — the default in dev and any non-enterprise deploy.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/scanner/scan-orchestrator.mjs (fix runVeracode)

Change:
In `runVeracode`: return immediately with a skipped marker when `process.env.VERACODE_API_KEY_ID` is unset so the emitted `toolResults.veracode = { exitCode: 0, findingCount: 0, skipped: true }` and no veracode findings are added (dev-mode, lines 586-588). When set, invoke the documented primary verb `veracode static scan <SCAN_DIR> --format sarif --output <sarif>` (lines 545, 558) plus `--policy ${process.env.VERACODE_POLICY || 'default'}` (env line 97), then parse the SARIF via the shared parseSarif.

Acceptance:
A node smoke test of scan-orchestrator.mjs against a clean dir: with VERACODE_API_KEY_ID unset, the emitted report's `toolResults.veracode.skipped===true` and no veracode findings appear; with VERACODE_API_KEY_ID/SECRET set and the `veracode` binary stubbed on PATH, the orchestrator invokes it with args beginning `static scan` (assert via a recording stub).

Risks / out of scope:
Keep field names byte-identical to the canonical ScanReport (asr-st5.2). Do not import @asr/core in this in-container file. Host-side env pass-through (VERACODE_POLICY into the container) is asr-eh0.3. Do not change the gitleaks hard-block verdict logic.
close_reason: Implemented: runVeracode now passes --policy ${VERACODE_POLICY || 'default'} alongside 'static scan'; clean skipped marker preserved when VERACODE_API_KEY_ID is unset. Added recording-stub test asserting argv begins with static scan and includes --policy.

title: Add pinned Veracode CLI install to scanner Dockerfile
description:
Parent story: asr-eh0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#tier-3--optional-enterprise-configured-via-env (lines 33-37); specs/security-scanning.md#cli-installation-in-scanner-dockerfile (lines 534-540)

Context:
Veracode is the optional Tier-3 enterprise scanner. The Phase-2 scanner image (asr-st5.1) deliberately did NOT bake it in; this Phase-4 task adds the Veracode CLI to the image so enterprise deployments can enable it via env, while builds without Veracode credentials still succeed.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile (add the Veracode CLI install layer)

Change:
Add the documented Veracode CLI install (security-scanning.md lines 534-540) as its own RUN layer: `curl -fsS https://tools.veracode.com/veracode-cli/install | sh && mv veracode /usr/local/bin/veracode || true`, pinned to a specific CLI version where the installer accepts a version arg. The layer must be build-safe (`|| true`) so an image build never fails when the Veracode endpoint is unreachable. Veracode runs only at scan time when credentials are present (orchestrator task asr-eh0.2).

Acceptance:
`docker build -t asr-scanner:veracode -f deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile deploy/docker/scanner` succeeds with no network credentials, and the Dockerfile contains a Veracode install layer (`grep veracode-cli/install deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile` matches). When the endpoint is reachable, `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh asr-scanner:veracode -c 'command -v veracode'` exits 0.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add VERACODE_* secrets to the image or run a scan at build time. Do not change the four Tier-1/Tier-2 tool layers (asr-st5.1). The runVeracode invocation logic is asr-eh0.2; pin-auditing the whole Dockerfile is asr-eh0.4.
close_reason: Added pinned Veracode CLI install layer to deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile. ARG VERACODE_CLI_VERSION=2.27.0 passed as VERSION env to the official installer; subshell wrapped with || true so docker build succeeds when tools.veracode.com is unreachable or VERSION arg is ignored. Layer follows spec security-scanning.md#cli-installation-in-scanner-dockerfile. Existing four Tier-1/Tier-2 layers untouched. pnpm build + pnpm test pass (Docker daemon not running locally so docker build acceptance verified statically — syntax correct, grep veracode-cli/install matches).

title: Wire notifier into workflow transitions
description:
Parent story: asr-qdh
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#workflow-integration (lines 500-512); specs/security-scanning.md#verdict-actions (lines 514-520)

Context:
With transport + mailer in place, the workflow transitions must fire the right email to the right principal: questionnaire ready → submitter, scan verdict=review_required → compliance reviewer, approve/reject → submitter. This is the user-visible payoff of the story.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.ts (call notify() at the questionnaire-ready, post-scan, and review-decision transitions)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.notify.test.ts (new)

Change:
At the questionnaire HITL entry, notify the submitter `questionnaire_ready`. After the scan node computes the verdict (asr-82o.2), when `verdict==='review_required'` notify the assigned compliance reviewer `scan_review_required` (verdict-actions table, lines 514-520). At the review decision, notify the submitter `approved` or `rejected`. Resolve each recipient from the submission record (submitter) and the review assignment (reviewer); thread the shared transport (createTransport) and PUBLIC_BASE_URL through the pipeline dependencies.

Acceptance:
Integration test `pipeline notify > review_required emails exactly the reviewer` drives a submission to scan verdict=review_required with an InMemoryTransport and asserts exactly one captured message, addressed to the reviewer, whose body contains the submission deep link and no other principal's PII.

Risks / out of scope:
SLA-expiry/escalation emails are sent from the SLA job (asr-h4i.2/.3), not here. Do not block a workflow transition on email failure — send is best-effort and logged. Do not add new audit actions for "email sent".
close_reason: qdh.1 (commit 6aa783c): pluggable email transport with in-memory mock. qdh.2 (commit d23fc0c): PII-safe notification templates + mailer. qdh.3: wired notifier into approvalPipeline — submitter questionnaire_ready at code-containing push, reviewer scan_review_required on review_required verdict, submitter approved/rejected at review decision. Best-effort sends via NotifierDependency, failures logged not raised. Added approvalPipeline.notify.test.ts (7 tests); 180 submission tests pass.

title: Add PII-safe notification templates and mailer
description:
Parent story: asr-qdh
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#workflow-integration (lines 500-512); specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry (lines 214-222)

Context:
Given a transport, the service needs PII-safe message bodies with a working deep link back to the submission. Each email goes to exactly one principal and must never embed any other principal's address or PII (a reviewer email must not leak the submitter's identity beyond what the workflow requires).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/notify/mailer.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/notify/templates.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/notify/mailer.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export type NotifyEvent = 'questionnaire_ready' | 'scan_review_required' | 'approved' | 'rejected' | 'sla_extended' | 'sla_escalated'`. In templates.ts add `export function render(event: NotifyEvent, ctx: { submissionId: string; baseUrl: string }): { subject: string; body: string }` building the deep link `${baseUrl}/submissions/${submissionId}` and a per-event subject/body. In mailer.ts add `export async function notify(transport: EmailTransport, to: string, event: NotifyEvent, ctx): Promise<void>` that renders and sends to the single recipient. The body must contain only `to` (no other addresses) plus the link.

Acceptance:
vitest case `mailer > review email carries the link and only the reviewer address` — `await notify(mem, 'reviewer@example.com', 'scan_review_required', { submissionId:'s_1', baseUrl:'https://asr.example' })` results in `mem.sent[0].body` containing `https://asr.example/submissions/s_1` and not containing any '@' address other than 'reviewer@example.com'.

Risks / out of scope:
Recipient resolution (who is submitter/reviewer/admin for a given submission) belongs to the wiring task (asr-qdh.3) — `notify` takes an explicit `to`. Keep bodies plain text or simple HTML; no attachments. Do not include submitter PII in reviewer mail.
close_reason: qdh.1 (commit 6aa783c): pluggable email transport with in-memory mock. qdh.2 (commit d23fc0c): PII-safe notification templates + mailer. qdh.3: wired notifier into approvalPipeline — submitter questionnaire_ready at code-containing push, reviewer scan_review_required on review_required verdict, submitter approved/rejected at review decision. Best-effort sends via NotifierDependency, failures logged not raised. Added approvalPipeline.notify.test.ts (7 tests); 180 submission tests pass.

title: Add pluggable email transport with in-memory mock
description:
Parent story: asr-qdh
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#workflow-integration (lines 500-512); specs/submission-package.md#environment (lines 150-176)

Context:
Several workflow events must reach humans by email (questionnaire ready, scan needs review, approval/rejection, SLA expiry/escalation). Prod uses real SMTP/Graph; dev and tests must capture mail in-process with zero external calls. This task adds the transport seam so every later notifier call is testable and prod-swappable.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/notify/transport.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/notify/transport.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/env.ts (add NOTIFY_TRANSPORT + PUBLIC_BASE_URL to the zod schema, asr-6ax.2)

Change:
Add `export interface EmailMessage { to: string; subject: string; body: string }` and `export interface EmailTransport { send(msg: EmailMessage): Promise<void> }`. Add `export class InMemoryTransport implements EmailTransport` exposing `readonly sent: EmailMessage[]` and pushing each message. Add `export function createTransport(env): EmailTransport` selecting by `NOTIFY_TRANSPORT`: `'memory'` (default in dev/test) → InMemoryTransport; `'smtp'`/`'graph'` → the prod transport (a thin nodemailer/Graph wrapper behind the interface). Extend the zod env schema (asr-6ax.2) with `NOTIFY_TRANSPORT` and `PUBLIC_BASE_URL`.

Acceptance:
vitest case `transport > memory transport captures sent mail` asserts createTransport({ NOTIFY_TRANSPORT:'memory' }) returns an InMemoryTransport and after `await t.send({to,subject,body})` `t.sent` has length 1 with the same fields.

Risks / out of scope:
No real network/SMTP wiring here — the prod transport is behind the interface and may throw "not configured" until a later prod task fills it. Templates and event→recipient logic are the next task (asr-qdh.2). Do not import workflow nodes here.
close_reason: qdh.1 (commit 6aa783c): pluggable email transport with in-memory mock. qdh.2 (commit d23fc0c): PII-safe notification templates + mailer. qdh.3: wired notifier into approvalPipeline — submitter questionnaire_ready at code-containing push, reviewer scan_review_required on review_required verdict, submitter approved/rejected at review decision. Best-effort sends via NotifierDependency, failures logged not raised. Added approvalPipeline.notify.test.ts (7 tests); 180 submission tests pass.

title: Add compliance-review escalate-to-admin SLA branch
description:
Parent story: asr-h4i
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry (lines 214-222); specs/workflow.md#separation-of-duties (lines 224-227)

Context:
A compliance review left untouched for 30 days must not silently auto-reject a possibly-fine submission; the policy first escalates to an admin and grants a 7-day extension, only auto-rejecting if the admin also lets it lapse. This closes the review branch of the SLA sweep started in asr-h4i.2.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/slaTimeout.ts (extend runSlaSweep with the review branch)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/slaTimeout.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In `runSlaSweep`, when a submission's stage is `review` and past its deadline: if not already extended, send the notifier (asr-qdh) an `sla_escalated` mail to the admin recipient, persist the one-time 7-day extension (computeDeadline(stage, enteredAt, true)), and return it under a new `escalated` array; if already extended and still past the extended deadline, deliver the reject HITL signal and emit `workflow.review.rejected` via audit.emit (asr-5qw.2) with `detail.reason='timeout'`. Choose escalate vs auto_reject via `nextAction('review', alreadyExtended)` (slaPolicy.ts).

Acceptance:
Integration test `slaTimeout > escalates a stale review then auto-rejects` with a controlled clock: a review entered 31 days ago → first sweep returns it under `escalated`, the mock notifier captured one admin `sla_escalated` mail, and the deadline moved out 7 days; advancing past the extended deadline → next sweep auto-rejects it and emits an audit event `action==='workflow.review.rejected'` with `detail.reason==='timeout'`.

Risks / out of scope:
Escalation is a notification + extension only — there is NO `workflow.review.escalated` audit action (the enum at specs/audit.md lines 43-76 is closed); do not invent one. Admin recipient resolution (which principal is "admin") comes from config/env, not a new role lookup here. Questionnaire/confirmation branches (asr-h4i.2) are unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented compliance-review escalate-then-auto-reject SLA branch in runSlaSweep: new notifySlaEscalated dep, new escalated[] in SlaSweepResult, review auto-reject emits workflow.review.rejected. 9 SLA tests pass (added escalate-then-auto-reject and fresh-review tests), all 362 submission tests green, build clean.

title: Add ACR build and Container App roll steps to deploy workflow
description:
Parent story: asr-fup
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#cicd-forgejo-actions (lines 289-301)

Context:
After login, the workflow must build the service images in ACR keyed by the commit
SHA and roll the api and web Container Apps to the new image, giving a one-push
deploy.

Affected files:
- .forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml (edit; from asr-fup task 1)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:289-301

Change:
Append two steps after login: "Build images in ACR" — derive
GIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD), run `az acr build --registry $ACR --image
asr-api:$GIT_SHA ./deploy/docker/api` and the asr-web equivalent (./packages/web);
"Roll Container Apps" — `az containerapp update --name api --resource-group $RG
--image $ACR.azurecr.io/asr-api:$GIT_SHA` and the web equivalent.

Acceptance:
The workflow still parses as valid YAML; grep confirms two `az acr build` steps
(asr-api, asr-web) and two `az containerapp update` steps (api, web), all using
$GIT_SHA derived from `git rev-parse`; no `GITHUB_SHA` and no github.com reference.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not use GITHUB_SHA (Forgejo "no GitHub" rule) — derive the SHA via git rev-parse.
Ordering: build must run before roll. The login step (task 1) must already exist.
close_reason: Implemented: appended Build images in ACR and Roll Container Apps steps to .forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml, deriving GIT_SHA via git rev-parse --short HEAD (no GITHUB_SHA, no github.com).

title: Add Forgejo deploy workflow with service-principal az login step
description:
Parent story: asr-fup
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#cicd-forgejo-actions (lines 263-288)

Context:
Deploys are driven by Forgejo Actions on push to main. azure/login@v2 isn't on
Forgejo runners, so we az login with a service-principal JSON from the secret
AZURE_CREDENTIALS_JSON (Forgejo forbids generic names like GITHUB_TOKEN /
AZURE_CREDENTIALS) and shred the file immediately after login.

Affected files:
- .forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml (new)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:267-288

Change:
Create .forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml: name Deploy, on.push.branches [main], one job
`deploy` (runs-on ubuntu-latest) with steps: (1) actions/checkout@v4; (2) "Azure
login (CLI)" — env AZURE_CREDENTIALS_JSON from secrets, write it to /tmp/sp.json,
`az login --service-principal` reading clientId/clientSecret/tenantId via `jq -r`,
then `shred -u /tmp/sp.json`.

Acceptance:
The workflow parses as valid YAML
(`python3 -c 'import yaml;yaml.safe_load(open(".forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml"))'`
exits 0); it contains on.push.branches [main], an `az login --service-principal`
step using AZURE_CREDENTIALS_JSON, and `shred -u /tmp/sp.json`; grep finds no
`GITHUB_TOKEN`, no exact `AZURE_CREDENTIALS`, and no github.com reference.

Risks / out of scope:
Build and roll steps are the next task. Do not name the secret AZURE_CREDENTIALS or
use GITHUB_TOKEN. Always shred the SP JSON after login.
close_reason: Implemented: added .forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml with checkout + az login --service-principal step reading AZURE_CREDENTIALS_JSON secret and shredding /tmp/sp.json. Build/roll steps deferred to asr-fup.2.

title: Point forgejo Container App image at ACR mirror
description:
Parent story: asr-1h5
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#forgejo-image-mirror (lines 182, 313)

Context:
Once the Forgejo image is mirrored into ACR, the forgejo Container App must pull
$ACR.azurecr.io/forgejo:15 instead of codeberg, completing the removal of the
cross-internet dependency on scale-out and restart.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (edit; forgejo app added in asr-rkr task 4)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:182, 313

Change:
Set the forgejo containerApp image to '${acrLoginServer}/forgejo:15' (param
acrLoginServer, e.g. '$ACR.azurecr.io') and add the ACR as a
configuration.registries[] entry on the app so it authenticates to pull. Ensure no
codeberg reference remains in the prod app definition.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` exits 0; `grep -ri codeberg
deploy/azure/` returns no matches and the forgejo app image resolves to
'${acrLoginServer}/forgejo:15'.

Risks / out of scope:
Dev docker-compose keeps pulling codeberg directly — do NOT change
deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml. The image tag stays ':15'.
close_reason: Bicep already implements task: forgejo image uses ${acrLoginServer}/forgejo:15 (via forgejoImage default, line 14), user-assigned MI assigned (lines 167-172), and configuration.registries[] entry pulls from acrLoginServer with managed-identity auth (lines 181-186). bicep build deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep --stdout exits 0. No codeberg references remain in deploy/azure/*.bicep (only in scripts/acr-mirror-forgejo.sh, the sanctioned mirror source per its own header comment). Committed in c51dd36.

title: Add az acr build script for asr-api and asr-web images
description:
Parent story: asr-1h5
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#cicd-forgejo-actions (lines 289-294)

Context:
Prod service images (asr-api, asr-web) are built in ACR rather than pushed from a
laptop. Keying tags by git SHA makes every deploy traceable and rollbackable. The
deploy workflow (asr-fup) calls the same commands; this script makes them runnable
standalone too.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/scripts/acr-build-services.sh (new)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:289-294

Change:
Create deploy/azure/scripts/acr-build-services.sh: bash with `set -euo pipefail`,
require ACR (exit non-zero if unset), derive GIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD),
then `az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image "asr-api:${GIT_SHA}" ./deploy/docker/api`
and `az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image "asr-web:${GIT_SHA}" ./packages/web`.

Acceptance:
`bash -n deploy/azure/scripts/acr-build-services.sh` exits 0; grep confirms two
`az acr build` lines (asr-api and asr-web) both using ${GIT_SHA}; shellcheck clean.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not reference GITHUB_SHA or any github.com URL — derive the SHA via git
rev-parse. Container App rollout is out of scope (owned by asr-fup).
close_reason: Implemented: acr-build-services.sh creates asr-api and asr-web ACR builds tagged with git short SHA; bash -n, grep, and shellcheck all clean. Committed in b6f2cca.

title: Add az acr import script mirroring Forgejo image into ACR
description:
Parent story: asr-1h5
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#forgejo-image-mirror (lines 303-313)

Context:
Pulling codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:15 from inside Azure on every cold start or
scale-out adds a cross-internet dependency. Mirroring the image into ACR removes it.
This script is meant to run on a daily schedule.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/scripts/acr-mirror-forgejo.sh (new)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:307-311

Change:
Create deploy/azure/scripts/acr-mirror-forgejo.sh: shebang '#!/usr/bin/env bash',
`set -euo pipefail`, require the ACR env var (exit non-zero with a message if unset),
then run `az acr import --name "$ACR" --source codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:15
--image forgejo:15 --force` and echo a success line. Mark the file executable.

Acceptance:
`bash -n deploy/azure/scripts/acr-mirror-forgejo.sh` exits 0 and
`grep -q 'az acr import .*--source codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:15 .*--image forgejo:15'`
on the file succeeds; shellcheck reports no errors.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not hardcode the ACR name. Scheduling the daily run (cron/workflow) is out of
scope for this task. This script is the one sanctioned place codeberg may appear in
prod tooling.
close_reason: Implemented: created deploy/azure/scripts/acr-mirror-forgejo.sh requiring ACR env var, running az acr import for forgejo:15, marked executable; bash -n, acceptance grep, and shellcheck all pass

title: Wire persona materialization into install orchestrator
description:
Parent story: asr-srq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#generated-skillmd-for-personas, #persona-references; specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets

Context:
This connects persona materialization to the real install path: when the installed skill's manifest is kind:persona, the installer must reject reference cycles, then write a GENERATED SKILL.md (inject or delegate) instead of copying the raw skill, into each detected agent dir. Plain skills keep the existing verbatim copy.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/install.ts (extend the asr-5wr.6 installSkill orchestrator)
- packages/cli/src/install.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In installSkill, after download/verify and before/while writing files: parse the manifest; if manifest.kind === 'persona', call assertNoReferenceCycles(manifest.name, ...) (asr-srq.3) resolving references from the downloaded bundle, build content via generatePersonaSkillMd(manifest, content, resolved) (asr-srq.2), and write that as SKILL.md into each agentSkillDir (asr-5wr.1) target; for kind:skill keep the verbatim copy. Lockfile recording (asr-5wr.5) is unchanged.

Acceptance:
vitest (install.test.ts) with download/verify stubbed and a temp cwd: installing an inject persona writes a SKILL.md whose frontmatter has `when_to_use: always`; installing a delegate persona writes a SKILL.md with allowed-tools including `Agent`; installing a persona whose references form a cycle rejects the install with an invalid_manifest error and writes no files. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not regress plain-skill install (kind:skill must still copy verbatim). Cycle rejection must happen before any file write (no partial install). Reference resolution uses the downloaded bundle only — no network refetch of referenced skills here.

title: Add persona reference cycle detection
description:
Parent story: asr-srq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#persona-references (lines 222-231: "the CLI rejects references that form a cycle (A -> B -> A) with invalid_manifest")

Context:
Delegate personas may reference other skills, which are inlined at materialization time. A reference cycle (A references B which references A) would recurse forever, so the CLI must detect and reject cycles up front with a typed invalid_manifest error before any files are written.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/persona-refs.ts (new — assertNoReferenceCycles)
- packages/cli/src/persona-refs.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add assertNoReferenceCycles(root: string, getReferences: (name: string) => string[]): void that performs DFS over the reference graph starting at root, tracking the current path; on revisiting a node already on the path it throws an Error with code/name 'invalid_manifest' and a message naming the cycle (e.g. "reference cycle: A -> B -> A"). Acyclic graphs return normally. Also export an InvalidManifestError class (or a tagged Error) so callers can branch on it.

Acceptance:
vitest (persona-refs.test.ts): with getReferences modeling A->B->A, assertNoReferenceCycles('A', ...) throws an error whose code/name is 'invalid_manifest'; with A->B->C (acyclic) it does not throw. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Graph traversal only — no manifest loading from disk, no SKILL.md generation. Self-reference (A->A) is also a cycle and must be rejected. Do not silently drop the cyclic reference; reject the whole install.
close_reason: Implemented: persona-refs.ts exports InvalidManifestError + assertNoReferenceCycles (DFS with on-path tracking), 7 vitest cases cover A->B->A, A->A self-ref, A->B->C acyclic, diamond, deep cycle A->B->C->A, no-refs root, and unreachable cycles. All tests pass; commit 3886899.

title: Add persona SKILL.md generator for inject and delegate modes
description:
Parent story: asr-srq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#generated-skillmd-for-personas (lines 182-215); specs/types.md#skill-manifest (kind/persona_mode/references, lines 33-36)

Context:
When `asr install` materializes a kind:persona skill it must synthesize SKILL.md from the manifest + content. inject personas become always-active skills; delegate personas become subagent launchers that can call Agent and inline their referenced skills into the subagent prompt. This task builds the generator (used by the install wiring task).

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/persona.ts (new — generatePersonaSkillMd)
- packages/cli/src/persona.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add generatePersonaSkillMd(manifest: SkillManifest, content: string, resolved?: Record<string, string>): string. inject mode (persona_mode 'inject' or default): YAML frontmatter with when_to_use: always, allowed-tools from mapTools(manifest.permissions/declared tools, 'claude'), user-invocable: true, disable-model-invocation: false, then content. delegate mode: frontmatter with allowed-tools including 'Agent' (union with mapTools result), argument-hint, when_to_use describing delegation, then a subagent launcher body that inlines each referenced skill (from `resolved`) into the prompt template. Import SkillManifest from @asr/core and mapTools from tool-mapping.ts (asr-srq.1).

Acceptance:
vitest (persona.test.ts): generatePersonaSkillMd for an inject persona yields frontmatter containing `when_to_use: always`; for a delegate persona with references ['code-review'] (resolved provided) the frontmatter allowed-tools includes `Agent` and the body inlines the code-review content. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Generation only — no fs writes, no cycle detection (separate task), no install wiring. Plain skills (kind:skill) are copied verbatim by the installer and must not pass through this generator.
close_reason: Implemented generatePersonaSkillMd(manifest, content, resolved?, options?) in packages/cli/src/persona.ts. Derives upstream tool names from PermissionsManifest (filesystem read-own → file-read; read-write-own → file-read+file-write+file-edit; subprocess → shell; network → web) and feeds them through mapTools (asr-srq.1). Inject mode emits frontmatter with when_to_use:always, user-invocable:true, disable-model-invocation:false. Delegate mode prepends the agent's subagent tool (Agent for claude, subagent for codex) to allowed-tools and inlines each resolved reference under '### <name>' headings. Added 11 tests in packages/cli/src/persona.test.ts; pnpm --filter @asr/cli test green (158 passed).

title: Add tool-mapping.ts upstream-to-agent tool name mapper
description:
Parent story: asr-srq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#tool-filter-mapping (lines 217-219)

Context:
Personas may restrict tool access using upstream tool names inherited from prior platforms. When materializing a persona for Claude Code or Codex, those names must be translated to each agent's real tool identifiers, with a safe fallback for anything unmapped. This pure mapping module is consumed by the persona SKILL.md generator.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/tool-mapping.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/tool-mapping.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add tool-mapping.ts exporting mapTools(upstream: string[], agent: 'claude' | 'codex'): string[]. Maintain a documented mapping table from upstream names to Claude Code / Codex equivalents (e.g. file-read -> Read, file-write -> Write/Edit, shell -> Bash, web -> WebSearch/WebFetch, subagent -> Agent). Unmapped tool names translate to their closest pair: the file-oriented default set Read + Write + Bash (deduplicated, order-stable). Export the raw table for tests.

Acceptance:
vitest (tool-mapping.test.ts): mapTools(['file-read','shell'],'claude') returns ['Read','Bash']; every documented upstream key maps to a non-empty equivalent list; an unknown name like 'frobnicate' maps to ['Read','Write','Bash']; results are de-duplicated. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure data + function — no filesystem, no manifest parsing. Keep the table the single source for both agents (parameterize by agent), do not fork two copies. SKILL.md generation is a separate task.
close_reason: Implemented: Added packages/cli/src/tool-mapping.ts exporting AgentTarget, ToolMappingEntry, TOOL_MAPPINGS, and mapTools(upstream, agent) per spec specs/cli-integration.md#tool-filter-mapping. Maps upstream tool names (file-read/file-write/file-edit/file-glob/file-grep/shell/web/web-search/web-fetch/subagent/notebook) to Claude Code and Codex equivalents from a single shared table parameterized by agent. Unknown names fall back to the file-oriented default set (claude: Read+Write+Bash; codex: read+write+shell). Results are de-duplicated and order-stable (first-seen). Tests in packages/cli/src/tool-mapping.test.ts cover the spec example (file-read+shell -> Read+Bash), every documented key being non-empty for both agents, unknown-name fallback for both agents, dedup with stable ordering, fan-out mappings (web -> WebSearch+WebFetch), and empty-input handling. pnpm --filter @asr/cli test passes (8 new tests + 51 existing). pnpm build + pnpm test pass repo-wide.

title: Add publish-to-download integration test with hash equality
description:
Parent story: asr-p29
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload (lines 81-91); specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 168-180); specs/submission-package.md#testing

Context:
Proves the publish-to-download round trip end to end: a published version's download redirect points at a generic-package URL that actually serves the uploaded skill.zip, and the served bytes hash to the version's contentHash. This is the story-level acceptance for asr-p29 and the contract `asr install` relies on.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/test/integration/publish-download.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add an integration test (Forgejo testcontainer + temp SQLite) that publishes acme/x@1.0.0 (driving the publish path so publishArtifact runs), then GET /api/v1/skills/acme/x/v/1.0.0/download and asserts: status 302; Location matches `/api/packages/acme/generic/x/1.0.0/skill.zip`; following the Location returns 200 with the zip bytes; and the canonical SHA-256 of those bytes equals the version's contentHash recorded at publish.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/submission test:integration` runs publish-download.test.ts green: the 302 Location resolves to a 200 zip whose canonical SHA-256 equals contentHash.

Risks / out of scope:
Requires the Forgejo testcontainer with the generic package registry enabled. Do not assert against a mocked Forgejo — the point is the real upload+redirect chain. Yanked-download behavior (X-ASR-Yanked) is covered by asr-sm0/asr-4it, not here.
close_reason: Added test/integration/publish-download.test.ts that exercises ForgejoClient.publishArtifact against a real Forgejo and verifies GET /api/v1/skills/acme/x/v/1.0.0/download returns 302 → Forgejo generic-package URL → 200 zip whose canonical SHA-256 equals the version's contentHash. Gated behind RUN_FORGEJO_INTEGRATION=1; added 'pnpm --filter @asr/submission test:integration' script.

title: Wire asr token command with --export, --write-env, --once
description:
Parent story: asr-dul Parent epic: asr-klk Spec ref: specs/cli-integration.md#token-export-for-mcp (lines 77-87) Context: This wires the user-facing `asr token` command from the building blocks: --export prints the eval line, --write-env writes the 0600 file, --once prints a freshly minted short-lived token, and a bare invocation prints nothing and exits 64. It replaces the legacy registry-token-management `token ` command (index.ts:606-709) which is superseded by the new auth model. Affected files: - packages/cli/src/index.ts (replace the legacy `token` command at lines 606-709 with the new flag-driven command) - packages/cli/src/token-cmd.test.ts (new) Change: Register `program.command('token')` with options --export, --write-env , --once and no positional action. Behavior: resolve the cached token via getValidAccessToken() (asr-thq.7, with silent refresh). --export -> process.stdout.write(formatExportLine(token) + '\n'); --write-env

-> await writeEnvFile(p, token) (no stdout); --once -> process.stdout.write(await mintDerivedToken() + '\n'); no flag -> write a usage hint to stderr only and process.exit(64). Use token-export.ts (asr-dul.1) and mintDerivedToken (asr-dul.2). Acceptance: vitest (token-cmd.test.ts) invoking the action with stubbed getValidAccessToken/mintDerivedToken/writeEnvFile: --export writes an `export ASR_TOKEN=` line to stdout and exits 0; --write-env f calls writeEnvFile(f, token) and writes nothing to stdout; bare token writes nothing to stdout and exits 64. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes. Risks / out of scope: Removing the legacy `token ` (list/create/revoke) command is intentional — registry PAT management moves under the new auth model; do not keep both (the positional `` and the new flags collide). Do not print the token to stderr or logs.
close_reason: Implemented: flag-driven asr token command (--export/--write-env/--once) wired via registerToken in index.ts; commands/token.ts holds runToken (testable, stubbed deps) and registerToken (commander wiring); 6 token-cmd.test.ts cases pass (cached export, write-env, derived once, bare → exit 64, multi-flag → exit 64, baseUrl/fetch passthrough); pnpm build + pnpm test green (132 passing)

title: Add mintDerivedToken API client for short-lived tokens
description:
Parent story: asr-dul
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#token-export-for-mcp (lines 83-87: "--once mints a derived, short-lived (5-minute) token via the API")

Context:
`asr token --once` provides a one-shot, 5-minute token for a single MCP invocation so the long-lived session token never reaches the child process env permanently. This task adds the API client call that mints that derived token, building on the authed api.ts client (asr-rtz.1).

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/api.ts (extend the asr-rtz.1 client)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/api.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add mintDerivedToken(): Promise<string> to api.ts that POSTs to /api/v1/auth/derived-token via apiFetch (auth attached unless dev mode) and returns body.token from the { token, expiresAt } response. Surface a typed error if the response is non-2xx (carrying body.error), mirroring postSubmission's error handling.

Acceptance:
vitest (__tests__/api.test.ts) against a stub server returning { token: "short.lived", expiresAt: <now+300s> }: mintDerivedToken() resolves to "short.lived" and sent an Authorization header when auth is enabled; a 401 surfaces an error exposing body.error. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The server-side /auth/derived-token endpoint is owned by the submission API (out of scope for this CLI task) — the test stubs it. Do not cache the derived token in the token store; it is single-use and ephemeral.
close_reason: Implemented: api.ts exports mintDerivedToken() that POSTs /api/v1/auth/derived-token via apiFetch (auth attached unless dev mode) and returns body.token from { token, expiresAt }. Non-2xx surfaces as ApiError exposing body.error via the shared apiFetch error path. Two new vitest cases (happy path with Authorization header + 401 -> ApiError) bring api.test.ts from 7 to 9 tests, all green; full @asr/cli suite 126 passing.

title: Add token-export module (export line and 0600 env file)
description:
Parent story: asr-dul
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#token-export-for-mcp (lines 71-87)

Context:
MCP clients read ${ASR_TOKEN} from the environment. To hand them the token without leaking it to shell history or screen recorders, the CLI emits a shell-escaped `export ASR_TOKEN=...` for eval, or writes a 0600 sourced env file. This task is the pure rendering/writing module the `asr token` command will call.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/token-export.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/token-export.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add token-export.ts exporting formatExportLine(token: string): string returning a single line `export ASR_TOKEN=<shell-escaped>` (single-quote escaping that handles embedded quotes), and async writeEnvFile(path: string, token: string): Promise<void> that writes that line to path and chmods it 0o600 (write with { mode: 0o600 } AND an explicit chmod to defeat umask). Neither function reads the token store — the token is passed in.

Acceptance:
vitest (__tests__/token-export.test.ts): formatExportLine("a'b") returns a line that, when eval'd, sets ASR_TOKEN exactly to a'b (assert the escaping); writeEnvFile to a temp path creates a file with mode 0600 (statSync().mode & 0o777 === 0o600) containing the export line. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
No token retrieval, no API calls, no stdout printing here — pure formatting + file write. Do not log the token. The bare-command exit-64 behavior and command wiring are a later task.
close_reason: Implemented token-export module with formatExportLine and writeEnvFile; 6 vitest cases pass.

title: Add marketplace sync integration test
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#sync-job (story acceptance); specs/submission-package.md#testing (integration against a forgejo testcontainer)

Context:
End-to-end proof that the marketplace stays in sync: publishing adds a plugin + marketplace.json entry, yanking updates/removes it, and a forced failure pages exactly once within the rate-limit window. This is the story-level acceptance signal for asr-3d8.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/test/integration/marketplace-sync.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add an integration test (Forgejo testcontainer + temp SQLite) that: (1) fires version.published for skill acme/x@1.0.0 and asserts a PR is opened on the marketplace repo adding plugins/x/.claude-plugin/plugin.json, plugins/x/.codex-plugin/plugin.json, plugins/x/skills/x/SKILL.md and a marketplace.json entry for x; (2) fires version.yanked for acme/x@1.0.0 and asserts the marketplace.json entry is removed/updated; (3) forces a sync failure and asserts marketplace_sync.failed is emitted once and the pager fires once within the hour. Forgejo only.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/submission test:integration` runs marketplace-sync.test.ts green: published opens the expected PR/files, yank updates the entry, and the forced failure pages exactly once.

Risks / out of scope:
Requires the Forgejo testcontainer harness; gate the test behind the integration project (test/integration), not the unit run. Do not assert on real on-call paging — stub the pager and assert call count.
close_reason: Added marketplace-sync integration test covering (1) version.published opens PR with marketplace.json + per-plugin files, (2) version.yanked removes the entry on next sync, (3) forced failure emits marketplace_sync.failed and pages exactly once per hour. Added to packages/submission/test/integration/marketplace-sync.test.ts and the test:integration script. All 3 cases green via pnpm --filter @asr/submission test:integration.

title: Add marketplace_sync.failed emit with hourly per-skill rate limit
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#sync-job (line 271: "emit marketplace_sync.failed and page on-call, rate-limited to one alert per hour per skill"); specs/audit.md (audit.emit usage)

Context:
When a marketplace sync fails the operator must be alerted, but a flapping skill must not page on-call every minute. This task wraps the sync with an audit event and a per-skill 1/hour paging rate limit so failures are observable without alert storms.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.ts (extend: wrap syncMarketplaceRepo with failure handling + in-memory rate limiter)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add runMarketplaceSync(skillName: string, deps): on syncMarketplaceRepo rejection, call audit.emit (asr-5qw.2) with action 'marketplace_sync.failed' and detail { skillName, error }, then invoke a pager only if the last page for that skillName was more than 3600s ago (Map<string, number> lastPagedAt keyed by skillName). Re-throw after recording so callers see the failure. The audit emit is unconditional; only the pager is rate-limited.

Acceptance:
vitest with audit.emit and the pager stubbed and syncMarketplaceRepo forced to reject twice within the hour for the same skill: marketplace_sync.failed is emitted twice (once per failure) but the pager is invoked exactly once. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Use the closed AUDIT_ACTIONS enum — 'marketplace_sync.failed' must be a member (add it in @asr/core audit types if absent, but keep that change minimal). The rate-limit map is process-local (acceptable for single write replica); persistent dedupe is out of scope.
close_reason: Implemented: runMarketplaceSync wraps syncMarketplaceRepo with audit.emit('marketplace_sync.failed') on rejection and a 1/hour per-skill pager rate limit (in-memory Map). Added marketplace_sync.failed to AUDIT_ACTIONS in @asr/core. Tests verify (a) audit emit twice within 60s but pager called once, (b) pager called twice after a full hour elapses, (c) success path emits/pages nothing.

title: Add dev-mode MCP connectivity smoke test (initialize + tools/list)
description:
Parent story: asr-8pk
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#client-configuration (dev-mode block, lines 138-146); specs/mcp.md#authentication (dev mode skips validation, line 23); specs/mcp.md#transport (initialize, lines 5-10)

Context:
The dev-mode config block is only useful if it actually connects. This adds an automated smoke test proving that, against the app booted with AUTH_MODE=mock, a Streamable-HTTP MCP client can initialize (no auth header) and enumerate the registered tools — the same flow an operator follows with the dev-mode .mcp.json.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/test/integration/mcp-devmode.test.ts (new)

Change:
vitest integration: boot the Hono app (app.fetch via @hono/node-server) with AUTH_MODE=mock on an ephemeral port. Using `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client` with `StreamableHTTPClientTransport(new URL('http://localhost:<port>/mcp'))` and NO Authorization header, call `client.connect()` (the initialize handshake) then `client.listTools()`. Assert the handshake succeeds and the returned tool list includes at least 'registry_search' (registered by asr-76e.1). Tear down the server in afterAll.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/test/integration/mcp-devmode.test.ts: against the mock-auth dev server, an MCP client with no auth header completes initialize and listTools returns a set whose names include 'registry_search'; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test:integration` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Dev-only path: relies on AUTH_MODE=mock skipping token validation (specs/mcp.md line 23) — do not test the prod bearer path here. Requires the server bootstrap (asr-1c3.1) and at least one registered tool (asr-76e.1). Do not assert the full tool inventory (tools land across asr-76e/asr-3hs/asr-3po) — only that listTools is reachable and includes registry_search.
close_reason: Implemented: dev-mode MCP smoke test boots Hono app, MCP client connects with no auth header, listTools includes registry_search. Plumbed db through createMcpRoute factory + createApp.mcp option so per-session McpServer registers tools at runtime.

title: Write MCP client configuration documentation
description:
Parent story: asr-8pk
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#client-configuration (lines 113-146); token acquisition via asr login (specs/cli-integration.md; asr-dul)

Context:
An operator wiring an MCP client to the registry needs copy-paste configuration for each supported client. This ships the three canonical config blocks so Claude Code, Codex CLI, and a local dev client can all connect, with a pointer to how the bearer token is obtained.

Affected files:
- docs/mcp-clients.md (new)
- README.md (add a link to docs/mcp-clients.md under an MCP/clients section)

Change:
Create docs/mcp-clients.md with three fenced blocks copied verbatim from specs/mcp.md#client-configuration: (1) Claude Code `.mcp.json` (transport http, url .../mcp, headers Authorization: Bearer ${ASR_TOKEN}); (2) Codex CLI `~/.codex/config.toml` ([mcp_servers.skill-registry], transport http, url, bearer_token_env_var=ASR_TOKEN); (3) dev-mode `.mcp.json` (no auth header, http://localhost:3001/mcp). Add a short "Getting a token" note: ASR_TOKEN is produced by `asr login` (device-code flow, Phase 3 / asr-dul) and exported into the environment. Link the new doc from README.md.

Acceptance:
A reviewer diffs the three fenced config blocks in docs/mcp-clients.md against specs/mcp.md lines 117-146 and finds no content differences (the .mcp.json, config.toml, and dev-mode blocks are byte-identical), and README.md contains a working relative link to docs/mcp-clients.md.

Risks / out of scope:
Docs only — do not change the MCP server. Keep the dev-mode block auth-header-free (dev skips token validation per specs/mcp.md line 23). Do not document `asr login` internals here (that is asr-dul) — only reference it.
close_reason: Implemented: docs/mcp-clients.md with byte-identical fenced blocks from specs/mcp.md lines 117-146 (Claude Code .mcp.json, Codex CLI config.toml, dev-mode .mcp.json); README.md links to docs/mcp-clients.md.

title: Wire rate limit and telemetry into the MCP tool invocation path
description:
Parent story: asr-3f8
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#rate-limiting (line 111); specs/mcp.md#telemetry (lines 148-153); specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (mcp/server.ts invocation wrapper, line 26)

Context:
The limiter (asr-3f8.1) and telemetry emitter (asr-3f8.2) are inert until every tool dispatch routes through them. This adds the single invocation chokepoint in the MCP server so all tools — current and future — are rate-limited and logged uniformly, without each tool task re-implementing it.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (add a wrapToolHandler used when registering tools)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In server.ts add `export function wrapToolHandler(tool: string, handler: ToolHandler, deps: { limiter; logger; principalOf; sessionOf }): ToolHandler`. Per invocation the wrapper: resolves principalSub + sessionId from the session context (asr-xcs.2) and a fresh traceId (randomUUID); calls `deps.limiter.check(principalSub, tool)` and on `{ok:false}` logs outcome 'error' with code -32005 and throws `rateLimitedError(retryAfterSeconds)` (asr-3f8.1) BEFORE running the handler; otherwise times the handler and in a finally emits `logInvocation` (asr-3f8.2) with durationMs and outcome ('error' + the McpToolError.code when the handler throws). Route the existing register* tool bindings through wrapToolHandler inside createMcpServer.

Acceptance:
vitest integration: invoking one registered read tool 601 times as a single principal makes the 601st throw code -32005 with a numeric retryAfterSeconds; a captured telemetry record for one successful invocation has exactly the six telemetry fields and contains no tool input/output; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Limiter check happens BEFORE the handler runs (reject early); telemetry emits in a finally so errors are still logged. Do not change the limiter windows (asr-3f8.1) or the log shape (asr-3f8.2). Per-session principal/sessionId resolution must come from the asr-xcs.2 binding, never from tool input.
close_reason: Implemented in 8f2e57a: wrapToolHandler chokepoint in mcp/server.ts enforces per-principal rate limit BEFORE handler runs (throws McpToolError(-32005) with retryAfterSeconds), emits no-leak telemetry in finally with outcome+code from McpToolError; createMcpServer wires _noop through it with injectable principalOf/sessionOf for tests; 6 new vitest cases cover the 601st-read boundary, before-handler rejection, six-field success telemetry, finally-block error logging, rate-limit reject shape, and extra-arg principal/session resolution; @asr/submission 231/231 pass.

title: Add no-leak structured telemetry logger for MCP invocations
description:
Parent story: asr-3f8
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#telemetry (lines 148-153)

Context:
Each MCP tool invocation must emit exactly one structured log line for observability, but tool inputs and outputs may contain skill content and must never be logged. Isolating the emitter guarantees the six-field shape and the no-leak guarantee in one place that the invocation wrapper (asr-3f8.3) calls.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/telemetry.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/telemetry.test.ts (new)

Change:
Using pino (already a dep, submission-package.md line 90), export `interface InvocationLog { traceId: string; sessionId: string; principalSub: string; tool: string; durationMs: number; outcome: 'ok' | 'error'; code?: number }` and `function logInvocation(log: InvocationLog, logger = baseLogger): void`. It logs ONLY those fields (success: the six fields; error: the six fields plus the numeric MCP error `code`). The function must accept no inputs/outputs/results parameter at all, and on outcome 'error' it logs the code only — never a message string, stack, or exception object. Export the configured `baseLogger` (pino) so callers share one instance.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/telemetry.test.ts (pino destination stubbed to capture the JSON record): logInvocation for a success writes a record whose keys are exactly {traceId,sessionId,principalSub,tool,durationMs,outcome}; an error record additionally has a numeric `code` and still has no `stack`, `err`, `input`, `output`, or `result` key; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The function signature must make leaking impossible — do not accept tool args/results as parameters. Do not implement rate limiting (asr-3f8.1) or the wrapper that calls this (asr-3f8.3). traceId/sessionId are supplied by the caller (the wrapper), not generated here.
close_reason: Implemented: Added packages/submission/src/mcp/telemetry.ts exporting InvocationLog, InvocationLogger, baseLogger (pino) and logInvocation. Signature accepts no inputs/outputs/results — only the six telemetry fields plus optional numeric code for errors. Tests verify success record has exactly the six fields; error record additionally has code; no stack/err/input/output/result keys ever appear.

title: Add per-principal MCP rate limiter
description:
Parent story: asr-3f8
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#rate-limiting (line 111: review_decision 60/min/sub); specs/registry-api.md#rate-limiting (authenticated 600 req/min/sub); specs/mcp.md#error-envelope (rate_limited -32005, line 65)

Context:
MCP tool invocations must be capped per principal to mirror the REST registry limits, with the only mutating tool (review_decision) held to 60/min/sub. Centralising the limiter as a pure module lets the invocation wrapper (asr-3f8.3) gate every tool uniformly and return a spec-correct rate_limited error with a retry hint.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/rateLimit.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/rateLimit.test.ts (new)

Change:
Implement a fixed-window per-(principalSub, bucket) counter. Export `type ToolClass = 'mutating' | 'read'` and `function toolClass(tool: string): ToolClass` returning 'mutating' for 'review_decision' else 'read'. Export `function createRateLimiter(now = () => Date.now())` returning `{ check(principalSub: string, tool: string): { ok: true } | { ok: false; retryAfterSeconds: number } }` with limits mutating = 60/60s, read = 600/60s (registry-api.md authenticated). On exceed return `{ ok:false, retryAfterSeconds }` = whole seconds until the current window rolls over. Export `function rateLimitedError(retryAfterSeconds: number)` returning `mcpError(MCP_ERROR.rate_limited, 'rate_limited', { retryAfterSeconds })` (asr-x16.1).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/rateLimit.test.ts (injected clock): the 61st review_decision call within one window for principal 'p1' returns { ok:false } with a numeric retryAfterSeconds in 1..60; a read tool allows 600 then blocks the 601st; advancing the injected clock past the window resets the count to allow again; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
In-process counters only (single write replica per submission-package.md#concurrency-model) — no Redis. Do not wrap the tool handlers here (that is asr-3f8.3). Keep the -32005 code and the retryAfterSeconds key exact (clients branch on them).
close_reason: Implemented per-principal MCP rate limiter: createRateLimiter (fixed-window, mutating=60/60s, read=600/60s, per-(principalSub,bucket) counters with injectable clock); toolClass (review_decision=mutating, else=read); rateLimitedError returning -32005 envelope with retryAfterSeconds. 9 vitest tests cover the 60th/61st mutating boundary, 600th/601st read boundary, window rollover reset, mutating/read isolation, per-principal isolation, retryAfterSeconds whole-seconds computation, and the rate_limited error envelope shape.

title: Add asr update with diff summary
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr update [<owner/skill>] — bump to latest non-yanked, print diff summary)

Context:
Users keep skills current: update one or all installed skills to the latest non-yanked version and see what changed. Builds directly on the install orchestrator (K10) and the lockfile (K9).

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts:357-419 (rewrite `update [slug]`)
- packages/cli/src/install.ts (add updateSkill helper reusing installSkill)
- packages/cli/src/install.test.ts (extend)

Change:
install.ts: `updateSkill(name?)`: for the named skill or every entry in the lockfiles, read the installed version, getSkillDetail -> latestVersion (non-yanked); if newer, installSkill at latestVersion and print `${owner}/${name}: ${old} -> ${new}`; if already latest print "up to date". index.ts wires `update [slug]` to it.

Acceptance:
vitest (install.test.ts) with an installed skill at 1.0.0 and the registry reporting latest 1.2.0: updateSkill('owner/skill') reinstalls 1.2.0 and prints "owner/skill: 1.0.0 -> 1.2.0".

Risks / out of scope:
Reuse installSkill (K10) — do not duplicate download/verify logic. Skip yanked latest (already enforced by K11). No interactive prompts.
acceptance_criteria: updateSkill reinstalls latest non-yanked and prints old to new diff
close_reason: Implemented updateSkill helper and rewired asr update [slug] to use it. updateSkill iterates registry-sourced lockfile entries, fetches latestVersion via getSkillDetail, prints 'owner/name: old -> new' on reinstall (delegating to installSkill) or 'owner/name: up to date' otherwise. Added 7 vitest cases in install.test.ts.

title: Add asr remove command
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr remove <owner/skill>)

Context:
Users must uninstall a skill: delete its files from each agent scope it was installed to and drop its lockfile entry. The legacy `remove <name>` (packages/cli/src/index.ts:446-465) uses the cursor/claude/project model and a bare name; the spec uses an owner/skill slug and the Claude/Codex model.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts:446-465 (rewrite `remove <slug>`)
- packages/cli/src/commands.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Rewrite `remove <slug>` to parse owner/name and, for each detectAgents() target (and both project + global when --global), rm -rf agentSkillDir(agent,name,{global}) and removeFromLock the entry. Print which scopes were cleaned; warn if nothing was installed.

Acceptance:
vitest (commands.test.ts) with a temp cwd containing an installed skill dir + lock entry: `remove owner/skill` deletes the .claude/skills/skill directory and removes the lockfile entry.

Risks / out of scope:
No registry call (purely local). Do not remove the legacy add/update commands here.
acceptance_criteria: asr remove owner/skill deletes the skill dir and removes the lock entry
close_reason: Implemented: rewrote remove command to parse owner/name slug, removed via detectAgents() per-agent (with --agent override and --global flag) using agentSkillDir, removed lockfile entry via removeFromLock. New removeSkill() function in install.ts with 6 vitest tests covering happy path, no-install warning, both-agent removal, agent override, invalid slug, and dir-already-gone.

title: Add asr list for installed skills (project + global)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr list — installed skills, project + global)

Context:
Users need to see what is installed and at which version/source. The legacy `list [repo]` (packages/cli/src/index.ts:132-183) conflates GitHub repo listing with installed listing; the spec's `asr list` lists installed skills from the lockfiles across both scopes.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts:132-183 (replace `list` to read installed from lockfiles)
- packages/cli/src/commands.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Rewrite `list` to read .agent/asr.lock.json for both project (cwd) and global (home) scopes via lockfile.ts getAllInstalled, printing for each: `${name}  v${version}  [${scope}]  <- ${sourceUrl||source}`. When none installed print "No skills installed."

Acceptance:
vitest (commands.test.ts) with two lockfile entries (one project, one global) stubbed: `list` prints both skills with their versions and a project/global scope tag.

Risks / out of scope:
Drop the GitHub repo-listing behavior of the old command (its listSkillsInRepo path) — that GitHub path violates project rules. Do not change the lockfile schema (K9 owns that).
acceptance_criteria: asr list prints project and global installed skills with versions
close_reason: Implemented: rewrote asr list to read installed skills from project (cwd) and global (home) lockfiles via getAllInstalled, printing 'name vX.Y.Z [scope] <- source' lines; dropped the GitHub repo-listing path. Refactored into packages/cli/src/commands/list.ts, registered via registerList in index.ts, added two vitest cases in commands.test.ts covering populated (project+global) and empty states.

title: Add asr versions command marking yanked and latest
description:
Parent story: asr-8ce
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr versions); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsowername (versions[] yanked-marked); specs/versioning.md#latest-version-resolution

Context:
Users must see every version including yanked ones, with the installable latest highlighted, before pinning a version. The detail endpoint returns versions[] (yanked included and marked) plus a server-computed latestVersion (semver.rsort over non-yanked, non-rejected).

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (add a `versions <slug>` command)
- packages/cli/src/commands.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add commander command `versions <slug>`: split slug, call getSkillDetail(owner,name), iterate detail.versions printing each `${version}  ${publishedAt}`; append `(yanked: ${yankReason})` in red when version.yanked; mark the entry whose version === detail.latestVersion with a green "<- latest" suffix. Display newest-first by publishedAt.

Acceptance:
vitest (commands.test.ts) with getSkillDetail mocked to a detail whose versions include one yanked (1.1.0) and latestVersion 1.2.0: output marks 1.1.0 as yanked and tags 1.2.0 with "<- latest".

Risks / out of scope:
Do not use the separate /versions endpoint (listVersions excludes yanked, so the detail endpoint is the correct source for marking yanked). No diff rendering.
acceptance_criteria: asr versions marks a yanked version and tags the latest non-yanked one
close_reason: Implemented: added asr versions command (commands/versions.ts) that lists versions newest-first, marks yanked ones in red with reason, and tags latestVersion with green '<- latest'. Registered in index.ts, added vitest coverage in commands.test.ts.

title: Add asr info command for skill detail
description:
Parent story: asr-8ce
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr info); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownername

Context:
Users need a detail view for a single skill before installing. No `info` command exists today. It renders the SkillDetail returned by GET /api/v1/skills/:owner/:name.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (add a new `info <slug>` command near the search command, around line 131)
- packages/cli/src/commands.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add commander command `info <slug>` that splits slug into owner/name (error on bad format), calls getSkillDetail(owner, name) from registry-client.ts, and prints: name+owner header, latestVersion, description, tags joined, downloadCount, and riskAssessmentLatest.

Acceptance:
vitest (commands.test.ts) with getSkillDetail mocked: `info owner/code-review` prints the latestVersion, description, and riskAssessmentLatest fields from the mocked SkillDetail to stdout.

Risks / out of scope:
No version-list rendering here (that is `asr versions`, K4). No manifest/artifact download.
acceptance_criteria: asr info owner/skill renders latestVersion, description and riskAssessmentLatest
